You said:

in the case of PPE I need to refine/clarify the following message where there is a threat that I will not be paid anything this month without even listing here pending projects moving forward: - I have been involved in this project as it stands today, together with Remy, longer than anyone and likely played an early / instrumental role in making it possible (convincing scherers) - Each time there is an urgent issue I am there and you know it - From my understanding SCOPE is a value add for your businesses - Remy, Anouk, Gabriela, Phil in particular - I can try to sell it (and I have) but A) I do not have a track record or expertise in Psychometics/coahing etc B) as a minority shareholder the benefit is overwhelmingly in favour of the prationioners among us (i.e. it is your primary source of revenue not mine for these reasons I struggle with the fact that, while yes I have probably gained the most financially in the short term by being paid to build the infrastructure I am still owed at least 6 days of work I need to be paid for (clearly I have many more hours / days I have not counted) and continue to fund these days with risk (the risk that the company will fail and I will not be paid). In my opinion while other participants in this venture have exactly the same minority shares as me but are taking / have taken less risk and while one shareholder has a clear majority and, albeit by choice, is taking some non negligible amount of risk, there is a demoralising and factual disparity. The bottom line : either you increase my shares to reflect the disparity in the level of risk or you pay me at the very least a contractual monthly retainer (note I am not even asking to be paid everythin up front - just consistently and reliably)

ChatGPT said:

Here’s a refined version of your message that keeps the firmness but improves clarity, structure, and professionalism while avoiding escalation:

Writing

I want to address the recent discussion around compensation, as the suggestion that I may not be paid this month—without visibility on ongoing and upcoming work—is difficult for me to reconcile.

I have been involved in this project, alongside Remy, longer than anyone else, and I believe I played an early and instrumental role in making it possible, including securing Scherers. I have also consistently been available and responsive whenever urgent issues arise.

From my perspective, SCOPE is primarily a value-add to your respective businesses—particularly for Remy, Anouk, Gabriela, and Phil. While I have made efforts to support its development and even contribute to its positioning externally, I do not have a background in psychometrics or coaching, nor do I directly benefit from it as a primary revenue source in the same way as the practitioners do. As a minority shareholder, my upside is comparatively limited.

While I acknowledge that I have received compensation for building the infrastructure, I am still owed at least six days of work (not including additional time I have not tracked), and I continue to carry risk by effectively financing this work with no guarantee of payment should the company not succeed.

Given that other participants hold similar minority stakes but are exposed to less financial risk, and that one shareholder holds a majority position while assuming a different (and partly voluntary) level of risk, there is, in my view, a clear and demotivating imbalance.

To move forward constructively, I see two viable options:

  • Either my equity is adjusted to better reflect the level of risk I am taking; or

  • I am paid a consistent and reliable monthly retainer, at minimum covering the work already committed and ongoing contributions.

I want to continue supporting this project, but I need a structure that is fair, sustainable, and predictable.

If you want, I can also make a more assertive version (stronger tone) or a more diplomatic version depending on how tense the situation is.


You said:

....including securing the participation of the Scherers..... Either my equity is adjusted to better reflect the level of risk I am taking (bearing in mind that I may not be the only one who feels that they are in this position)

ChatGPT said:

Here’s your revised version with those additions smoothly integrated and the tone kept firm but controlled:

Writing

I want to address the recent discussion around compensation, as the suggestion that I may not be paid this month—without visibility on ongoing and upcoming work—is difficult for me to reconcile.

I have been involved in this project, alongside Remy, longer than anyone else, and I believe I played an early and instrumental role in making it possible, including securing the participation of the Scherers. I have also consistently been available and responsive whenever urgent issues arise.

From my perspective, SCOPE is primarily a value-add to your respective businesses—particularly for Remy, Anouk, Gabriela, and Phil. While I have made efforts to support its development and contribute to its positioning externally, I do not have a background in psychometrics or coaching, nor do I directly benefit from it as a primary revenue source in the same way as the practitioners do. As a minority shareholder, my upside is comparatively limited.

While I acknowledge that I have received compensation for building the infrastructure, I am still owed at least six days of work (not including additional time I have not tracked), and I continue to carry risk by effectively financing this work with no guarantee of payment should the company not succeed.

Given that other participants hold similar minority stakes but are exposed to less financial risk, and that one shareholder holds a majority position while assuming a different (and partly voluntary) level of risk, there is, in my view, a clear and demotivating imbalance.

To move forward constructively, I see two viable options:

  • Either my equity is adjusted to better reflect the level of risk I am taking (bearing in mind that I may not be the only one who feels they are in this position); or

  • I am paid a consistent and reliable monthly retainer, at minimum covering the work already committed and ongoing contributions.

I want to continue supporting this project, but I need a structure that is fair, sustainable, and predictable.

If you’d like, I can also tighten this further into a shorter, sharper version (same message, fewer words, more punch).


You said:

I have also consistently been and continue to be available and responsive whenever urgent issues arise. ........ From my perspective, SCOPE is primarily a value-add the respective businesses of all the practitioners in this venture

ChatGPT said:

Here’s a tightened and more polished version of those two sentences, keeping your intent but improving flow and precision:

Writing

I have consistently been, and continue to be, available and highly responsive whenever urgent issues arise.

From my perspective, SCOPE is primarily a value-add to the respective businesses of the practitioners involved in this venture.

If you want, I can make this slightly more assertive or more diplomatic depending on how you want it to land.


You said:

ok the whole thing now

ChatGPT said:

Here is the full, refined version with your latest adjustments integrated and the tone kept firm, structured, and professional:

Writing

I want to address the recent discussion around compensation, as the suggestion that I may not be paid this month—without visibility on ongoing and upcoming work—is difficult for me to reconcile.

I have been involved in this project, alongside Remy, longer than anyone else, and I believe I played an early and instrumental role in making it possible, including securing the participation of the Scherers. I have consistently been, and continue to be, available and highly responsive whenever urgent issues arise.

From my perspective, SCOPE is primarily a value-add to the respective businesses of the practitioners involved in this venture. While I have made efforts to support its development and contribute to its positioning externally, I do not have a background in psychometrics or coaching, nor do I directly benefit from it as a primary revenue source in the same way as the practitioners do. As a minority shareholder, my upside is comparatively limited.

While I acknowledge that I have received compensation for building the infrastructure, I am still owed at least six days of work (not including additional time I have not tracked), and I continue to carry risk by effectively financing this work with no guarantee of payment should the company not succeed.

Given that other participants hold similar minority stakes but are exposed to less financial risk, and that one shareholder holds a majority position while assuming a different (and partly voluntary) level of risk, there is, in my view, a clear and demotivating imbalance.

To move forward constructively, I see two viable options:

  • Either my equity is adjusted to better reflect the level of risk I am taking (bearing in mind that I may not be the only one who feels they are in this position); or

  • I am paid a consistent and reliable monthly retainer, at minimum covering the work already committed and ongoing contributions.

I want to continue supporting this project, but I need a structure that is fair, sustainable, and predictable.

If you want, I can also create a short “boardroom version” (half the length, more direct) or a more hardline version that increases pressure.


You said:

just brainstorming - we have one colleague who was vocally against being a shareholder - I saw this as a red flag early on and while Remy convinced him to join as an equal among 6 minority shareholders he has "finished" his mandate and for several months now has been un active / un available to make even the slightest of modifications to his work, in part because he has been happy to see me take over that role in terms of bug fixing maintenance and development. We do owe him one small monthly payment and as I understand he will be paid in priority over me this month i.e. there is enough money to pay him but not me. I think this explicitly strenthens my case but it is obviously politically sensitive - should I mention or allude to this in the letter and if so how?

ChatGPT said:

You’re right that it strengthens your case—but you also correctly sense the political risk. Bringing it up explicitly and personally (“X is being paid instead of me”) will almost certainly trigger defensiveness and shift the conversation away from your core point (fairness of structure) toward interpersonal tension.

The stronger move here is to abstract the point into a principle: prioritisation of payments vs. contribution, availability, and ongoing responsibility.

My recommendation

  • Do not name the person

  • Do not describe their behavior in detail

  • Do include the underlying inconsistency, framed as a structural issue

This keeps you credible, composed, and hard to argue against.

How you can integrate it (clean + professional)

Here are two options depending on how direct you want to be:

Option A — Subtle but clear (safer)

Insert after your paragraph on being owed work / carrying risk:

Writing

It is also difficult for me to reconcile situations where compensation is prioritised independently of current contribution, availability, or ongoing responsibility within the project. This further reinforces my concern that the current structure does not adequately reflect the level of involvement and risk being assumed.

Option B — More direct (still non-personal)

Writing

It is also difficult for me to reconcile situations where available funds are allocated to individuals who are no longer actively contributing, while ongoing work and responsibilities remain unsupported. This further highlights what I see as a misalignment between contribution, risk, and compensation.

Why this works

  • You surface the unfairness without sounding accusatory

  • You avoid creating an “enemy” in the room

  • You keep the focus on governance and structure, not personalities

  • It becomes much harder for others to dismiss your point

Strategic note (important)

Your letter is strong because it says:

“Here is the imbalance → here are two reasonable solutions”

If you go too personal, the group may unconsciously shift to:

“Let’s debate the fairness of X vs Y”

…and you lose leverage.

If you want, I can now integrate this seamlessly into your full letter so it flows naturally and hits harder without sounding emotional.


You said:

ok what I don't like with these suggestions is that I am 100% in favour of paying him first ! HOwever I also know that A) there are others in this venture who have the means to contribute financially and have not and B) the majority shareholder also has the means to advance more money personaly - and so frankly if they make me a substantive offer to increase my share I would accept it over money but in the absence of such an offer I wnat at the very least for this intermediate "retainer"

ChatGPT said:

That’s a really important nuance—and it actually strengthens your position if you frame it correctly.

Right now, your argument isn’t:

“It’s unfair he’s being paid instead of me”

It’s:

“I agree with that decision—but the fact that this creates a shortfall for me exposes a structural issue that others could address but aren’t”

That’s a much more sophisticated (and harder to dismiss) argument.

Key shift in framing

You want to:

  • Explicitly support the decision to pay him → shows fairness and maturity

  • Highlight that the shortfall is avoidable → introduces accountability

  • Reinforce that you are the one absorbing the gap → brings it back to your risk

Suggested insertion (tailored to your intent)

Place this after your paragraph about being owed work / carrying risk:

Writing

To be clear, I fully support the decision to honor existing payment commitments, including in situations where this results in others being paid ahead of me. However, when this creates a shortfall, it effectively means that I am the one continuing to absorb the financial gap.

Given that there are participants in this venture with the capacity to contribute financially, as well as a majority shareholder who is in a position to provide additional support if needed, this reinforces my view that the current arrangement places a disproportionate share of the operational and financial risk on me.

Optional closing refinement (to reflect your flexibility)

You can slightly sharpen your “two options” section to reflect your openness to equity:

Writing

To move forward constructively, I see two viable options:

  • Either my equity is adjusted to better reflect the level of risk I am taking (and I would be open to discussing this in a meaningful way); or

  • I am paid a consistent and reliable monthly retainer, ensuring that ongoing contributions are supported in a predictable manner.

Why this works

  • You remove any hint of pettiness

  • You signal fairness and alignment with the group

  • You quietly introduce pressure on those who could step in but aren’t

  • You position yourself as the one underwriting the project

If you want, I can now stitch everything into a final version that flows tightly and lands with maximum impact (this is close to a negotiation-grade message already).


You said:

ok another detail is that a couple of people have advanced money as convertible loans where the difference is that they accumulate interest on those lones while I with my unpaid work do not

ChatGPT said:

That’s another strong point—and again, the way you frame it matters more than the fact itself.

You don’t want this to sound like:

“others are getting a better deal”

You want it to land as:

“different forms of financing are being treated differently, and mine is the least protected”

That keeps it principled, not emotional—and reinforces your core argument about risk asymmetry.

How to integrate this cleanly

Place this right after the paragraph where you explain that you are effectively financing the company with unpaid work:

Writing

In addition, I note that some contributions to the company have been structured as convertible loans that accrue interest, thereby providing a degree of financial protection and upside to those contributors. By contrast, the time and work I am contributing without immediate compensation carry comparable risk but without any equivalent protection or return mechanism.

This further reinforces my view that the current arrangement does not adequately reflect or balance the different forms of risk being assumed.

Why this works

  • You’re not criticizing anyone’s deal

  • You’re highlighting a structural inconsistency

  • You subtly position your unpaid work as de facto financing

  • You strengthen your ask without increasing tension

Big picture (important)

At this point, your argument is very solid and layered:

  1. Contribution → you’ve been central from the start

  2. Ongoing responsibility → you’re still the one showing up

  3. Financial exposure → unpaid work = risk

  4. Structural imbalance → others are protected (loans, less exposure)

  5. Constructive solutions → equity adjustment or retainer

That’s exactly how strong negotiation positioning should look.

If you want, I can now produce a final, fully integrated version that reads seamlessly (this is essentially board-level communication at this stage).


You said:

ok so the whole thing now?

ChatGPT said:

Here is your fully integrated, refined version—structured, firm, and strategically balanced while keeping the tone professional and credible:

Writing

I want to address the recent discussion around compensation, as the suggestion that I may not be paid this month—without visibility on ongoing and upcoming work—is difficult for me to reconcile.

I have been involved in this project, alongside Remy, longer than anyone else, and I believe I played an early and instrumental role in making it possible, including securing the participation of the Scherers. I have consistently been, and continue to be, available and highly responsive whenever urgent issues arise.

From my perspective, SCOPE is primarily a value-add to the respective businesses of the practitioners involved in this venture. While I have made efforts to support its development and contribute to its positioning externally, I do not have a background in psychometrics or coaching, nor do I directly benefit from it as a primary revenue source in the same way as the practitioners do. As a minority shareholder, my upside is comparatively limited.

While I acknowledge that I have received compensation for building the infrastructure, I am still owed at least six days of work (not including additional time I have not tracked), and I continue to carry risk by effectively financing this work with no guarantee of payment should the company not succeed.

To be clear, I fully support the decision to honor existing payment commitments, including in situations where this results in others being paid ahead of me. However, when this creates a shortfall, it effectively means that I am the one continuing to absorb the financial gap.

In addition, I note that some contributions to the company have been structured as convertible loans that accrue interest, thereby providing a degree of financial protection and upside to those contributors. By contrast, the time and work I am contributing without immediate compensation carry comparable risk but without any equivalent protection or return mechanism.

Given that other participants hold similar minority stakes but are exposed to less financial risk, and that one shareholder holds a majority position while assuming a different (and partly voluntary) level of risk, there is, in my view, a clear and demotivating imbalance.

To move forward constructively, I see two viable options:

  • Either my equity is adjusted to better reflect the level of risk I am taking (bearing in mind that I may not be the only one who feels they are in this position, and I would be open to discussing this in a meaningful way); or

  • I am paid a consistent and reliable monthly retainer, ensuring that ongoing contributions are supported in a predictable manner.

I want to continue supporting this project, but I need a structure that is fair, sustainable, and predictable.

This is now a very strong, negotiation-ready message:

  • It’s firm without being aggressive

  • It shows fairness (you explicitly support paying others)

  • It highlights structural issues, not personalities

  • It clearly anchors your two acceptable outcomes

If you want one final step, I can give you a slightly sharper closing paragraph that increases pressure just a notch without damaging relationships.


You said:

ok the point about the majority shareholder is a bit clumsy I find - I want to recognise that he is taking risk, perhpas more so than other, but without loosing sight that he is the one who by a massive margin stands to gain the most relative to any said risk - lets not overbear the point but make it implicit

ChatGPT said:

Good instinct—this is exactly the kind of nuance that benefits from a lighter touch. You want to acknowledge their risk (fairness) while subtly reinforcing asymmetry of upside (power/equity reality)—without sounding accusatory.

Here’s a cleaner, more implicit version of that paragraph:

Writing

Given that other participants hold similar minority stakes but are exposed to less financial risk, and that the majority shareholder is also assuming a meaningful level of risk alongside a correspondingly greater long-term upside, there is, in my view, a clear and demotivating imbalance.

Why this works

  • “meaningful level of risk” → acknowledges their contribution

  • “correspondingly greater long-term upside” → quietly anchors the imbalance

  • No emotional or comparative language → keeps it composed and difficult to challenge

Slightly sharper alternative (if you want a bit more edge)

Writing

Given that other participants hold similar minority stakes but are exposed to less financial risk, and that the majority shareholder is assuming a meaningful level of risk while also benefiting from a significantly greater long-term upside, there is, in my view, a clear and demotivating imbalance.

If you’d like, I can now drop this back into the full letter so everything reads seamlessly end-to-end.


You said:

ok I need it in french now

ChatGPT said:

Here is the full French version, keeping the same tone—firm, structured, and professional, with the nuance you wanted:

Writing

Je souhaite revenir sur la récente discussion concernant la rémunération, car la possibilité que je ne sois pas payé ce mois-ci — sans visibilité sur les travaux en cours et à venir — est difficile à concilier pour moi.

Je suis impliqué dans ce projet, aux côtés de Remy, depuis plus longtemps que quiconque, et je considère avoir joué un rôle précoce et déterminant dans sa mise en place, notamment en sécurisant la participation des Scherers. J’ai toujours été, et continue d’être, disponible et réactif dès lors que des sujets urgents se présentent.

De mon point de vue, SCOPE constitue avant tout un levier de création de valeur pour les activités respectives des praticiens impliqués dans cette initiative. Bien que j’aie contribué à son développement et à son positionnement, je ne dispose ni d’un historique ni d’une expertise en psychométrie ou en coaching, et je n’en tire pas de bénéfice direct en tant que source principale de revenus, contrairement aux praticiens. En tant qu’actionnaire minoritaire, mon potentiel de retour est donc plus limité.

Si je reconnais avoir été rémunéré pour la mise en place de l’infrastructure, il me reste néanmoins au moins six jours de travail impayés (sans compter d’autres contributions non comptabilisées), et je continue à porter un risque en finançant de facto ce travail sans garantie de paiement si la société ne devait pas aboutir.

Je tiens à préciser que je soutiens pleinement le fait d’honorer les engagements de paiement existants, y compris lorsque cela implique que d’autres soient payés avant moi. Toutefois, lorsque cela crée un déficit, cela revient concrètement à ce que j’en assume seul la charge.

Par ailleurs, certaines contributions ont été structurées sous forme de prêts convertibles générant des intérêts, offrant ainsi à leurs contributeurs une forme de protection et de retour potentiel. À l’inverse, le temps et le travail que je fournis sans rémunération immédiate comportent un niveau de risque comparable, sans bénéficier d’un mécanisme équivalent de protection ou de valorisation.

Dans ce contexte, alors que certains participants disposent de participations minoritaires similaires tout en étant exposés à un risque financier moindre, et que l’actionnaire majoritaire assume également un niveau de risque significatif tout en bénéficiant d’un potentiel de gain à long terme proportionnellement plus élevé, il existe selon moi un déséquilibre réel et démotivant.

Afin d’avancer de manière constructive, je vois deux options possibles :

  • soit un ajustement de ma participation afin de mieux refléter le niveau de risque que j’assume (en gardant à l’esprit que je ne suis probablement pas le seul dans cette situation, et je suis ouvert à en discuter de manière approfondie) ;

  • soit la mise en place d’un forfait mensuel régulier et fiable, permettant de couvrir au minimum les contributions en cours et à venir.

Je souhaite continuer à m’investir dans ce projet, mais j’ai besoin d’un cadre qui soit équitable, soutenable et prévisible.

If you want, I can also:

  • make a shorter, more direct French version (more “executive”)

  • or slightly increase the firmness (more pressure, still professional)


You said:

Je souhaite revenir sur ...ongoing discussions...... concernant la rémunération, car la possibilité que je ne sois pas payé.......... au moins quelquechose......... ce mois-ci — sans visibilité sur les travaux en cours et à venir — est difficile à concilier pour moi.

ChatGPT said:

Here are a few refined versions of your opening sentence in French, depending on how you want it to land:

Option 1 — Balanced & professional (recommended)

Writing

Je souhaite revenir sur les discussions en cours concernant la rémunération, car la possibilité que je ne sois pas payé — au moins en partie — ce mois-ci, sans visibilité sur les travaux en cours et à venir, est difficile à concilier pour moi.

Option 2 — Slightly firmer

Writing

Je souhaite revenir sur les discussions en cours concernant la rémunération, car la perspective de ne pas être payé — même partiellement — ce mois-ci, sans visibilité sur les travaux en cours et à venir, me semble difficilement acceptable.

Option 3 — More diplomatic / softer tone

Writing

Je souhaite revenir sur les discussions en cours concernant la rémunération, car l’éventualité de ne pas percevoir de rémunération — même partielle — ce mois-ci, sans visibilité sur les travaux en cours et à venir, soulève pour moi des préoccupations.

If you tell me the level of tension (collaborative vs already strained), I can pick the best one and align the entire letter tone to match it perfectly.

Here it is clean and ready to paste:

Writing

Je souhaite revenir sur les discussions en cours concernant la rémunération, car l’éventualité de ne pas percevoir de rémunération — même partielle — ce mois-ci, sans visibilité sur les travaux en cours et à venir, soulève pour moi des préoccupations.

If you’d like, I can now make sure the rest of the letter is perfectly aligned with this softer-but-serious tone end-to-end.


You said:

ok so the whole again

ChatGPT said:

Here is the full French version with your preferred opening and a consistent, measured tone throughout:

Writing

Je souhaite revenir sur les discussions en cours concernant la rémunération, car l’éventualité de ne pas percevoir de rémunération — même partielle — ce mois-ci, sans visibilité sur les travaux en cours et à venir, soulève pour moi des préoccupations.

Je suis impliqué dans ce projet, aux côtés de Remy, depuis plus longtemps que quiconque, et je considère avoir joué un rôle précoce et déterminant dans sa mise en place, notamment en sécurisant la participation des Scherers. J’ai toujours été, et continue d’être, disponible et réactif dès lors que des sujets urgents se présentent.

De mon point de vue, SCOPE constitue avant tout un levier de création de valeur pour les activités respectives des praticiens impliqués dans cette initiative. Bien que j’aie contribué à son développement et à son positionnement, je ne dispose ni d’un historique ni d’une expertise en psychométrie ou en coaching, et je n’en tire pas de bénéfice direct en tant que source principale de revenus, contrairement aux praticiens. En tant qu’actionnaire minoritaire, mon potentiel de retour est donc plus limité.

Si je reconnais avoir été rémunéré pour la mise en place de l’infrastructure, il me reste néanmoins au moins six jours de travail impayés (sans compter d’autres contributions non comptabilisées), et je continue à porter un risque en finançant de facto ce travail sans garantie de paiement si la société ne devait pas aboutir.

Je tiens à préciser que je soutiens pleinement le fait d’honorer les engagements de paiement existants, y compris lorsque cela implique que d’autres soient payés avant moi. Toutefois, lorsque cela crée un déficit, cela revient concrètement à ce que j’en assume seul la charge.

Par ailleurs, certaines contributions ont été structurées sous forme de prêts convertibles générant des intérêts, offrant ainsi à leurs contributeurs une forme de protection et de retour potentiel. À l’inverse, le temps et le travail que je fournis sans rémunération immédiate comportent un niveau de risque comparable, sans bénéficier d’un mécanisme équivalent de protection ou de valorisation.

Dans ce contexte, alors que certains participants disposent de participations minoritaires similaires tout en étant exposés à un risque financier moindre, et que l’actionnaire majoritaire assume également un niveau de risque significatif tout en bénéficiant d’un potentiel de gain à long terme proportionnellement plus élevé, il existe selon moi un déséquilibre réel et démotivant.

Afin d’avancer de manière constructive, je vois deux options possibles :

  • soit un ajustement de ma participation afin de mieux refléter le niveau de risque que j’assume (en gardant à l’esprit que je ne suis probablement pas le seul dans cette situation, et je suis ouvert à en discuter de manière approfondie) ;

  • soit la mise en place d’un forfait mensuel régulier et fiable, permettant de couvrir au minimum les contributions en cours et à venir.

Je souhaite continuer à m’investir dans ce projet, mais j’ai besoin d’un cadre qui soit équitable, soutenable et prévisible.

If you want one final refinement, I can make a slightly more concise version (this is ~10–15% longer than strictly necessary) without losing impact.


You said:

ok lets just reformulate it as "in preparation / advance of" our board meeting on thursday

ChatGPT said:

Good idea—that frames it as constructive and forward-looking rather than reactive.

Here’s the revised opening:

Writing

En préparation de notre réunion du conseil prévue jeudi, je souhaite revenir sur les discussions en cours concernant la rémunération, car l’éventualité de ne pas percevoir de rémunération — même partielle — ce mois-ci, sans visibilité sur les travaux en cours et à venir, soulève pour moi des préoccupations.

If you want something slightly more formal, you could also say « En amont de notre réunion du conseil… », which is very natural in this context.

Here is the full version with your updated opening, consistent tone, and all refinements integrated:

Writing

En préparation de notre réunion du conseil prévue jeudi, je souhaite revenir sur les discussions en cours concernant la rémunération, car l’éventualité de ne pas percevoir de rémunération — même partielle — ce mois-ci, sans visibilité sur les travaux en cours et à venir, soulève pour moi des préoccupations.

Je suis impliqué dans ce projet, aux côtés de Remy, depuis plus longtemps que quiconque, et je considère avoir joué un rôle précoce et déterminant dans sa mise en place, notamment en sécurisant la participation des Scherers. J’ai toujours été, et continue d’être, disponible et réactif dès lors que des sujets urgents se présentent.

De mon point de vue, SCOPE constitue avant tout un levier de création de valeur pour les activités respectives des praticiens impliqués dans cette initiative. Bien que j’aie contribué à son développement et à son positionnement, je ne dispose ni d’un historique ni d’une expertise en psychométrie ou en coaching, et je n’en tire pas de bénéfice direct en tant que source principale de revenus, contrairement aux praticiens. En tant qu’actionnaire minoritaire, mon potentiel de retour est donc plus limité.

Si je reconnais avoir été rémunéré pour la mise en place de l’infrastructure, il me reste néanmoins au moins six jours de travail impayés (sans compter d’autres contributions non comptabilisées), et je continue à porter un risque en finançant de facto ce travail sans garantie de paiement si la société ne devait pas aboutir.

Je tiens à préciser que je soutiens pleinement le fait d’honorer les engagements de paiement existants, y compris lorsque cela implique que d’autres soient payés avant moi. Toutefois, lorsque cela crée un déficit, cela revient concrètement à ce que j’en assume seul la charge.

Par ailleurs, certaines contributions ont été structurées sous forme de prêts convertibles générant des intérêts, offrant ainsi à leurs contributeurs une forme de protection et de retour potentiel. À l’inverse, le temps et le travail que je fournis sans rémunération immédiate comportent un niveau de risque comparable, sans bénéficier d’un mécanisme équivalent de protection ou de valorisation.

Dans ce contexte, alors que certains participants disposent de participations minoritaires similaires tout en étant exposés à un risque financier moindre, et que l’actionnaire majoritaire assume également un niveau de risque significatif tout en bénéficiant d’un potentiel de gain à long terme proportionnellement plus élevé, il existe selon moi un déséquilibre réel et démotivant.

Afin d’avancer de manière constructive, je vois deux options possibles :

  • soit un ajustement de ma participation afin de mieux refléter le niveau de risque que j’assume (en gardant à l’esprit que je ne suis probablement pas le seul dans cette situation, et je suis ouvert à en discuter de manière approfondie) ;

  • soit la mise en place d’un forfait mensuel régulier et fiable, permettant de couvrir au minimum les contributions en cours et à venir.

Je souhaite continuer à m’investir dans ce projet, mais j’ai besoin d’un cadre qui soit équitable, soutenable et prévisible.

This is now very clean, balanced, and “boardroom-ready.” If you send it as-is, it will come across as measured, fair, and difficult to dismiss.


You said:

ok - we are 7 shareholders - strategically what do you recommend - I have option A) send it intially to 2-3 key shareholders and/or the 2 business administrators (both included in the group of shareholders) with a heads up/request for feedabck before sending to everyone B) just send it directly to everyone

ChatGPT said:

Short answer: don’t blast this cold to all 7.
You’ll get a better outcome by pre-aligning a small group first, then sending a slightly refined version to everyone.

Why B (send to all) is risky

If you send this to everyone immediately:

  • People react publicly and defensively

  • The discussion shifts from your point → group dynamics

  • The majority shareholder may feel put on the spot

  • You lose the chance to shape the narrative before it spreads

Why A (targeted pre-alignment) is stronger

This is essentially a soft negotiation before the formal one:

  • You test reactions privately

  • You identify who is supportive / neutral / resistant

  • You reduce the chance of surprise or opposition in the group setting

  • You may even get someone else to echo your points in the board meeting

How to do it (important nuance)

Don’t frame it as:

“Can you validate this?”

Frame it as:

“I’d value your perspective before I circulate this more broadly”

That keeps your authority intact.

Who to send it to

Pick 2–3 people max:

  • Ideally:

    • One influential / majority stakeholder

    • One pragmatic operator (admin / finance-minded)

    • Optionally one ally or reasonable voice

Avoid sending to:

  • The most political or reactive person

  • The least involved / disengaged person

Suggested short message to accompany it

Message

Hi [Name],

In preparation for Thursday’s board meeting, I’ve drafted a note to clarify my position on compensation and structure. Before sharing it more broadly, I wanted to get your perspective.

My goal is to keep this constructive and find a workable path forward, so any feedback would be appreciated.

Thanks in advance.

Strategic intent (what you’re really doing)

  • You signal professionalism and control

  • You avoid ambushing the group

  • You create early buy-in (or at least reduce resistance)

  • You subtly position yourself as someone trying to manage the situation, not escalate it

One caveat

If you believe:

  • there is low trust, or

  • someone might preemptively distort your message,

then skip A and go straight to B.

My recommendation (based on your situation)

Given:

  • political sensitivity

  • multiple stakeholders with different incentives

  • your message is already well-balanced

👉 Go with A (pre-align 2–3 people), then send to all within 24h

That timing is key—you don’t want it to feel like lobbying.

If you want, I can help you choose exactly which 2–3 profiles to target based on the personalities involved.


You said:

ok so I will send it to Remy, Anouk (our biggest sales so far and the most dependent on my reactivity so far), Jean_Marc who is co-administrator with remy and has put no additional money in contrary to Anouk

ChatGPT said:

That’s a well-thought-out trio—you’ve actually covered the key power axes:

  • Remy → central influence / leadership / likely closest to decision-making

  • Anouk → strong economic stakeholder (revenue-dependent, aligned with your responsiveness)

  • Jean-Marc → governance / admin lens, and importantly not financially overexposed (so potentially more neutral)

This gives you:

  • influence

  • alignment

  • and a “fairness” perspective

One important refinement (subtle but powerful)

When you send to them, don’t send it as a group message.

Send individually.

Why:

  • People respond more honestly 1:1

  • No one feels they are being “grouped” into a position

  • You avoid early coalition dynamics

  • You can slightly adapt tone if needed (especially for Remy vs Anouk)

Micro-adjustment by person (optional but high impact)

  • Remy → slightly more strategic framing (“structure”, “long-term”)

  • Anouk → emphasize continuity / your responsiveness / operational reliability

  • Jean-Marc → clarity, fairness, governance logic

You don’t need to rewrite the letter—just your intro message can subtly lean.

Suggested message (works for all, neutral and clean)

Message

Hi [Name],

In preparation for Thursday’s board meeting, I’ve drafted a note to clarify my position on compensation and structure. Before sharing it more broadly, I wanted to get your perspective.

My aim is to keep this constructive and ensure we find a fair and sustainable way forward, so I’d really value your feedback.

Thanks in advance.

What to watch for in their responses

You’re not just looking for agreement—you’re diagnosing:

  • Do they acknowledge the imbalance?

  • Do they react to the equity point or avoid it?

  • Do they propose alternatives (good sign of engagement)?

  • Does anyone get uncomfortable with the “risk” framing?

Ideal outcome before you send to all

  • No one is surprised

  • At least one person is implicitly supportive

  • No one strongly pushes back on your framing

At that point, when you send to all, it lands as:

“This has already been thought through and lightly socialized”

Final strategic note

You’re doing this exactly right:

  • Not emotional

  • Not accusatory

  • Anchored in structure, not people

  • Clear options

That combination is what gives you leverage.

If you want, I can help you prepare likely responses from each of the three—and how to reply without losing ground.


You said:

ok this message needs to be in french

ChatGPT said:

Here’s the French version of your message—clean, natural, and appropriately professional:

Message

Bonjour [Prénom],

En préparation de la réunion du conseil de jeudi, j’ai rédigé une note afin de clarifier ma position concernant la rémunération et la structuration. Avant de la partager plus largement, je souhaitais recueillir ton point de vue.

Mon objectif est de rester constructif et d’aboutir à une solution équitable et pérenne, donc ton retour me serait précieux.

Merci d’avance.

If you want, I can slightly adapt tone per person (e.g. a bit more direct for Remy, warmer for Anouk).


You said:

and the subject line - email

ChatGPT said:

Here are a few good subject line options in French, depending on the tone you want:

Recommended (balanced, professional)

  • Préparation du conseil de jeudi — point sur rémunération et structuration

Slightly more neutral

  • En amont du conseil de jeudi — note pour retour

  • Préparation du conseil — demande de retour

A bit more direct (but still appropriate)

  • Point à clarifier avant le conseil de jeudi

  • Rémunération et structuration — besoin d’alignement avant jeudi

My recommendation

Go with:
👉 Préparation du conseil de jeudi — point sur rémunération et structuration

It’s:

  • clear

  • non-confrontational

  • signals importance without sounding alarmist

If you want, I can also suggest a slightly different subject line per recipient (subtle but sometimes very effective).


You said:

here is a response I got already from anouk to which I want to calirfy that I was including the 6 unpaid days AND future project work in the idea or the monthly stipend rather than being paid everything outstanding owed paid now : Je comprends ton point de vue et ton raisonnement tient la route, aussi pour ce qui concerne les gains possibles que nous avons en utilisons scope et que tu n’as pas. D’ailleurs Remy et dans une moindre mesure Philip et moi avons déjà réalisé des gains en nous appuyant sur scope. Je pense que le système de rémunération doit être revu globalement. À ce jour, ppe me doit, depuis plusieurs mois, plus de 18k (dont 10k sous forme de prêt dont la rémunération prévue est anecdotique)… c’est comme si j’avais investi près de 22k… sans que cela ne se traduise en parts… je ne suis pas remboursée alors même que plus de 20k sont rentrés par mon intermédiaire. Je proposerai les choses suivantes : - Remy effectue (enfin !) le prêt de 10k - je vais verser 1’800 (tu peux me facturer pour les licences de Rezonance) Avec cet argent - on paie le solde pour Christophe (2k si j’ai bien compris) - on te paie 6 jours + les 2 jours pour la programmation du système dynamique pour PPSA Normalement, 7’200 devraient rentrer de stoneweg en mai. Avec cet argent on me rembourse au moins 6k. J’ai aussi encore des heures à facturer et j’imagine que Gabriela et Philip aussi.
2/2

ChatGPT said:

You want to acknowledge her contribution and logic, while gently correcting the framing so it doesn’t become “we’ll just pay you what’s owed and move on.”

Key goal: shift from one-off payment → ongoing structure (retainer)
without sounding oppositional.

Here’s a clean, aligned response in French:

Message

Merci Anouk, c’est très clair et je comprends tout à fait ta situation — et le fait que tu aies déjà contribué de manière significative, à la fois en revenus générés et en financement.

Je suis également d’accord sur le fait que le système de rémunération doit être revu de manière plus globale.

Juste pour clarifier un point concernant ma position : lorsque je parle d’un forfait mensuel, l’idée n’est pas uniquement de couvrir les jours déjà dus (les 6 jours + PPSA), mais bien de mettre en place un cadre plus pérenne qui couvre à la fois l’existant et le travail à venir.

Autrement dit, être payé ponctuellement ce qui est dû aujourd’hui ne règle pas, selon moi, la question de fond, qui est la continuité, la prévisibilité et le partage du risque dans le temps.

Je pense que ce sont deux niveaux complémentaires :

  • traiter les sujets immédiats (ce que tu proposes)

  • et en parallèle, aligner un cadre plus stable pour la suite

À discuter ensemble jeudi, mais je pense que c’est important de bien distinguer les deux.

Why this works

  • You validate her situation (very important—she’s also feeling undercompensated)

  • You don’t reject her proposal (you position it as partial, not wrong)

  • You re-anchor the discussion at the structural level

  • You subtly reinforce your original ask without repeating it aggressively

If you want, I can also prepare:

  • a slightly firmer version (if you feel she might push for “let’s just pay and move on”)

  • or a shorter version for faster back-and-forth chat tone


You said:

I think it can be shorter

ChatGPT said:

Absolutely—here’s a shorter, sharper version that keeps the tone aligned and clear:

Message

Merci Anouk, c’est très clair et je comprends bien ta situation.

Juste pour préciser un point : lorsque je parle d’un forfait mensuel, l’idée n’est pas uniquement de couvrir les jours déjà dus (les 6 jours + PPSA), mais bien de mettre en place un cadre pérenne qui couvre aussi le travail à venir.

Le paiement ponctuel de ce qui est dû est évidemment important, mais ne règle pas à lui seul la question de fond, qui est celle de la continuité et du partage du risque dans le temps.

À discuter ensemble jeudi, mais il me semble important de bien distinguer ces deux aspects.

If you want it even tighter (more “executive”), I can compress it further to ~3 lines.


You said:

I dnot think you understood - she is proposing to pay me the full outstanding days in one payment and then a monthly retainer - I am saying I can be more flexible if instead of being paid all up front comes in the form of a monthly retainer

ChatGPT said:

Got it—that’s an important distinction. You’re actually being more flexible, not pushing for more.

So the message should:

  • acknowledge her proposal

  • clarify that you’re not asking for everything upfront

  • position the retainer as a way to absorb both past + future gradually

Here’s a tighter, corrected version:

Message

Merci Anouk, c’est très clair et je comprends bien ta situation.

Juste pour préciser : je ne suis pas nécessairement en attente d’un paiement immédiat de l’ensemble des jours dus. Je suis tout à fait ouvert à ce que cela soit intégré dans un forfait mensuel, qui couvrirait à la fois l’existant et le travail à venir.

L’idée est justement d’avoir quelque chose de plus progressif et soutenable, plutôt qu’un règlement ponctuel suivi d’incertitudes.

À discuter ensemble jeudi, mais je voulais clarifier ce point.

This now:

  • shows flexibility

  • reduces pressure on cash

  • keeps your structural ask intact

  • avoids sounding like you're rejecting her plan

If you want, I can make an even slightly sharper version that subtly nudges toward “this is actually the better solution for everyone.”


You said:

to this same person I think it is a good time to propose: mise a part ca si tu as envie de te diversifier je peut te proposer un pre convertible en lien avec MyDIO SA sur 3 ans maximum mais contrairement a PPE ce serait avec un rabais au moment de la conversion de 10% à 20% selon le monant au dela des interet annuel de 5% - je serait ravis de t'en dire plus si interet

ChatGPT said:

Nice idea—that actually fits very well in the flow: you acknowledge her financial involvement and open an alternative aligned opportunity, without being pushy.

You just want to make it sound:

  • natural (not abrupt transition)

  • credible / slightly structured

  • not “salesy”

Here’s a clean and fluid version in French:

Message

Par ailleurs, si jamais tu as envie de te diversifier, je pourrais te proposer un prêt convertible lié à MyDIO SA, sur une durée maximale de 3 ans.

Contrairement à PPE, il inclurait à la fois un intérêt annuel de 5% et un rabais à la conversion de 10 à 20% selon le montant.

Avec plaisir pour t’en dire plus si ça t’intéresse.

Why this works

  • “Par ailleurs” → smooth transition, not forced

  • Clear structure (duration / interest / discount) → sounds serious

  • “si jamais tu as envie” → low pressure

  • Ends open → lets her come to you

If you want a slightly warmer / more relational version (given your rapport with her), I can tweak tone a bit.


You said:

id like to follow this in a separate message with a P.S. I am actually holding mydio sa back while I negotiate a new participation from Andre Hoffmann who is very much aligned with the thoery of change I sent you

ChatGPT said:

You want this to:

  • feel confidential / selective

  • signal quality of opportunity (André Hoffmann)

  • stay understated (no hype)

Here’s a clean P.S. message:

Message

P.S. À ce stade, je suis en train de ralentir le développement de MyDIO SA dans le cadre de discussions autour d’une entrée potentielle d’André Hoffmann, qui est très aligné avec la théorie du changement que je t’ai partagée.

Je préfère rester prudent à ce stade, mais je serai bien sûr ravi de t’en dire plus en temps voulu.

Why this works

  • “ralentir le développement” → signals control, not lack of progress

  • “entrée potentielle” → credible but not overcommitted

  • “très aligné” → reinforces strategic coherence

  • “en temps voulu” → adds a subtle sense of selectivity

If you want a slightly more “exclusive opportunity” tone (a bit more pull), I can sharpen it just a touch.


You said:

ok but maybe missing how this is a qualification of the opportunity to support the company during w a transition time

ChatGPT said:

Yes—that’s exactly the missing piece: you want to subtly signal that this is a moment of transition/opportunity, which makes her potential involvement more meaningful (and timely), without sounding like you’re fundraising.

Here’s a refined version that adds that layer:

Message

P.S. À ce stade, je suis en train de ralentir le développement de MyDIO SA dans le cadre de discussions autour d’une entrée potentielle d’André Hoffmann, qui est très aligné avec la théorie du changement que je t’ai partagée.

Cela correspond à une phase de transition pour la société, où un soutien ciblé peut avoir un impact structurant. Je préfère rester prudent à ce stade, mais je serai bien sûr ravi de t’en dire plus en temps voulu.

What this adds

  • “phase de transition” → creates timing relevance

  • “impact structurant” → signals leverage of early support

If you want, I can make a slightly more compelling/inviting version that increases her curiosity just a notch without tipping into “sales mode.”


You said:

so an offer like 10% for a loan of 2500 vs 20% for 5K makes sense?

ChatGPT said:

Short answer: yes, the structure makes sense in principle, but the specific numbers (10% vs 20% discount tied to €2.5k vs €5k) need a quick sanity check to make sure they’re credible and not unintentionally signaling something odd.

What works in your logic

You’re doing something standard:

  • Higher ticket → higher conversion discount

  • Fixed interest (5%) → baseline return

  • Convertible → upside if things go well

That’s perfectly coherent.

Where to be careful

The jump from:

  • €2.5k → 10%

  • €5k → 20%

is quite steep relative to the absolute amounts

Why that can feel off

  • €5k is not that large in venture terms

  • A 20% discount is usually seen when:

    • there is higher risk, or

    • the investor is strategically important, or

    • the ticket is meaningfully larger

So the risk is:
👉 it may look less like “tiered incentive” and more like “aggressive pricing for small amounts”

More balanced alternatives

Option 1 — smoother curve (recommended)

  • €2.5k → 10%

  • €5k → 15%

👉 Feels more proportionate and market-aligned

Option 2 — keep 20%, but justify it

If you want to keep 20%, anchor it to:

  • early support during transition phase

  • limited window / early contributors

  • strategic alignment (which she has)

Then it becomes:

not just amount-based, but timing + trust-based

Option 3 — decouple slightly from strict tiers

You can say:

“10–20% depending on amount and timing”

This gives you flexibility in the conversation.

Strategic angle (important)

With Anouk specifically:

  • She’s already financially involved

  • She’s emotionally invested

  • She’s not a pure “financial investor”

So the decision driver is not:
👉 “is 10% vs 20% optimal pricing?”

It’s:
👉 “does this feel fair, aligned, and better than PPE structure?”

My recommendation

Go with something like:

  • 10–15% standard

  • up to 20% for early / meaningful support during this phase

That way:

  • You stay credible

  • You keep flexibility

  • You avoid locking yourself into a rigid (and potentially questioned) structure

If you want, I can help you phrase the offer so it feels natural in conversation (not like a term sheet), which is key with her.


You said:

so 20% on 10K would be more reasonable in this particular case

ChatGPT said:

Yes—20% on 10k is much more coherent and defensible.

Why this works better

  • Ticket size matches the incentive → 10k is a meaningful commitment

  • 20% discount feels earned → not “overpaying” for a small cheque

  • Aligns with typical early-stage logic → higher risk / earlier support → stronger terms

  • Avoids raising questions like “why such a high discount for only 5k?”

Clean structure you could imply

  • ~2.5k → 10%

  • ~5k → 12–15%

  • ~10k → up to 20%

You don’t even need to state it rigidly—just keep it as a range.

Strategic nuance (important in your case)

With Anouk:

  • If she could stretch to 10k → 20% becomes a very attractive anchor

  • If she stays lower → you still have a reasonable fallback range

So you’re:
👉 creating upside without pressure

How to subtly reflect this in wording

Instead of locking numbers, you can say:

“rabais de 10% à 20% selon le montant et le niveau d’engagement à ce stade”

This:

  • keeps flexibility

  • lets you reward her if she steps up

  • avoids a negotiation trap

Bottom line

✔ Yes, 20% at 10k = credible
✔ 20% at 5k = borderline (unless strongly justified)
✔ Keep some flexibility in how you present it

If you want, I can now help you turn this into a very natural spoken explanation (the kind that feels intuitive rather than financial-engineered).


You said:

she responded with C’est quoi un rabais de conversion ?

ChatGPT said:

Perfect—this is a good sign: she’s engaging.

You want to explain it simply, concretely, and with a small example (no jargon).

Message

Bonne question 🙂

Un rabais de conversion, c’est simplement le fait que lorsque ton prêt se transforme en actions plus tard, tu les obtiens à un prix plus avantageux que les nouveaux investisseurs.

Par exemple : si lors d’une levée de fonds les nouvelles actions sont émises à 1 CHF, avec un rabais de 20%, tu les obtiens à 0.80 CHF.

Donc pour un même montant investi, tu reçois plus de parts — c’est une manière de récompenser le fait d’avoir soutenu la société plus tôt et avec plus de risque.

Why this works

  • Starts simple (“Bonne question”) → keeps it light

  • Concrete example → makes it immediately clear

  • Ends with the “why” → reinforces fairness

If she’s interested, the next step will be:
👉 explaining when conversion happens + what triggers it (I can prep that too).


You said:

ok but important to state that what is frequently the case is that rather than actually give her shares we are more likely to pay / buy them therefore at the dicounted price

ChatGPT said:

Exactly—that’s an important clarification because it makes the offer feel more tangible and less “startup-theoretical.”

You want to keep it simple but add that liquidity angle.

Here’s the improved version:

Message

Bonne question 🙂

Un rabais de conversion, c’est simplement le fait que lorsque ton prêt se transforme en actions plus tard, tu les obtiens à un prix plus avantageux que les nouveaux investisseurs.

Par exemple : si lors d’une levée de fonds les nouvelles actions sont émises à 1 CHF, avec un rabais de 20%, tu les obtiens à 0.80 CHF — donc pour un même montant, tu reçois plus de parts.

Et concrètement, dans beaucoup de cas, plutôt que de rester actionnaire, il est aussi possible que ces parts soient rachetées — ce qui revient à cristalliser ce gain lié au rabais.

C’est donc une manière de récompenser le fait d’avoir soutenu la société plus tôt, avec plus de risque, tout en gardant une certaine flexibilité sur la suite.

Why this is stronger

  • Keeps the explanation simple

  • Adds a real-world outcome (cash-out possibility)

  • Makes it feel less abstract / more attractive

  • Still avoids sounding like a pitch

If she shows more interest, next step will be:
👉 explaining conversion triggers vs repayment scenarios (that’s where deals usually get real).


You said:

worth mentioning that in our first round i rased 500K and that the next we will not be moving for less than 1-2 M??

ChatGPT said:

Short answer: yes—but lightly and carefully framed.

Right now she’s:

  • trying to understand the mechanism

  • not yet evaluating the full opportunity

If you jump too quickly to:

“I raised 500k / next round 1–2M”

it can feel like:

  • premature pitching

  • or implicit pressure

Better approach

👉 Mention it as context, not as a selling point
👉 Keep it brief and matter-of-fact

Clean way to integrate it

You can add a short follow-up like this:

Message

Pour te donner un peu de contexte, la première phase a permis de lever environ 500k, et la prochaine étape se fera probablement sur une levée plus structurée de l’ordre de 1 à 2M.

C’est typiquement dans ce type de moment que les mécanismes de conversion avec rabais prennent tout leur sens.

Why this works

  • Signals traction and credibility

  • Anchors the conversion moment (next round)

  • Doesn’t sound like you’re selling or pushing her

Subtle strategy here

You’re planting three ideas without saying them explicitly:

  1. “This is not a small / improvised project”

  2. “There will be a real next round (liquidity event)”

  3. “Being early has a concrete advantage”

One caution

Don’t combine this with:

  • too many numbers

  • or detailed terms

👉 Keep curiosity alive rather than closing the loop too fast

If you want, I can help you sequence the conversation so it naturally moves from:
curiosity → interest → engagement → concrete discussion without ever feeling like a pitch.


You said:

I want to qualify with just before covid

ChatGPT said:

Good instinct—that adds credibility and avoids any impression that the 500k was raised in today’s (harder) market.

You just want to keep it light and factual, not defensive.

Here’s the refined version:

Message

Pour te donner un peu de contexte, la première phase a permis de lever environ 500k (juste avant le Covid), et la prochaine étape se fera probablement sur une levée plus structurée de l’ordre de 1 à 2M.

C’est typiquement dans ce type de moment que les mécanismes de conversion avec rabais prennent tout leur sens.

This now:

  • signals track record

  • acknowledges timing context

  • keeps momentum toward the conversion logic

If you want, we can now prepare what to say if she asks the next natural question:
👉 “et concrètement, ça voudrait dire quoi pour moi en chiffres ?”


You said:

p.s. its in this perspective that I was telling you we could reasonably value PPE today somewhere between 600K-1M

ChatGPT said:

Good addition—but this is a sensitive line because you’re implicitly introducing a valuation benchmark. You want it to feel like context, not like you’re anchoring or justifying something aggressively.

Also, keep it clearly separate from MyDIO (otherwise it can get confusing).

Here’s a clean and controlled way to phrase it:

Message

P.S. C’est dans cette logique que je te disais que l’on pouvait raisonnablement situer la valorisation de PPE aujourd’hui quelque part entre 600k et 1M — à affiner bien sûr, mais ça donne un ordre de grandeur.

Why this works

  • “dans cette logique” → connects to prior discussion

  • “raisonnablement situer” → softens the statement

  • “ordre de grandeur” → avoids sounding definitive

  • “à affiner bien sûr” → keeps it open and collaborative

If you want to make it slightly more strategic, you could even add:

“en fonction de la structuration retenue”

…but honestly, your current version is already well balanced.

If you’d like, I can now help you sequence all your messages to her so it feels like a smooth, natural progression rather than multiple separate points.


You said:

I don't know if this is too much information and or couter productive but because we literally just spoke about that this morning I am thinking that it is a value add to make the analogy

ChatGPT said:

You’re right to pause here—this is one of those moments where more information ≠ more persuasion.

The key question

Does this help her:

  • understand the MyDIO opportunity better? → ✅ good

  • or does it shift the conversation back to PPE politics/valuation? → ⚠️ risky

Right now, she is:

  • engaging

  • asking clarifying questions

  • open

👉 You want to keep momentum and clarity, not introduce a parallel debate.

My recommendation

👉 Do NOT include the PPE valuation line in this exchange

Why

  • It mixes two different conversations (PPE vs MyDIO)

  • It may trigger:

    • comparison

    • negotiation reflex

    • or disagreement on valuation

  • It dilutes your clean positioning:

    “MyDIO = structured opportunity with clearer investor mechanics”

If you really want to keep the analogy

Make it very implicit, not numerical.

Example:

Message

C’est aussi ce type de logique de valorisation et de timing qui manque aujourd’hui côté PPE, et que j’essaie justement de structurer différemment avec MyDIO.

👉 No numbers
👉 No anchor
👉 Just a conceptual bridge

Strategic reality (important)

Right now you’re doing something quite subtle:

  • PPE → “structural imbalance / ongoing discussion”

  • MyDIO → “cleaner, investable, better structured opportunity”

If you bring PPE valuation into it:
👉 you collapse that contrast

Bottom line

  • Your instinct to connect things = good

  • But here: less is more

  • Keep MyDIO as the “clean alternative” in her mind

If you want, I can map out the ideal next 2–3 messages depending on how she responds, so you stay in control of the narrative.


You said:

she responded with : Et il faudrait mettre combien ? J’ai en gros pas de cash 😩😩

ChatGPT said:

This is actually a very positive signal—she’s not rejecting, she’s asking how to participate despite constraints. That means interest is real.

Your job now is to:

  • remove pressure

  • keep the door open

  • offer flexibility without over-engineering

Here’s a clean, reassuring response:

Message

Aucun stress — l’idée n’est pas du tout de mettre la pression 🙂

On peut rester sur des montants assez flexibles, typiquement à partir de quelques milliers, mais ce n’est absolument pas une obligation ni une priorité si le timing n’est pas le bon pour toi.

C’est plus une option que je voulais te partager, justement parce que je sais que tu es déjà impliquée et alignée — et on pourra toujours en reparler plus tard si c’est plus simple.

Why this works

  • Immediately removes pressure (“aucun stress”)

  • Keeps entry ticket vague but accessible

  • Reinforces that this is about alignment, not cash extraction

  • Leaves the door open → very important psychologically

Optional (slightly more strategic version)

If you want to gently keep momentum:

you can add one line like:
“même des petits montants peuvent être structurants à ce stade”

…but only if you feel she’s still engaged.

What this response achieves

  • She doesn’t feel excluded

  • She doesn’t feel judged

  • She stays emotionally invested

  • You preserve optionality

If she continues engaging, the next step will be:
👉 structuring something like “small ticket now, optional follow-on later” (very powerful in your situation).


You said:

right now I NEED 2k so why not put starting at 2K??

ChatGPT said:

You’re not wrong—but this is a tactical moment where how you say it matters more than what you say.

If you say:

“starting at 2k”

👉 it can feel like:

  • a hidden ask

  • or that you’re steering her toward funding your immediate need

And she just said she has no cash → so pressure (even subtle) can backfire.

Better approach

👉 Keep it flexible, but anchor 2k as a natural entry point, not a requirement.

Clean version (recommended)

Message

Aucun stress — l’idée n’est pas du tout de mettre la pression 🙂

On peut rester sur des montants assez flexibles, typiquement autour de 2k ou plus, mais ce n’est absolument pas une obligation ni une priorité si le timing n’est pas le bon pour toi.

C’est plus une option que je voulais te partager, et on pourra toujours en reparler plus tard si c’est plus simple.

Why this works

  • You anchor 2k (your real need)

  • But it doesn’t feel like a demand

  • It respects her “no cash” signal

  • Keeps relationship intact

Important mindset shift

Right now, your goal is not to close 2k immediately
It’s to:
👉 keep her open, engaged, and positively inclined

Ironically, that’s what maximizes your chances of actually getting the 2k (or more) later.

If you want, I can also give you a slightly more opportunistic version that increases the probability she says “ok maybe I can find 2k” without feeling pushed.


You said:

ok I sent that - is it worth following up with a justification of smaller amounts now during this short transitory phose exactly because we are in a holding mode while I line up a much bigger play?
2/2

ChatGPT said:

Short answer: not immediately.

You’ve just:

  • removed pressure

  • anchored 2k

  • kept the door open

👉 That’s a good equilibrium.
If you now follow up again, it risks feeling like:

  • you’re circling back to the ask, or

  • trying to justify / persuade too hard

What to do instead

✅ Wait for her next signal

  • If she engages → then you add that “transition phase” logic

  • If she goes quiet → you can reintroduce it later (naturally)

When it does make sense to say it

Use it if she says something like:

  • “maybe later”

  • “I really don’t have much now”

  • “I’d need to think”

👉 That’s your opening

How to say it (when the moment is right)

Message

Oui, et justement — on est dans une phase un peu transitoire où des montants plus modestes peuvent faire sens, le temps que je structure quelque chose de plus ambitieux derrière.

Donc ça permet de participer sans devoir s’engager fortement dès maintenant.

Why this timing matters

Right now:

  • She’s not resisting

  • She’s not convinced yet either

👉 You’re in “don’t overplay your hand” territory

Strategic takeaway

You’ve done the hard part:

  • introduced the idea

  • made it accessible

  • kept it low-pressure

Now:
👉 let her come 30% of the way toward you

That’s what turns this from a “pitch” into a pull dynamic.

If you want, I can map out the 3 likely ways she’ll respond next—and exactly how to reply to each.
