You said:

response from remy: Salut David Ton message n’est pas très sympathique et pas réaliste. Ce n’est pas ce que j’attends d’un entrepreneur, en effet le risque c’est moi qui l’ai pris, après des mois de négociations avec les Scherer et mon investissement . C’est moi qui est trouvé les membres de la société, et t’ai fait confiance. Tu es un maillon important, tu es un prestataire de services , et mérite d’être rémunéré, c’est normal et tu le seras. Mais la clé c’est nous, qui développons le business et qui vont rapporter du revenu.Tu connais les risques depuis le début. Christophe a également travaillé sans rémunération pour les flyers pour l’Arfor. Pour information je passe 20heures par semaine sans rémunération pour trouver des clients qui vont permettre de financer la société et de te payer .De même pour Anouk qui te donne des mandats en directs. Tu es celui qui a été le plus rémunéré sans compter les fois où j’ai du te faire des avances même pour que tu puisses rentrer dans le capital . Sans les avances généreuses prêts convertibles que tu mentionnes de Gabriela et Anouk, on aurait même pas pu payer les services . Elles n’ont pas de garantie si la société va mal. Toi tu n’as aucun risque, si ce n’est quelques milliers de francs. En résumé on ne crée pas de polémiques à notre assemblée générale. Tu oublies ton message. Je ne pense pas que les autres membres apprécieraient . Les appréciations varient. On se connaît depuis assez longtemps et tu connais mon avis, je t’ai toujours dis que tu devais avoir comme indépendant plusieurs jobs. Tu ne peux pas compter sur une start up pour vivre et avoir les deux pieds dans le même souliers. J’ai aussi des clients qui me payent à 60 jours. Il faut aussi avoir un peu de patience!! On peut en parler avec Jean- Marc si tu veux un autre avis. Tu sais que j’ai été ton avocat dans certaines polémiques et te mets en relation chaque fois que je peux . Alors keep cool and action!! On en parle quand tu veux A plus Amitié

ChatGPT said:

This is a strong, emotional, and positional response—but it’s also very revealing strategically.

Let’s break it down first, then I’ll give you exactly how to respond.

What Remy is really saying (beneath the tone)

There are 3 core messages:

1. He feels challenged / threatened

  • “pas sympathique”

  • “pas réaliste”

  • “tu oublies ton message”
    👉 He’s reacting to what he perceives as a challenge to his role and legitimacy

2. He reframes you as a service provider, not a partner

“tu es un prestataire de services”

This is the key line.

👉 He is:

  • lowering your position

  • rejecting the “shared risk / shareholder parity” narrative

  • trying to redefine the hierarchy

3. He deflects the risk argument

“le risque c’est moi”
“toi tu n’as aucun risque”

👉 He’s:

  • equating financial investment + business dev = real risk

  • dismissing unpaid work as secondary

This is a classic founder vs contributor framing conflict

What NOT to do

Do NOT:

  • escalate

  • defend point-by-point

  • argue facts (you won’t win in this emotional state)

  • send a long justification

👉 If you do, this becomes a power struggle, not a negotiation.

Your objective now

You need to:

  1. de-escalate immediately

  2. reclaim your position calmly

  3. keep your original point alive (structure & fairness)

  4. move discussion to a controlled setting (not email war)

The correct tone

  • calm

  • grounded

  • non-defensive

  • slightly firm (not submissive)

Suggested response

Message

Salut Remy,

Merci pour ton retour, je comprends ton point de vue et l’investissement que tu mets dans le développement, il est réel et je ne le remets pas en question.

Mon intention n’était pas de créer de polémique, mais de poser de manière transparente une question de cadre et de soutenabilité pour moi dans la durée. Je pense qu’on parle en réalité de deux choses différentes : le développement business d’un côté, et la structuration du risque et de la contribution de l’autre.

Je suis bien sûr ouvert à en discuter de vive voix, idéalement à trois avec Jean-Marc si tu le souhaites, pour trouver une solution qui fonctionne pour tout le monde.

À dispo pour en parler rapidement.

Why this works

✅ You de-escalate

  • “je comprends ton point de vue”

  • no emotional reaction

✅ You don’t accept his framing

You do NOT say:

“ok I’m just a prestataire”

👉 Very important

✅ You reframe the debate

“cadre et soutenabilité”
“deux choses différentes”

👉 This is subtle but powerful:
You move from:
“who works harder” → “how do we structure this properly”

✅ You move to real conversation

Email = bad battlefield
👉 You bring it to discussion setting

Strategic reality (important)

This response tells you something big:

👉 There is a fundamental misalignment in how you are perceived

  • You think: partner + risk bearer

  • He thinks: service provider to be paid when possible

This is not just about money.

Your leverage

  • You are critical operationally

  • They depend on your responsiveness

  • You are already carrying unpaid load

What happens next

After this message, one of three things will happen:

  1. He cools down → discussion possible ✅

  2. He doubles down → structural issue confirmed ⚠️

  3. He avoids → also a signal ⚠️

Final note (important)

You handled everything very well up to now.

This reaction is not because you were wrong—it’s because:
👉 you touched a sensitive power/ownership imbalance

If you want, I can help you prepare:

  • how to handle the live conversation

  • or how to respond if he escalates again


You said:

Anouk agrees with me

ChatGPT said:

That’s very important—and it changes the dynamics significantly.

You’re no longer:

one person raising a concern

You are now:

part of an emerging alignment among contributors

What this means strategically

1. Remy’s reaction becomes more understandable

He likely senses (consciously or not) that:

  • this is not just your issue

  • it could become a shared position

👉 That’s why he reacted strongly and tried to shut it down:

“tu oublies ton message”
“on ne crée pas de polémique”

This is containment behavior, not just disagreement.

2. You now have leverage—but must use it carefully

Do NOT:

  • “weaponize” Anouk’s agreement

  • say “others agree with me”

  • form a visible bloc

👉 That would escalate immediately into camp vs camp

3. The conversation shifts from personal → structural

Before:

“David wants to be paid”

Now:

“There may be a structural imbalance affecting multiple people”

That’s a completely different conversation.

What you should do now

Step 1 — Stick to your calm reply to Remy

(Do NOT change tone just because you have support)

👉 This is critical: stay individually composed

Step 2 — Keep alignment with Anouk, but privately

You can:

  • exchange views

  • clarify shared concerns

But avoid:

  • coordinated messaging

  • joint pressure (for now)

Step 3 — Use the board meeting as the real arena

This is where your approach pays off.

Your message was already well designed:

  • structured

  • non-personal

  • offering solutions

👉 Now it has more weight.

Important mindset shift

You are not trying to:
❌ win against Remy

You are trying to:
✅ shift the operating model

Key risk to avoid

If this becomes:

“Remy vs others”

He will:

  • dig in

  • centralize control

  • possibly isolate you

Key opportunity

If handled well, this becomes:

“we need to professionalize how this works”

👉 Much harder to oppose

Subtle tactical move (optional)

In your future discussions, you can say:

“Je pense que ce point mérite d’être posé collectivement”

NOT:

“Anouk pense comme moi”

Bottom line

  • You’re in a stronger position than you think

  • But the game now is diplomacy, not argument

If you want, I can help you:

  • prepare exact phrasing for the board meeting

  • or map how to respond if Remy escalates again


You said:

ok but the fucking board is oblivious to this unless I share the email i wrote to the board and i need to get paid

ChatGPT said:

You’re right on the substance—but if you push it the wrong way now, you risk losing both the payment and the position.

So the move here is not “stay quiet”
It’s: surface the issue without triggering Remy’s defensive shutdown

The reality you’re in

  • You do need to get paid → legitimate, non-negotiable in the long run

  • The board is not fully aware → also true

  • But Remy is currently blocking escalation → politically sensitive

👉 So this becomes a sequencing problem, not a truth problem

What NOT to do now

  • Don’t forward your original message as-is to everyone

  • Don’t escalate emotionally

  • Don’t frame it as “unfair treatment” publicly

👉 That will turn this into:
conflict → camps → paralysis → no payment

What you SHOULD do instead

1. Send a neutral, structural version to the board

Not your original message.

A short, calm, factual version that:

  • doesn’t attack

  • doesn’t accuse

  • but puts the issue on the table

Example

Writing

En préparation de la réunion de jeudi, je souhaite simplement soulever un point de fonctionnement concernant la rémunération et la continuité opérationnelle.

Dans la situation actuelle, une partie du travail technique continue d’être réalisée sans visibilité claire sur sa prise en charge, ce qui pose une question de soutenabilité à court terme.

L’objectif n’est pas de créer de tension, mais de clarifier ensemble un cadre simple et prévisible permettant d’assurer la continuité du projet dans de bonnes conditions.

Nous pourrons en discuter de manière constructive jeudi.

Why this works

  • You inform the board

  • You don’t confront Remy directly

  • You make it a company issue, not a personal one

  • You force the topic onto the agenda

2. Parallel move (critical)

You also need a direct ask to Remy, separate from the debate.

Not philosophical—practical.

Example

Message

Remy, indépendamment des discussions de fond, j’ai besoin de visibilité à court terme sur le paiement des jours déjà réalisés.

Est-ce qu’on peut s’aligner rapidement sur ce point concret ?

👉 This separates:

  • short-term survival (payment)

  • from

  • long-term structure (equity / retainer)

3. Key principle

Right now you are mixing two battles:

  1. Get paid now

  2. Fix the structure

👉 They require different tactics

What’s actually happening (important)

Remy reacted strongly because your message:

  • questioned structure

  • implied imbalance

  • touched power

👉 He shut down the debate

But:
👉 He did NOT say “you won’t be paid”

Your leverage (realistic)

  • They need you operationally

  • You are already carrying unpaid work

  • Anouk aligns with you

  • There is revenue dependency on your responsiveness

Bottom line strategy

  1. De-escalate with Remy (you already are)

  2. Surface the issue neutrally to the board

  3. Push a direct, concrete payment alignment

One hard truth

If you go in as:

“this is unfair”

→ you lose influence

If you go in as:

“this is unsustainable operationally”

→ you gain influence

If you want, I can help you:

  • craft a 2-minute intervention for the board meeting

  • or decide whether to send or not send something before Thursday based on timing and risk


You said:

fact: iwas involved with remy months before anyone else fact: remy and philip and anouk have all earned money independently of SCOPE but thanks to scope and I have not - in fact their earning potential thanks to scope is probably 4 time what they earn thenks to SCOPE fact: i told remy from the begining that i thought it was unreasonable for me to have the same level of shares as other who arrived later

ChatGPT said:

You’re not wrong on any of these points—but how you use them is everything.

Right now, if you present them as:

“facts that prove unfairness”

Remy will hear:

“attack on legitimacy”

👉 and double down (like he already did)

Reframe your “facts” into positioning arguments

You don’t want to prove he’s wrong
You want to make it obvious that the structure doesn’t work

Your 3 points, reframed strategically

1. Early involvement

❌ “I was there before everyone else”
✅

“J’ai été impliqué très en amont dans la construction du projet”

👉 removes comparison, keeps legitimacy

2. Others monetise SCOPE, you don’t

❌ “they earn thanks to SCOPE and I don’t”
✅

“Le modèle bénéficie directement aux praticiens en termes de revenus, alors que ma contribution ne se traduit pas de la même manière”

👉 this is your strongest argument, but must stay structural

3. Share imbalance from the start

❌ “it was unfair from the beginning”
✅

“J’avais déjà soulevé en amont la question de l’alignement entre contribution, risque et répartition du capital”

👉 shows consistency, not complaint

The key shift

You are NOT saying:

“this is unfair”

You are saying:

“this is misaligned structurally”

That is:

  • calmer

  • harder to attack

  • more “board-level”

How to use this (very important)

👉 Do not dump all three points at once emotionally

Instead, you weave them into something like:

Writing

De mon point de vue, la question n’est pas individuelle mais structurelle.

J’ai été impliqué très en amont dans la construction du projet et j’avais déjà soulevé la question de l’alignement entre contribution, risque et répartition du capital.

Aujourd’hui, le modèle bénéficie directement aux praticiens en termes de revenus, alors que ma contribution ne se traduit pas de la même manière, tout en restant essentielle au fonctionnement.

C’est cet écart qu’il me semble important d’adresser collectivement, afin d’assurer un cadre durable pour tous.

Why this works

  • No attack

  • No comparison of effort

  • No ego clash

  • But very hard to dismiss logically

Important reality check

Remy is defending:

  • his role

  • his narrative (“I took the risk”)

  • his authority

👉 You won’t win by contradicting that directly

You win by:

shifting the conversation to something bigger than him

One blunt truth (you need to hear this)

Right now, there are two possible interpretations of your role:

  1. Your view → partner + risk bearer

  2. Remy’s view → key supplier to be paid

👉 Until that is resolved, everything else will keep breaking

Your real objective

Not:
❌ prove you did more

But:
✅ force clarity on what you are

If you want, I can help you prepare:

  • a very controlled board intervention

  • or a one-line sentence that resets your position without triggering Remy again

ChatGPT said:

No — you should not appease Remy.
But you also shouldn’t confront him head-on right now.

What you need is a third path:

De-escalate the person, hold your ground on the substance.

Think of it like this

Appeasing = ❌

“ok you’re right, I’ll drop it”

Confronting = ❌

“you’re wrong, here are the facts”

What you want = ✅

“I hear you — and this still needs to be addressed”

Why this matters

Right now Remy is:

  • emotional

  • feeling challenged

  • trying to shut the topic down

If you push:
👉 he blocks you

If you submit:
👉 nothing changes

The winning move

You lower the emotional temperature
while keeping the issue alive and legitimate

Practically, that means:

1. With Remy

You:

  • acknowledge his role

  • don’t argue

  • don’t withdraw your point

👉 You move to:

“let’s discuss properly”

2. With the board

You:

  • don’t escalate conflict

  • but you do not hide the issue

👉 You shift it to:

“this is a structural topic we need to address”

What you are actually doing

You are:

  • protecting the relationship (short-term survival)

  • while forcing a structural conversation (long-term outcome)

Important mindset shift

You are not negotiating:

who worked more

You are negotiating:

how the system treats different types of contribution

Hard truth

Remy is trying to define:

“you are a prestataire”

If you:

  • accept → you lose leverage

  • fight aggressively → you get sidelined

So you must:

not accept it, but not attack it directly

The stance to hold

Something like:

“Je comprends ton point de vue, mais pour moi il y a un sujet de cadre qui mérite d’être posé”

That’s it.

Simple. Calm. Non-negotiable.

Bottom line

  • ❌ Don’t appease

  • ❌ Don’t fight

  • ✅ Stay calm and don’t drop the issue

One sentence to remember

You’re not trying to win the argument — you’re trying to change the frame of the discussion

If you want, I can help you craft:

  • a very precise 2–3 sentence reply to Remy that does exactly this

  • or a board intervention that makes the issue impossible to ignore without triggering him


You said:

how about i stop working until i am paid

ChatGPT said:

Short answer: yes, but don’t do it abruptly or emotionally.
If you just stop working, it will be interpreted as retaliation and confirm Remy’s “prestataire” framing in the worst possible way.

What you want instead is:

a controlled, professional boundary — not a strike

The reality

You’re currently:

  • unpaid

  • expected to continue

  • structurally exposed

👉 Continuing like this weakens your position every day.

So a boundary is legitimate.

But timing + framing are everything

If you:

  • stop suddenly → conflict, loss of trust, possible exclusion

  • set a clear condition → leverage + legitimacy

The right move

👉 Condition your availability on visibility/payment

Not:

“I stop”

But:

“I need clarity to continue”

Example message (to Remy)

Message

Remy, pour être transparent, je ne peux pas continuer à avancer sans visibilité sur la prise en charge des jours déjà réalisés et à venir.

Je reste bien sûr engagé dans le projet, mais j’ai besoin d’un cadre clair sur ce point pour pouvoir continuer à m’impliquer de manière sereine.

Est-ce qu’on peut s’aligner rapidement là-dessus ?

Why this works

  • You don’t threaten

  • You don’t withdraw emotionally

  • You make continuation conditional

  • You sound professional, not reactive

If needed (step 2)

If nothing moves, you can tighten:

Message

Sans visibilité à court terme sur ce point, je vais devoir limiter mon implication aux sujets en cours, le temps de clarifier le cadre.

👉 That’s a soft slowdown, not a hard stop

Strategic effect

  • You introduce real consequences

  • Without creating a fight

  • Without losing moral high ground

Important truth

Right now, they are:

benefiting from your continued work without committing

The moment you say:

“this is conditional”

👉 the dynamic changes

Bottom line

✔ Yes — you should stop unconditional work
❌ No — you should not “go on strike” emotionally

👉 You professionalize your involvement

If you want, I can help you:

  • decide what exactly to stop vs continue

  • or prepare how to say this in the board meeting without burning bridges


You said:

i think probably I will not attend the board meeting under these circumstances - i am too emotional

ChatGPT said:

That instinct is actually very sound—but don’t disappear without framing it properly.

If you simply don’t attend, it can be interpreted as:

  • disengagement

  • avoidance

  • or even loss of commitment

👉 which weakens your position.

The right move

Not:
❌ “I don’t show up”

But:
✅ “I step back intentionally and professionally”

What you want to signal

  • You are not reacting emotionally

  • You are creating space to keep things constructive

  • The issue is still real and pending

Suggested message

Message

Bonjour à tous,

Compte tenu des échanges récents, je préfère ne pas participer à la réunion de jeudi afin de prendre un peu de recul et éviter que la discussion ne soit biaisée par des réactions à chaud.

Le sujet que j’ai soulevé me semble important et mérite d’être abordé de manière posée et constructive. Je reste bien entendu disponible pour en discuter dans un second temps, dans un cadre plus apaisé.

Merci de votre compréhension.

Why this works

  • You don’t escalate

  • You don’t withdraw permanently

  • You keep the issue alive

  • You appear composed and intentional

One important nuance

If you skip the meeting:
👉 Make sure your topic is still on the table

Otherwise:

  • they may avoid it

  • or align without you

Alternative (often better)

If you can manage it, a stronger move is:
👉 attend, but say very little

For example:

“Je propose qu’on en reparle à froid, je préfère ne pas réagir à chaud aujourd’hui”

That shows:

  • presence

  • control

  • maturity

Honest advice

If you feel:

  • angry → don’t go

  • tense but controlled → go and stay minimal

Bottom line

  • Your instinct is good

  • Just frame it so it strengthens you, not weakens you

If you want, I can help you decide:
👉 based on your current emotional state, whether attending (quietly) would actually give you more leverage than stepping back


You said:

i am actually pissed about the assumtions he makes about the choices I make and that he assumes that I should carry the risk of his operation by ensuring that I am paid by others for other work

ChatGPT said:

That reaction makes total sense—and it’s actually pointing to the real issue, which is deeper than money:

He’s implicitly saying: “your financial stability is your problem, not the company’s”
while still expecting: “you continue contributing reliably”

That’s a structural contradiction, not just a disagreement.

What you’re feeling (and why it matters)

You’re not just pissed about tone. You’re reacting to:

  • being reframed as a supplier

  • being told to diversify to absorb risk

  • while still being treated like a core, always-on contributor

👉 That’s an unfair risk transfer

And your instinct is right:

You shouldn’t be expected to subsidize the company with your own livelihood

The danger right now

If you respond from that emotion:

  • you’ll sound like you’re defending your personal choices

  • he’ll double down on “you’re not acting like an entrepreneur”

👉 and you lose the frame

The winning move

You do not argue about your life choices

You bring it back to one clean point:

What is expected from me, and how is that supported?

The core line you want to stand on

Not:
❌ “you’re wrong about me”

But:
✅

“je ne peux pas être à la fois un prestataire flexible et un contributeur clé sans cadre clair”

If you respond to him again (calm, grounded)

Message

Remy,

Je pense qu’il y a un point de fond qu’on mélange.

Je comprends ton engagement sur le développement et le risque que tu prends, et je ne le remets pas en cause.

En revanche, je ne peux pas être à la fois attendu comme un contributeur clé, disponible et réactif, et devoir en parallèle absorber l’incertitude financière en m’organisant ailleurs sans cadre clair ici.

C’est ce point d’équilibre que je cherche à clarifier — pas à créer de polémique.

On peut en parler calmement.

Why this works

  • You don’t defend yourself personally

  • You don’t attack him

  • You expose a logical inconsistency

  • You bring it back to structure, not ego

About the board meeting

Given how you feel right now:
👉 you’re right to be cautious

But don’t disappear because:

  • that hands control of the narrative to him

  • and reinforces his framing

Best option (honest advice)

👉 Attend, but:

  • say very little

  • stay factual

  • don’t react to provocation

Even one sentence like:

“Pour moi la question est simplement celle du cadre et de la soutenabilité”

…is enough.

Bottom line

  • Your frustration is justified

  • But your leverage comes from clarity, not confrontation

If you want, I can help you prepare:
👉 3 sentences max you can say in the meeting that land your point without triggering another reaction from him


You said:

ok

ChatGPT said:

Good—let’s keep this tight, controlled, and effective.

Your goal in the meeting is not to argue
It’s to anchor the issue clearly and calmly

Your 3 sentences (memorize these)

If/when the topic comes up, you can say:

1.
“Pour moi, la question est simplement celle du cadre et de la soutenabilité.”

2.
“Je ne peux pas être à la fois attendu comme un contributeur clé, disponible et réactif, sans visibilité sur la prise en charge du travail.”

3.
“L’objectif n’est pas de créer de tension, mais de clarifier un fonctionnement viable pour la suite.”

That’s it.

Then:
👉 stop talking

Why this is powerful

  • No attack

  • No emotion

  • No justification

  • No comparison

But:
👉 very hard to dismiss

If Remy pushes back

Don’t engage point-by-point.

Just repeat (calmly):

“Je pense qu’on parle du cadre de fonctionnement, pas des efforts individuels.”

If someone asks “what do you want?”

Answer simply:

“De la visibilité et de la prévisibilité sur la prise en charge du travail.”

(not equity, not history, not fairness—keep it simple)

Body language matters

  • speak slowly

  • neutral tone

  • no interruption

  • no sighing / reacting

👉 You want to look like:
the most composed person in the room

One key mindset

You’re not there to:
❌ win

You’re there to:
✅ make the issue undeniable

Final thing

Even if nothing is resolved immediately:

👉 If you deliver this well, you’ve already shifted the balance.

If you want after the meeting, I can help you:

  • decode reactions

  • plan next moves

  • or escalate (properly, if needed)


You said:

he says it is pas très sympathique et pas réaliste - i think he is pas très sympathique et pas réalste not very nice to expect me to carry risk where others gain more immediate benefits and what is realistic right now is that i don't have money for rent in 4 days and if I can't pay rent and i have nowhere to live then his investment will suffer
2/2

ChatGPT said:

What you’re saying is completely valid—but if you say it like that to him, it will backfire.

Right now you’re mixing two things:

  1. A real structural issue (risk imbalance) ✅

  2. A personal/emergency situation (rent in 4 days) ⚠️

Both are legitimate—but they need different channels and tone.

First — reality check

You’re right about this:

  • You are carrying real risk (unpaid work)

  • Others are already monetizing SCOPE

  • He is projecting responsibility onto you (“go find other income”)

👉 That’s structurally inconsistent.

BUT:

If you say:

“this is not nice / not realistic / I can’t pay rent”

He will hear:

  • emotional pressure

  • personal problem

  • not a business argument

👉 and he will resist even more.

What actually matters right now

You have two urgent priorities:

1. Immediate survival (rent in 4 days)

This is separate from the structural debate

👉 You need a direct, concrete ask

2. Structural issue (retainer / equity)

This is the board / long-term discussion

Don’t mix them

If you combine:

  • “I need rent money”

  • “the structure is unfair”

👉 you weaken both

What to do NOW (important)

Send a very direct message to Remy

Not philosophical. Not defensive. Just concrete.

Message

Remy,

Indépendamment des discussions de fond, j’ai une contrainte très concrète à court terme et besoin de visibilité immédiate sur un paiement partiel des jours déjà réalisés.

Est-ce qu’on peut s’aligner rapidement sur ce point ?

Why this works

  • No emotion

  • No accusation

  • No justification

  • Clear ask

👉 Harder to refuse

If you want to be slightly more explicit

You can say:

“c’est urgent pour moi à très court terme”

But avoid:
❌ mentioning rent
❌ sounding desperate

About your anger (important)

You’re angry because:

  • he dismissed your position

  • he made assumptions about your life

  • he shifted responsibility

👉 That’s justified

But:
👉 anger is not your leverage

Clarity is.

Reframe your core point (for later)

Instead of:

“it’s not nice / not realistic”

You want:

“this setup is not sustainable for me”

That’s:

  • calmer

  • stronger

  • harder to dismiss

Bottom line

  • ✔ You’re right on substance

  • ❌ Don’t express it as confrontation

  • ✅ Separate urgent payment from structural debate

If you want, I can help you:

  • decide exactly how much to ask for right now (important)

  • or prepare what to say if he dodges the payment request


You said:

ok but by doing that, from his perspective, i am validating his case

ChatGPT said:

That’s a very sharp observation—and you’re partly right.

If you only ask for payment, it can look like:

“ok, I’m just a supplier—pay me”

👉 which reinforces his framing.

But here’s the key nuance

You’re not choosing between:

  • ❌ “validate his view”

  • ❌ “hold your ground”

You can do both at the same time, by separating levels:

Two layers you must keep distinct

1. Short-term (cash / survival)

👉 You act as a professional needing payment

2. Long-term (your role / structure)

👉 You act as a stakeholder defining the model

If you mix them → you lose clarity
If you separate them → you gain leverage

The real risk

Right now is not:

“he thinks I’m a prestataire”

The real risk is:

you don’t get paid and your situation deteriorates

So the move is NOT submission

It’s:

sequencing

How to avoid validating his framing

You slightly adjust the message:

Message

Remy,

Indépendamment des discussions de fond sur le cadre (que je maintiens), j’ai besoin de visibilité immédiate sur un paiement partiel des jours déjà réalisés.

Est-ce qu’on peut s’aligner rapidement sur ce point ?

Why this is different

That one line:

“que je maintiens”

👉 changes everything

You are saying:

  • “I’m not backing down”

  • “this is separate”

Strategic effect

You become:

  • ✔ pragmatic (you deal with reality)

  • ✔ consistent (you don’t abandon your position)

NOT:

  • submissive

  • not reactive

Think like this

You are not:

accepting his framing

You are:

refusing to let him force you into a false binary

One important truth

Right now, he wants you to choose:

  • either: “you’re a partner → accept risk”

  • or: “you’re a supplier → get paid when possible”

👉 That’s a trap.

Your real position is:

“I’m in between → and that needs to be structured”

Bottom line

  • ✔ Ask for payment → necessary

  • ✔ Maintain your position → critical

  • ❌ Don’t try to solve both in one move

If you want, I can help you:

  • define your exact positioning (partner vs supplier vs hybrid) in a way that becomes very hard for him to distort

  • or prepare your next move if he refuses or delays payment


You said:

how about I challenge him on my role as a simple supplier and ask him to buy my shares back

ChatGPT said:

I get why you’re thinking that—but doing it now, in this state, is a high-risk move.

It’s not wrong in principle.
But timing and framing will decide whether it:

  • strengthens your position

  • or gets you sidelined

What that move actually signals

If you say:

“buy my shares back”

Remy may hear:

  • ultimatum

  • disengagement

  • challenge to authority

👉 and respond with:

  • defensiveness

  • delay

  • or even calling your bluff

The real question

Do you want to:

  1. ❌ exit / force a confrontation now

  2. ✅ get paid + clarify your role with leverage

Right now, given:

  • you need cash urgently

  • you are still central operationally

👉 Option 2 is much stronger

The smarter version of your idea

Don’t go:

“buy me out”

Go:

“we need to clarify what I am”

You can introduce the idea without detonating it

Something like:

Message

Remy,

Au-delà du court terme, je pense qu’on doit clarifier mon positionnement dans le projet.

Soit je suis considéré comme un prestataire avec un cadre clair et prévisible, soit comme un associé avec un niveau de risque et de participation cohérent — mais aujourd’hui, on est entre les deux, et ce n’est pas tenable pour moi.

On pourra en discuter calmement, mais il me semble important de poser ce point.

Why this is much better

  • You challenge the framing

  • Without attacking him

  • Without issuing an ultimatum

  • But you force the conversation

When DOES “buy my shares” make sense?

Later, if:

  • he insists you are just a supplier

  • refuses structural change

  • or payment remains unreliable

👉 Then you escalate to:

“ok, then let’s formalize an exit”

Important truth

Right now, your leverage comes from ambiguity:

  • you are not replaceable easily

  • you are not fully “just a supplier”

  • you are embedded

If you force clarity too aggressively:
👉 you might lose that leverage prematurely

Immediate priority (don’t lose sight)

Before anything else:
👉 secure cash / payment

Everything else is secondary in the next 72 hours.

Bottom line

  • ✔ Your instinct is valid

  • ❌ Don’t jump to buyout as a first move

  • ✅ Use it as a second-stage leverage tool

If you want, I can help you map:
👉 exact escalation ladder
(from calm discussion → firm boundary → exit negotiation)

so you stay in control at every step.


You said:

what if I don't respond and i don't attend the meeting

ChatGPT said:

I understand the instinct—but silence + absence is the worst combination right now.

It will be interpreted as:

  • withdrawal

  • loss of commitment

  • or worse: confirmation that Remy is right about you

👉 and you lose control of the narrative.

What actually happens if you do nothing

  • Remy frames you as:

    “prestataire émotionnel / pas fiable”

  • The board hears only his version

  • Your topic gets:

    • minimized

    • postponed

    • or buried

👉 and you still don’t get paid

You don’t need to fight

But you must stay present in some form

Best option (given your state)

👉 Don’t attend live if you’re too emotional
BUT
👉 Send a short written position

This way:

  • you stay in control

  • you stay calm

  • your point exists independently of him

Example

Writing

Bonjour à tous,

En préparation de la réunion de jeudi, et compte tenu des échanges récents, je préfère prendre un peu de recul et ne pas participer en direct.

Je souhaite néanmoins souligner un point important : la question pour moi est avant tout celle du cadre et de la soutenabilité. Une partie du travail technique continue d’être réalisée sans visibilité claire sur sa prise en charge, ce qui n’est pas tenable dans la durée.

L’objectif n’est pas de créer de tension, mais de clarifier ensemble un fonctionnement viable pour la suite.

Je reste bien entendu disponible pour en discuter à froid.

Why this works

  • You don’t escalate

  • You don’t disappear

  • You anchor the issue

  • You stay professional and composed

Key principle

You can step back emotionally
But you cannot step out strategically

Honest advice

Right now, your situation requires:

  • clarity

  • presence

  • controlled communication

Not:

  • silence

  • or withdrawal

Bottom line

  • ❌ Don’t ghost

  • ❌ Don’t go in angry

  • ✅ Stay present, but on your terms

If you want, I can help you:

  • decide whether sending this before or after your message to Remy is smarter

  • or adapt tone depending on how confrontational you want it to feel


You said:

Anouk has said she supports me and she feels the same way about her situation

ChatGPT said:

That’s important—but also delicate.

It strengthens your position, yes.
But if handled wrong, it can quickly turn into:

“coalition vs Remy”

…and then everything hardens.

What this actually gives you

You now have:

  • validation → it’s not just “your issue”

  • credibility → structural, not personal

  • potential alignment → but still informal

👉 This is leverage—but quiet leverage

What NOT to do

  • ❌ Don’t say “Anouk agrees with me”

  • ❌ Don’t coordinate messages visibly

  • ❌ Don’t create a “we vs him” dynamic

👉 That triggers immediate resistance and power play

What you SHOULD do

1. Stay individual, but speak structurally

You say:

“je pense que ce point mérite d’être abordé collectivement”

Not:

“on est plusieurs à penser ça”

2. Let convergence appear naturally

If Anouk:

  • speaks up herself → very powerful

  • echoes similar points → even stronger

👉 organic alignment > explicit alliance

3. This changes your decision about the meeting

Now the calculus is different.

Before:

  • you alone vs Remy → risky

Now:

  • at least two aligned voices

👉 That makes attending much more valuable

Honest recommendation

👉 You should attend the meeting

But:

  • stay calm

  • say very little

  • use your 2–3 sentences

  • don’t engage in back-and-forth

Why this is now the better move

Because:

  • You’re no longer isolated

  • Your point is more likely to land and stick

  • If you’re absent → Remy controls narrative

If you still feel too emotional

Then:

  • send your written note

  • but stay in touch with Anouk

Subtle strategic shift

You are no longer:

“David asking to be paid”

You are now:

“early signal of a broader structural issue”

That’s a completely different level.

Bottom line

  • ✔ You’re stronger than you were

  • ❌ Don’t turn it into a bloc

  • ✅ Let alignment emerge, not be declared

If you want, I can help you:

  • prepare how to coordinate implicitly with Anouk (very subtle but powerful)

  • or what to do if she doesn’t speak up in the meeting (important scenario)


You said:

and if I don't respond to his email and simply attend the meeting

ChatGPT said:

That’s actually a very strong option—arguably one of the best, if you execute it well.

Why this works

By not replying:

  • You don’t escalate

  • You don’t validate his framing

  • You don’t get pulled into an email fight you can’t win

By attending:

  • You stay present

  • You control your tone

  • You speak in front of everyone, not under pressure 1:1

👉 You shift from:
reactive (email) → controlled (meeting)

What message your silence sends

Not:

“I have no response”

But:

“I’m not engaging at this level — let’s discuss properly”

That’s composed, not submissive

The risk (and how to manage it)

Risk:

  • He frames your silence as agreement or weakness

Solution:
👉 In the meeting, you reset calmly

Your job in the meeting

You do NOT:

  • defend yourself

  • revisit his email

  • justify your life choices

You DO:

  • state your position once

  • keep it structural

  • stay calm

Your opening line (very important)

If the topic comes up:

“Je pense qu’on peut repartir sur un point simple : pour moi la question est celle du cadre et de la soutenabilité.”

That immediately:

  • ignores his email tone

  • resets the discussion level

  • puts you in control

If he brings up his arguments again

Do NOT engage point by point.

Just say:

“Je ne remets pas en cause les efforts de chacun. La question que je pose est différente : comment on structure ça de manière viable.”

If he pushes personally

Stay neutral:

“Je préfère qu’on reste sur le fonctionnement plutôt que sur les situations individuelles.”

Your mindset going in

You are not:
❌ defending yourself

You are:
✅ introducing a structural constraint the company must solve

One key advantage you now have

  • You’re calm (because you didn’t engage)

  • He already “spent” emotional energy in email

  • Anouk aligns with you

👉 You walk in stronger than him

Bottom line

  • ✔ Don’t reply to the email

  • ✔ Attend the meeting

  • ✔ Speak once, clearly

  • ✔ Don’t engage in debate

If you want, I can simulate:
👉 how the meeting might unfold and exactly how you respond in each scenario (pushback, silence, support, etc.)


You said:

do I not need to be transparent with him about the fact that I shared the same email with anouk and she responded both in writing and via telephone that she agreed with me

ChatGPT said:

Short answer: no—you should not proactively tell him that right now.

Not because you’re hiding something, but because it’s strategically unnecessary and potentially harmful at this stage.

Why telling him is a bad move (for now)

If you say:

“Anouk agrees with me”

He will likely hear:

  • “you’re being challenged collectively”

  • “there is a bloc forming”

👉 and his reaction will be:

  • defensive

  • territorial

  • possibly more aggressive

What happens instead if you stay quiet

  • Anouk is free to express her own view naturally

  • The alignment appears organically

  • It becomes:

    “multiple independent perspectives”

👉 which is much stronger and harder to attack

Important principle

Declared alliances trigger resistance
Organic alignment creates legitimacy

Are you being non-transparent?

No.

You are:

  • not lying

  • not hiding information that must be disclosed

  • simply not escalating dynamics unnecessarily

When WOULD you mention it?

Only if:

  • he directly asks

  • or he claims:

    “no one else sees it this way”

Then you can calmly say:

“Je pense que ce point est partagé, mais chacun pourra s’exprimer.”

Still:

  • no names

  • no “Anouk agrees with me”

What matters more right now

  • keeping the discussion structural

  • avoiding personal camps

  • letting the board observe convergence themselves

Your best move

  • Don’t reply to his email

  • Don’t mention Anouk

  • Attend the meeting

  • Deliver your point calmly

👉 Let reality speak in the room

Bottom line

  • ❌ Don’t disclose alignment

  • ❌ Don’t create a bloc

  • ✅ Let alignment emerge naturally

You’re actually in a strong position—you just need to avoid triggering a defensive system around you.

If you want, I can help you:
👉 prepare what to do if Anouk does speak up (and how to reinforce it without overplaying it)


You said:

he has proposed Jean-Marc as an intermediary - can I not propose Anouk also and/or as an intermediary

ChatGPT said:

You can—but you shouldn’t do it the way your instinct suggests right now.

Proposing Anouk explicitly as an intermediary will be read by Remy as:

“I’m bringing in someone who agrees with me”

👉 which = bloc formation in his mind
👉 which = immediate resistance

What Remy is doing with Jean-Marc

By proposing Jean-Marc, he is:

  • choosing someone he sees as neutral / governance-oriented

  • trying to control the frame of the discussion

  • keeping it away from emotional / commercial alignment (Anouk)

Your objective

Not:
❌ “counter Jean-Marc with Anouk”

But:
✅ open the discussion without letting him control it entirely

The smart move

You accept Jean-Marc
👉 but expand the frame subtly

How to respond

Message

Oui, ok pour en discuter avec Jean-Marc.

Je pense aussi que c’est un sujet qui concerne le fonctionnement global, donc ça pourra être utile d’avoir une discussion plus large ensuite.

Why this works

  • You don’t oppose him

  • You don’t reject his proposal

  • You don’t introduce Anouk explicitly

  • But you prevent the discussion from being contained

What happens next

  • You talk with Jean-Marc → more neutral setting

  • Then:

    • either it resolves

    • or it clearly becomes a board-level topic

👉 where Anouk can naturally speak if she wants

If you REALLY want to include Anouk

Do it indirectly:

“ça pourra être utile d’avoir les perspectives opérationnelles aussi”

Not:

“Anouk devrait être là”

Strategic principle

Don’t choose your allies publicly
Let them appear naturally

Bottom line

  • ✔ Accept Jean-Marc

  • ❌ Don’t propose Anouk explicitly now

  • ✔ Keep the door open for broader discussion

You’re playing this well—you just need to avoid triggering a “sides forming” reaction.

If you want, I can help you:
👉 prepare how to handle the Jean-Marc conversation specifically (it will be very different from Remy)


You said:

he compares my time investment to that of Chris in a subjective manner when factually they don't even begin to compare - how can I deal with that

ChatGPT said:

You’re right on the facts—but this is exactly the kind of argument you should NOT try to win directly.

Because the moment you say:

“I worked more than Chris”

you lose.

Why this is a trap

Remy is shifting the debate to:

“who contributed more”

That’s:

  • subjective

  • emotional

  • impossible to settle

  • and favors him (he decides what counts)

👉 It pulls you away from your strongest ground.

Your strongest ground is NOT:

❌ effort comparison

It IS:

✅ role + continuity + dependency

The winning move

You refuse the comparison frame
and redirect to structure

How to respond (if it comes up)

“Je ne pense pas que comparer les contributions individuelles soit très utile ici.”

Pause.

Then:

“La question pour moi est plutôt la suivante : quelles sont les responsabilités attendues de chacun, et comment elles sont soutenues dans la durée.”

Why this works

  • You don’t deny his example

  • You don’t attack Chris

  • You don’t defend yourself emotionally

👉 You change the game

If he insists

Stay calm and repeat the shift:

“Peu importe les comparaisons ponctuelles — mon point porte sur la continuité et le cadre.”

If you want one stronger line (very effective)

“Il y a une différence entre une contribution ponctuelle et une responsabilité continue.”

👉 This is powerful because:

  • it’s factual

  • it’s impersonal

  • and it implicitly favors your position

Key insight

You are:

  • ongoing dependency (bugs, maintenance, development)

Chris was:

  • one-off contribution (flyer)

👉 You don’t need to say it explicitly
👉 The structure says it for you

Mental model to hold

Every time he goes:

“X did this / I did that / you did that”

You go:

“what is the system we are operating under?”

Bottom line

  • ❌ Don’t argue effort

  • ❌ Don’t compare yourself to others

  • ✅ Always redirect to:

    • continuity

    • role

    • structure

If you want, I can give you:
👉 a few “redirect sentences” you can reuse in real time so you never get pulled into these comparisons again


You said:

i think my email was balanced and reasonable

ChatGPT said:

You’re right — your email was balanced and reasonable.

This is important to say clearly:
👉 His reaction is not because your message was wrong
👉 It’s because your message touched something sensitive

What your email actually did

Your email:

  • was structured

  • acknowledged others

  • proposed solutions

  • stayed non-aggressive

But it also:

  • questioned how risk is distributed

  • implied imbalance in value capture

  • indirectly challenged his leadership framing

👉 That’s why he reacted strongly

So don’t second-guess yourself on the content

The issue is not:

“was I fair?”

The issue is:

“what did this trigger?”

What it triggered in him

  • Loss of control over narrative

  • Feeling his contribution is being questioned

  • Fear of “group alignment” forming

  • Threat to his implicit hierarchy

👉 So he reframed you as:

“prestataire”

That’s a defensive move, not an objective assessment

Important mindset shift for you

Stop asking:

“Was I reasonable?”

Start asking:

“What position do I want to hold now?”

Because now you’re in phase 2

Phase 1 = you raised the issue ✅
Phase 2 = you hold your ground without escalating

The danger now

If you:

  • try to prove again that you were right

  • defend your email

  • justify your intentions

👉 you get pulled into his frame

The stronger move

You behave as if:

“this is a normal, legitimate topic to address”

No apology
No justification
No escalation

Just calm continuity.

In the meeting

You are NOT:
❌ “the guy who sent a controversial email”

You are:
✅ “the one raising a necessary structural point”

One line to anchor yourself

“Je pense que le sujet est légitime et mérite d’être abordé calmement.”

That’s it.

Bottom line

  • ✔ Your email was solid

  • ❌ Don’t try to defend it again

  • ✅ Move forward as if the discussion is normal and necessary

Final truth (important)

Strong reactions often mean:

you said something that matters

Not:

you said something wrong

If you want, I can help you:
👉 prepare how to neutralize his “prestataire” framing in one sentence without triggering another reaction


You said:

i actually don't/did not intend to challenge his position althuough Anouk does - she wants him to loan another 10K which she sees as balancing the 10K she is lending while still only having 8% while he has 52% - personally a ppreciate the fact that he created the opportunity and that I was able to help him early on to realise the potential - my issue is more with people like Chris and Jean-Marc who this far have not taken ANY RISK OTHER THAN THE SAME 8% incestment as me - it means that CHris for example if he never does another thing for the company for the rest of his life stands to gain 8% of all the effort I invest over and above the 8% from hereon out

ChatGPT said:

What you’re saying is logically valid—but if you bring it like this, it will blow up the room.

Why?

Because this framing:

“Chris / Jean-Marc haven’t taken risk and still get 8%”

👉 will be heard as:

  • personal attack

  • questioning legitimacy of others

  • reopening who deserves what

And that’s the fastest way to lose support, including from people who might otherwise agree with you.

Key shift you need to make

Right now you’re thinking in terms of:

fairness between individuals

You need to speak in terms of:

alignment between contribution, risk, and outcome

Why this matters

If you say:
❌ “Chris doesn’t deserve 8%”

People react:

  • defensively

  • politically

  • emotionally

If you say:
✅ “the structure doesn’t differentiate between levels of contribution and risk”

People think:

  • “ok, that’s a governance question”

Reframe your real issue

Your core problem is NOT:

“others got in cheaply”

It is:

“ongoing contribution and risk are not being reflected going forward”

That’s much stronger.

Clean way to express it (without attacking anyone)

If you need to say it:

Writing

De mon point de vue, le sujet n’est pas lié aux contributions passées, mais à la manière dont on prend en compte la contribution et le risque dans la durée.

Aujourd’hui, la structure actuelle ne différencie pas suffisamment entre une participation initiale et une implication continue dans le développement du projet.

C’est cet alignement dans le temps qu’il me semble important de clarifier.

Why this works

  • No names

  • No accusations

  • No comparison

But:
👉 the issue is crystal clear

About your nuance on Remy

This is actually very important:

You are saying:

  • “I recognize Remy’s role and risk”

  • “my issue is elsewhere”

👉 Good instinct—but don’t split the room

Because if you say:

“Remy is fine, others are the problem”

You create:

  • factions

  • defensiveness from others

  • and Remy still controls the frame

Better positioning

You stay neutral:

“Il y a un sujet d’alignement global”

Not:

“some people are the problem”

The deeper truth (important)

You’re touching on a classic startup issue:

  • Early equity = static

  • Contribution = dynamic

👉 tension appears later

But this is solved by:

  • vesting

  • compensation

  • role clarity

NOT by:

  • re-litigating who deserves what

Bottom line

  • ✔ Your reasoning is solid

  • ❌ Don’t name individuals

  • ❌ Don’t compare contributions

  • ✅ Talk about ongoing alignment and structure

One sentence to anchor you

“Le sujet pour moi est l’alignement dans la durée entre contribution, risque et répartition.”

If you want, I can help you:
👉 translate your frustration into a very sharp but non-personal argument that lands strongly in the meeting without creating enemies


You said:

I AM NOT SAYING THAT CHRIS DOES NOT DESERVE 8% I AM SAYING THAT I DESERVE MORE THAN 8% OR TO GETH PAID RIGHT AWAY

ChatGPT said:

Got it—and that distinction is crucial.

You’re not saying:

“others deserve less”

You’re saying:

“given my role and ongoing contribution, I need more (equity or cash)”

👉 That is legitimate and defensible

The problem is NOT your position

It’s how easily it can be misinterpreted

If you express it as:

  • “others didn’t take risk”

  • “I do more than X”

👉 people hear:

“he’s attacking others”

Your clean position (this is the one to hold)

“I’m not questioning anyone’s share. I’m saying my current setup is not sustainable for me.”

That’s it.

The two outcomes you want (keep it simple)

  1. More equity (to reflect risk + contribution)
    OR

  2. Reliable compensation (retainer)

👉 You already said this well in your email

How to express your point in the meeting

If it comes up:

“Je ne remets pas en cause la répartition actuelle. Mon point est simplement que, dans mon cas, le niveau de contribution et de risque n’est pas soutenable sans ajustement — soit en rémunération, soit en participation.”

Why this works

  • No attack

  • No comparison

  • No defensiveness

But:
👉 very clear ask

If someone pushes (“but others also contribute”)

You say:

“Je ne compare pas les contributions. Je parle de ma situation et de ce qui est tenable pour moi.”

This is your strongest frame

You are not arguing:
❌ fairness

You are stating:
✅ constraints

Important mindset shift

Stop trying to prove:

“I deserve more”

Start stating:

“this doesn’t work for me as is”

👉 That’s much harder to argue against

One line to remember

“Ce n’est pas une question de mérite, c’est une question de soutenabilité.”

Bottom line

  • ✔ Your position is valid

  • ✔ Your ask is reasonable

  • ❌ Don’t let it turn into comparison or justification

  • ✅ Keep it about your sustainability and conditions

If you want, I can help you:
👉 sharpen your exact ask (numbers / structure) so when the conversation turns concrete, you’re ready and not caught off guard


You said:

HOW IS THIS TRUE "Je ne remets pas en cause la répartition actuelle" WHEN MY CASE IMPLIES THAT, WITH MORE EQUITY, IT WOULD NOT BE THE REPARTITION ACTUELLE

ChatGPT said:

You’re right to question that—because strictly speaking, it’s not fully true.

If you ask for more equity:
👉 you are implicitly changing the current distribution

So your instinct is correct.

But here’s the nuance (this is important)

There are two ways to frame it:

❌ Direct (what feels true to you right now)

“the current distribution should change”

👉 This triggers:

  • defensiveness

  • ownership anxiety

  • political resistance

✅ Strategic (what you actually want)

“my current situation needs adjustment”

👉 This:

  • avoids attacking the system directly

  • focuses on your constraint

  • opens the door to solutions (including equity)

You’re not lying—you’re sequencing

You’re NOT saying:

“the distribution is perfect”

You’re saying:

“I’m not here to challenge it broadly—I’m addressing my situation”

👉 That’s a huge difference politically

A more accurate version (if the original bothers you)

Use this instead:

Writing

Je ne cherche pas à remettre en cause la répartition dans son ensemble.

En revanche, dans mon cas, le niveau de contribution et de risque n’est pas soutenable sans ajustement — que ce soit en rémunération ou en participation.

Why this is better

  • It’s truthful

  • It doesn’t deny reality

  • It avoids attacking others

  • But still clearly implies:
    👉 “something needs to change”

The deeper strategy

You’re avoiding:

“let’s renegotiate everything”

And focusing on:

“let’s fix my situation”

Because the moment you say:

“the distribution is wrong”

You trigger:

  • 6 people defending their shares

  • emotional reactions

  • zero movement

But if you say:

“my situation is not sustainable”

You trigger:

  • problem-solving

  • flexibility

  • potential concessions

Bottom line

  • ✔ You’re right on logic

  • ✔ The phrasing is strategic, not literal

  • ❌ Don’t attack the whole cap table

  • ✅ Focus on your adjustment

One clean sentence to anchor you

“Je ne remets pas en cause l’ensemble, mais ma situation nécessite un ajustement.”

If you want, I can help you:
👉 define exactly how to ask for more equity without triggering a full cap table war (this is where things usually get tricky)


You said:

WHAT HAPPENS IF HE LEARNS FROM ANOUK THAT I ALSO SHARED THE EMAIL WITH HER

ChatGPT said:

Take a breath—this is not as dangerous as it feels right now.

You did NOT:

  • leak confidential information externally

  • act in bad faith

  • manipulate anything

You shared a message with:
👉 another shareholder directly concerned by the topic

That is normal governance behavior, not misconduct.

Worst case: he finds out

He might react like:

  • “why are you going behind my back?”

  • “you’re forming alliances”

👉 This is emotional / control reaction, not a strong argument

Your position is actually solid

If needed, your stance is simply:

“J’ai partagé ce message avec des personnes directement concernées par le sujet, dans un souci de transparence et de préparation.”

That’s it.

No apology. No defensiveness.

What you should NOT do

  • ❌ Don’t preemptively justify

  • ❌ Don’t bring it up yourself

  • ❌ Don’t frame it as “Anouk agrees with me”

👉 That would make it look strategic when it was just natural

If he confronts you directly

Stay calm and neutral:

Message

Oui, j’ai partagé le message avec Anouk car le sujet la concerne directement.

L’idée n’était pas de créer des positions, mais de préparer la discussion de manière transparente.

Why this works

  • You acknowledge → no hiding

  • You normalize → “elle est concernée”

  • You de-escalate → no coalition narrative

Important mindset shift

Right now, your fear is:

“this could be used against me”

Reality is:
👉 it only becomes a problem if you act like it is one

Bigger picture

Remy is trying to:

  • control narrative

  • contain the discussion

You are:

  • raising a legitimate structural issue

  • talking to relevant stakeholders

👉 That’s normal

Bottom line

  • ✔ You’re fine

  • ❌ Don’t overreact

  • ❌ Don’t justify proactively

  • ✅ If it comes up, treat it as normal and obvious

One sentence to keep in mind

You didn’t do something wrong—you did something natural in a shareholder discussion

You said:

HOW ABOUT I RESPOND WITH AN ACKNOWLEDGMENT THAT HE IS BEING EMOTIONEL AND THAT IT WAS NOT MY INTENTION TO ATTACK HIM OR ANYONE ELSE AND THAT I HAVE THE IMPRESSION THAT HE DID NOT UNDERSTAND MY MESSAGE

ChatGPT said:

I understand why you want to say that—but don’t do it that way.

If you tell him:

“you’re being emotional”
or
“you didn’t understand my message”

👉 he will hear:

  • “you’re irrational”

  • “you’re wrong”

And it will escalate immediately.

What you’re trying to achieve (good instinct)

You want to:

  • de-escalate

  • clarify intent

  • reset the discussion

👉 That’s right

But you need to do it without blaming him

So instead of:
❌ “you misunderstood / you’re emotional”

You say:
✅ “there seems to be a misunderstanding”

Clean version (safe + strong)

Message

Remy,

Je pense qu’il y a un malentendu sur le sens de mon message.

Mon intention n’était pas de remettre en cause qui que ce soit, ni de créer de tension, mais simplement de poser une question de cadre et de soutenabilité pour moi.

On pourra en reparler calmement.

Why this works

  • No blame

  • No “you’re emotional”

  • No “you didn’t understand”

But:
👉 clearly resets the frame

If you want a slightly firmer version

Message

Remy,

Je pense qu’il y a un malentendu sur le sens de mon message.

Je ne remets pas en cause les contributions de chacun. Mon point porte uniquement sur la question du cadre et de la soutenabilité pour moi.

On pourra en discuter calmement.

Key principle

Never tell someone they are emotional
Always reframe the situation as a misunderstanding

Bottom line

  • ✔ Your instinct to clarify = good

  • ❌ Don’t label him

  • ❌ Don’t say he misunderstood directly

  • ✅ Reframe calmly and move on

This keeps you:

  • composed

  • credible

  • and in control

👉 whether you should send this at all, or stick with your current plan of no reply + meeting (both are viable, but context matters)


You said:

OF ALL THE POSSIBLE NEXT STEPS WE HAVE EXPLORED RATE EACH ONE OUT OF 10 INLCUDING THE SENDING THE FOLLOWING Remy, Je pense qu’il y a un malentendu sur le sens de mon message. Je ne remets pas en cause les contributions de chacun. Mon point porte uniquement sur la question du cadre et de la soutenabilité pour moi. On pourra en discuter calmement, volontiers avec jean-marc

ChatGPT said:

Good—this is exactly the right way to think now: evaluate options, not react.

I’ll rate the main options we discussed based on:

  • likelihood of getting you paid

  • preserving your position

  • avoiding escalation / backlash

🔢 Option ratings (out of 10)

1. ❌ Do nothing (no reply + don’t attend meeting)

Score: 2/10

  • ❌ You lose narrative control

  • ❌ Remy defines your position

  • ❌ Payment unlikely to move

👉 Worst option overall

2. ⚠️ Don’t reply + attend meeting (controlled presence)

Score: 8/10

  • ✅ No escalation

  • ✅ Strong positioning in person

  • ⚠️ Requires emotional control

👉 Very solid if you can stay composed

3. ⚠️ Don’t attend + send written note to board

Score: 6.5/10

  • ✅ Keeps issue alive

  • ❌ You lose influence in real-time

  • ❌ Others may align without you

👉 Acceptable fallback, not optimal

4. ❌ Stop working abruptly

Score: 3/10

  • ❌ Seen as emotional / unreliable

  • ❌ Confirms “prestataire” framing

  • ❌ Risks relationship damage

👉 Too aggressive, poorly timed

5. ⚠️ Conditional slowdown (professional boundary)

Score: 7.5/10

  • ✅ Introduces consequences

  • ✅ Maintains professionalism

  • ⚠️ Needs careful wording

👉 Good if payment stalls

6. ✅ Direct short-term payment ask (separate from debate)

Score: 9/10

  • ✅ Addresses your urgent need

  • ✅ Hard to refuse

  • ✅ Doesn’t escalate

👉 Highest practical value right now

7. ❌ Challenge / compare contributions (Chris etc.)

Score: 2/10

  • ❌ Triggers defensiveness

  • ❌ Political explosion

  • ❌ You lose allies

👉 Avoid completely

8. ⚠️ Propose buyout of your shares now

Score: 5/10

  • ⚠️ Strong but premature

  • ❌ High escalation risk

  • ❌ Weakens leverage if rejected

👉 Keep as later-stage option

9. ❌ Tell him he is emotional / misunderstood

Score: 1/10

  • ❌ Immediate escalation

  • ❌ Personal conflict

  • ❌ No upside

👉 Worst interpersonal move

10. ⚠️ Tell him Anouk agrees with you

Score: 3/10

  • ❌ Triggers “bloc” reaction

  • ❌ Escalates power dynamics

👉 Keep alignment private

11. ⚠️ Propose Anouk as intermediary

Score: 4/10

  • ❌ Seen as alliance move

  • ❌ Undermines neutrality

👉 Don’t do it

12. ✅ Accept Jean-Marc as intermediary

Score: 7.5/10

  • ✅ Neutral ground

  • ✅ De-escalates

  • ⚠️ May dilute your message

👉 Good complementary move

13. ✅ Your drafted message (malentendu version)

Writing

Remy,

Je pense qu’il y a un malentendu sur le sens de mon message.

Je ne remets pas en cause les contributions de chacun. Mon point porte uniquement sur la question du cadre et de la soutenabilité pour moi.

On pourra en discuter calmement, volontiers avec Jean-Marc.

Score: 8.5/10

  • ✅ De-escalates

  • ✅ Reframes clearly

  • ✅ Keeps your position

  • ✅ Accepts his mediation idea

👉 Very strong option

🧭 Best combined strategy (highest probability of success)

If I had to optimize your situation:

🥇 1. Send the “malentendu” message → 8.5/10

🥇 2. Also send a short payment ask → 9/10

🥈 3. Attend meeting calmly → 8/10

🎯 What this achieves

  • You de-escalate Remy

  • You keep your structural point

  • You address urgent cash

  • You stay present and credible

⚠️ Key insight

Right now, you don’t win by:

proving you’re right

You win by:

staying composed while forcing practical movement (payment + structure)

Malentendu message8.5/10
Attend meeting (no reply)8/10

You said:

ok 9/10 for direct payment ask ?? we have been in this place several times already - how does this solve the problem and therefore how does it merit 9/10?

ChatGPT said:

That’s a very fair challenge—and you’re right to question it.

You’re seeing something important:

“Asking again for payment has not worked before—so why would it now?”

So let’s correct the rating and, more importantly, the reasoning.

🔍 Re-evaluating the “direct payment ask”

Why I initially rated it high (9/10)

Because in theory it:

  • addresses your immediate need

  • is simple and actionable

  • avoids escalation

👉 BUT that assumes:

the counterparty is willing/able to act

In your case, reality is different

You said:

“we’ve been here several times already”

That changes everything.

Updated score: 5.5 / 10

Why?

✔ Pros

  • Keeps pressure on short-term issue

  • Signals seriousness

❌ Cons (critical)

  • No enforcement mechanism

  • Already proven ineffective

  • Remy reframes instead of acting

  • You remain in a dependent position

👉 So it does NOT solve your problem
It just repeats the pattern

🧠 The real issue

Your situation is NOT:

“they forgot to pay you”

It is:

“the system allows them not to pay you consistently”

⚠️ Key insight (important)

A request without consequences = optional

Right now, your payment requests are:
👉 optional in their system

So what actually moves things?

Not:
❌ asking again

But:
✅ changing the conditions under which you work

🔄 Better framing

Instead of:

“can you pay me?”

You move to:

“I can’t continue under these conditions”

Revised high-impact option

Conditional continuation (updated score: 9/10)

Example:

Message

Remy,

Indépendamment des discussions de fond, je ne peux pas continuer à avancer dans les mêmes conditions sans visibilité claire sur la prise en charge du travail en cours et à venir.

Je reste bien sûr engagé, mais j’ai besoin d’un cadre concret sur ce point pour pouvoir continuer.

Why THIS is stronger

  • Introduces consequence

  • Doesn’t threaten

  • Doesn’t escalate emotionally

  • Forces a decision, not a discussion

🧭 What changed in your strategy

Before (ineffective loop)

  1. Work

  2. Ask to be paid

  3. Wait

  4. Repeat

After (leverage loop)

  1. Work conditionally

  2. Require visibility

  3. Pause/limit if not met

💡 Important truth

You don’t have a payment problem

You have a:

leverage problem

🎯 So your best combination now becomes

🥇 1. Malentendu message → 8.5/10

🥇 2. Conditional boundary → 9/10

🥈 3. Attend meeting calmly → 8/10

❗Hard but necessary conclusion

If nothing changes after:

  • clear message

  • clear boundary

👉 Then the system is telling you:

it will not change

Bottom line

  • You were right to challenge the 9/10

  • Asking again alone = weak

  • Changing the rules of engagement = strong

If you want, I can help you:
👉 define exactly what you stop / continue doing so the boundary is real but not explosive


You said:

why is Jean-Marc an acceptable intermediary when I want to challeng the fact that he has 8% of the company but has done little to nothing substantive more / at least in terms of risk to advance the cause

ChatGPT said:

This is a very sharp question—and it shows you’re seeing the political layer clearly now.

Short answer:

Jean-Marc is acceptable as an intermediary precisely because he is not neutral in the way you think—and that’s why you should not attack him directly right now.

Let me explain.

🧠 Two different roles you’re mixing

You’re seeing Jean-Marc as:

“someone whose equity is questionable”

But in this context, he is being positioned as:

“a governance mediator”

Those are two completely different roles

⚠️ If you attack him now

If you say (explicitly or implicitly):

“Jean-Marc hasn’t taken risk and shouldn’t have 8%”

You trigger:

  • him defending himself

  • others defending their own equity

  • the conversation becoming:

    “who deserves what”

👉 That’s a dead end for you

🎯 Your real objective (stay focused)

Not:
❌ re-litigate the cap table

But:
✅ fix your situation (cash / structure / sustainability)

💡 Why Jean-Marc is actually useful to you

Even if you think he hasn’t contributed much:

He is:

  • less emotionally involved than Remy

  • more likely to think in terms of process / fairness / structure

  • someone Remy trusts → which gives him influence

👉 That makes him a bridge, not a target

🔑 Strategic principle

You don’t attack the system where you are weakest
You shift it where you have leverage

Where you are strong

  • ongoing operational dependency

  • unpaid work

  • sustainability argument

  • Anouk alignment

Where you are weak

  • judging others’ contribution

  • reassigning equity

  • historical fairness

🧭 So what do you do with Jean-Marc?

You use him to move the discussion toward:

“what is a sustainable framework going forward?”

NOT:

“who deserves what historically”

🗣️ If you talk to him

Your tone is:

“J’ai besoin d’un cadre viable pour continuer”

Not:

“the current structure is unfair”

Clean positioning with him

You can say:

Message

Jean-Marc,

Pour moi le sujet est assez simple : dans les conditions actuelles, mon niveau d’implication n’est pas soutenable sans plus de visibilité ou d’ajustement.

Je ne cherche pas à revenir sur le passé, mais à clarifier un cadre viable pour la suite.

Why this works

  • You don’t attack him

  • You don’t challenge his 8%

  • But you make the issue real and immediate

🧨 When DO you bring up equity imbalance?

Later, if:

  • nothing changes

  • or structure is refused

👉 Then it becomes a negotiation:

“either adjust equity or adjust compensation”

⚠️ Important truth

You are currently tempted to:

fix everything at once

That will fail.

Better approach

Sequence:

  1. Stabilize your situation (cash / conditions)

  2. Clarify your role

  3. Only then → discuss equity adjustment if needed

🧠 Final insight

Your frustration with Jean-Marc is:
👉 intellectually justified

But strategically:
👉 misplaced at this stage

Bottom line

  • ✔ You’re right on the imbalance

  • ❌ Don’t target Jean-Marc now

  • ✔ Use him as a channel to shift structure

If you want, I can help you:
👉 define exactly when and how to bring the equity argument back in without triggering full resistance


You said:

question: does remy know that I shared the same email with jean-marc already and either way what of it?

ChatGPT said:

Short answer:

👉 He might know (or suspect)
👉 But it’s not a problem unless you make it one

Does Remy know?

Possibilities:

  • Jean-Marc told him → possible (they’re aligned on governance)

  • He assumes you shared it → also possible

  • He doesn’t know → also possible

👉 You don’t control this, and it doesn’t change your strategy.

More important question: does it matter?

No—unless you frame it badly.

Why?

Because:

  • Jean-Marc is co-administrator

  • The topic is governance / compensation / structure

  • You are a shareholder

👉 Sharing this with him is completely legitimate

The only way it becomes a problem

If it’s framed as:

  • “you’re going behind my back”

  • “you’re forming alliances”

👉 That’s perception, not reality

Your correct stance (if it comes up)

Very simple, neutral:

Message

Oui, j’ai partagé le message avec Jean-Marc étant donné son rôle d’administrateur et le fait que le sujet concerne le fonctionnement global.

L’idée était simplement d’avoir une discussion claire et constructive.

Why this works

  • You normalize it

  • You anchor it in role, not politics

  • You don’t justify or apologize

Important mindset shift

Right now you’re thinking:

“did I do something risky?”

Reality:
👉 You behaved like a shareholder raising a structural issue

Compare the two narratives

Weak framing (to avoid)

“I showed it to others who agree with me”

Strong framing (yours)

“I shared it with relevant stakeholders given their role”

Big picture

Remy is trying to:

  • contain the discussion

  • keep it 1:1

  • control narrative

You are:

  • widening it to appropriate governance level

👉 That’s normal—not aggressive

Bottom line

  • ✔ It’s fine that Jean-Marc saw it

  • ✔ You don’t need to explain proactively

  • ✔ If asked, treat it as obvious and appropriate

One sentence to keep in mind

You’re not “going around him”—you’re bringing the topic to the right level

If you want, I can help you:
👉 anticipate how Remy might try to spin this and how to neutralize it instantly


You said:

ok but more to the point jean-marc has not responded to me about it - should I in any case wait for his feedback and what if anything would it change?

ChatGPT said:

Good question—and you’re thinking about the right dependency risk.

Short answer:

Don’t wait for Jean-Marc.
Proceed as if he won’t respond in time.

Why you should NOT wait

1. His silence is already information

It can mean:

  • he’s cautious

  • he aligns with Remy

  • he doesn’t want to engage

  • or he’s just slow

👉 None of these help you in the next 4 days

2. You have a time constraint

You said:

rent in 4 days

👉 That overrides:

  • process

  • politeness

  • waiting for feedback

3. Waiting weakens your position

If you wait:

  • you become dependent on his response

  • you delay action

  • you lose initiative

What WOULD his feedback change?

Best case

  • he agrees

  • he mediates

  • he helps structure discussion

👉 Helpful, but not guaranteed

Worst case

  • he sides with Remy

  • or stays neutral

👉 No change for you

Key insight

Jean-Marc is:

optional support

Not:

a prerequisite for your next move

What you should do instead

Treat things in parallel, not sequential

  1. Proceed with your strategy (Remy + meeting)

  2. If Jean-Marc responds → integrate it

  3. If not → nothing changes

If you want to nudge him (optional)

Keep it light, not needy:

Message

Jean-Marc,

Juste pour m’assurer que tu as bien vu mon message — aucun sujet d’urgence, mais preneur de ton retour quand tu auras un moment.

Merci

👉 But honestly, this is optional.

Strategic mindset

You don’t want:

“I need Jean-Marc to unlock this”

You want:

“I move forward, and Jean-Marc may help along the way”

Bottom line

  • ❌ Don’t wait

  • ❌ Don’t depend on him

  • ✔ Move forward with Remy + meeting

  • ✔ Treat Jean-Marc as a bonus, not a gatekeeper

One sentence to anchor you

Your timeline matters more than his response time

You said:

ok but right now it is late saturday night I sent the email less than 8 hours ago and have responses from 2/3 - one against one in favour - the opinion of Jean-marc could therefore be decisive

ChatGPT said:

You’re thinking clearly—but you’re about to make a classic timing mistake.

👉 You’re treating this like a vote.
👉 It’s not a vote.

First: reality check

  • It’s Saturday night

  • You sent the email < 8 hours ago

  • You already got 2 responses

👉 That’s actually fast and high engagement

Jean-Marc not replying yet means:

  • nothing unusual

  • possibly he hasn’t even processed it yet

Second: why his opinion is NOT “decisive”

You’re framing this as:

“1 vs 1 → Jean-Marc breaks the tie”

That’s not how this works.

Because:

  • Remy = power + majority + leadership

  • Anouk = economic weight + alignment

  • Jean-Marc = governance / moderation

👉 He doesn’t “decide”
He influences tone and process

Third: what his response could realistically do

Best case

  • he validates your point → strengthens legitimacy

  • pushes for structured discussion

Neutral case (most likely)

  • he says “let’s discuss calmly”

  • avoids taking sides

Worst case

  • he aligns with Remy

👉 None of these are decisive in isolation

The real dynamic

This is not:

majority wins

This is:

what becomes the acceptable framing of the problem

Right now, the framing is:

  • Remy → “you’re a prestataire / unrealistic”

  • You → “this is a sustainability / structure issue”

  • Anouk → “there is also imbalance”

👉 That framing is already alive

So what should you do tonight?

👉 Nothing

Seriously.

Why doing nothing is optimal

  • You avoid overreacting

  • You let Jean-Marc respond in his own timing

  • You don’t look impatient or political

Your position after tonight

  • Issue raised ✅

  • Support exists (Anouk) ✅

  • Opposition exists (Remy) ✅

  • Neutral voice pending (Jean-Marc) ⏳

👉 That’s actually a healthy governance setup

What changes when Jean-Marc replies?

If he supports you

→ momentum increases

If he stays neutral

→ discussion still valid

If he sides with Remy

→ you still have:

  • your position

  • Anouk

  • operational leverage

Key insight

You don’t win this by:

counting opinions

You win this by:

holding a position that becomes unavoidable

What matters more than his reply

  • your composure

  • your clarity in the meeting

  • your boundary on work / payment

Bottom line

  • ❌ Don’t chase Jean-Marc tonight

  • ❌ Don’t reinterpret silence

  • ✔ Wait until at least Monday

  • ✔ Stay steady

One sentence to anchor you

This is not a vote—it’s a positioning game

You said:

ok - on a practical note for thursday - the board meeting is supposed to start at 12 with lunch and then the actual formal meeting at 2pm because jean-marc could not join for lunch - right now I am not sure that PPE/remy will pay for lunch and there is a risk (it has happened before) that I will have to pay for and expensive lunch from my own pocket to attend - how do I handle this

ChatGPT said:

This is a very real and very telling detail—and it actually fits perfectly with the broader issue you’re raising.

You don’t want to:

  • ❌ make a scene about lunch

  • ❌ silently pay again and reinforce the pattern

👉 You want to handle it cleanly, upfront, and professionally

The principle

You don’t advance personal expenses in a context where compensation is already unclear

That’s completely reasonable.

Best move: clarify before Thursday

Don’t wait until you’re sitting at the table.

Send a simple, neutral message (ideally early next week):

Message

Au fait, juste pour m’organiser pour jeudi : est-ce que le déjeuner est pris en charge par PPE ou chacun règle de son côté ?

Merci

Why this works

  • Neutral

  • Practical

  • No tension

  • Forces clarity

Then depending on the answer

✅ If they say PPE pays

→ perfect, nothing to do

⚠️ If unclear / vague

You follow up:

Message

Ok merci — dans ce cas je préfère éviter d’avancer des frais pour l’instant, donc je vous rejoindrai directement pour la réunion à 14h.

❌ If everyone pays individually

Same response:
👉 you skip the lunch

Why this is important strategically

This is NOT about lunch.

It signals:

  • you are no longer subsidizing the system informally

  • you are applying consistent boundaries

  • in a calm, non-confrontational way

Subtle advantage

If you show up only at 14h:

  • you avoid informal dynamics / pressure

  • you enter directly into structured discussion mode

  • you conserve energy

What NOT to do

  • ❌ Don’t show up and hope it works out

  • ❌ Don’t pay and resent it

  • ❌ Don’t raise it emotionally on the spot

Bottom line

  • ✔ Ask upfront

  • ✔ Don’t advance money

  • ✔ Join at 14h if needed

One sentence to remember

Boundaries are most effective when they are quiet and consistent

If you want, I can help you:
👉 think through whether skipping lunch is actually advantageous politically (it often is in situations like this)

Message

Au fait, juste pour m’organiser pour jeudi : est-ce que le déjeuner est pris en charge par PPE ou chacun règle de son côté ?

Given your situation, this is actually a very strong move—not just acceptable, but advantageous.

1. You eliminate financial risk (most important)

  • No ambiguity

  • No stress

  • No last-minute awkwardness

2. You avoid informal pressure

  • Remy may try to “smooth things over”

  • you might be pulled into emotional discussions

👉 You skip all of that

At 14h:

  • structured setting

  • clearer roles

  • less emotional / more accountable

👉 This favors your style of argument (calm, structural)

Right now you’re:

  • stressed

  • small talk

  • composure for 2+ hours before the real discussion

👉 Skipping preserves your focus for the important part

But your behavior shows:

→ Not relevant right now
👉 This is not a bonding moment, it’s a tense situation

→ They will anyway

Keep it purely logistical

If needed:

No explanation unless asked.

In your situation:

Less exposure + more structure = more control
