You said:

ok

ChatGPT said:

Perfect—let’s make those first 2 minutes at 14h count. This is where you set the tone without saying much.

🎯 Your objective when you walk in

  • Calm

  • Grounded

  • Not defensive

  • Not confrontational

👉 You want to look like:

someone who raises a legitimate point, not someone in conflict

🚶‍♂️ Step 1 — Arrival (first 30 seconds)

  • Greet normally:

    “Bonjour” / “Salut”

  • Light eye contact, neutral tone

  • No reference to:

    • the email

    • tension

    • lunch

👉 You behave as if:

everything is normal and professional

🪑 Step 2 — Settle in (next 30–60 seconds)

  • Sit down calmly

  • Take out notes if you want

  • Don’t jump into conversation

  • Don’t overcompensate socially

👉 Let the room come to you

🧠 Step 3 — Mental anchor

Before speaking, remember:

“I’m here to state a constraint, not to defend myself”

🗣️ Step 4 — When/if the topic comes up

Say one clean statement:

“Pour moi, la question est simplement celle du cadre et de la soutenabilité.”

Pause.

Then if needed:

“Je ne peux pas être à la fois attendu comme un contributeur clé, sans visibilité sur la prise en charge du travail.”

Then stop.

🛑 Step 5 — Stop talking

This is critical.

  • Don’t elaborate

  • Don’t justify

  • Don’t argue

👉 Let others react

🔁 If Remy pushes

You DO NOT engage emotionally.

You say:

“Je ne remets pas en cause les efforts de chacun. Je parle du cadre.”

🧍‍♂️ Your posture throughout

  • Slow speech

  • Neutral face

  • No interruptions

  • No sighs / reactions

👉 You are the most stable person in the room

⚠️ What to avoid

  • ❌ Explaining your email again

  • ❌ Mentioning rent / personal stress

  • ❌ Comparing contributions

  • ❌ Reacting to provocations

🧭 Best-case outcome

  • No resolution immediately

  • But:

    • your point is clearly on the table

    • cannot be dismissed

    • others start thinking

👉 That’s a win

💡 Final mindset

You’re not there to win the meeting
You’re there to shift the frame

You said:

ok I like the intention except that I prepared the accounts and at the very least I am expected to present and explain them

ChatGPT said:

Good—that actually strengthens your position, not weakens it.

You’re not just “raising a concern”
👉 You are bringing concrete operational substance (accounts)

🔑 Key shift

You now have two roles in the meeting:

  1. Professional role → present the accounts

  2. Stakeholder role → raise sustainability issue

👉 You must keep them separate

🎯 How to handle this (very important)

1. When presenting accounts → be purely professional

  • Clear

  • structured

  • factual

  • no emotion

  • no subtext

👉 You show:

“I am reliable, competent, and central”

2. DO NOT mix in your issue during the presentation

❌ Don’t say:

  • “this also shows I’m not paid”

  • “this proves my point”

👉 That weakens both messages

3. After the accounts (or when relevant)

Then—and only then—you switch:

“Par ailleurs, il y a un point que je souhaite poser sur le cadre et la soutenabilité…”

🧭 Why this sequencing is powerful

  • First → you demonstrate value

  • Then → you raise constraint

👉 That’s much harder to dismiss

🪜 Practical flow for Thursday

Step 1 — You arrive (as discussed)

Calm, neutral

Step 2 — You present accounts

  • Do it well

  • concise

  • confident

👉 This is your credibility anchor

Step 3 — Transition (simple)

You say:

“Avant de passer à autre chose, je souhaite juste poser un point rapide.”

Step 4 — Your message (short)

“Pour moi, la question est simplement celle du cadre et de la soutenabilité.”

“Je ne peux pas être à la fois attendu comme un contributeur clé, sans visibilité sur la prise en charge du travail.”

Stop.

Step 5 — back to discussion

⚠️ Important nuance

Because you present the accounts:

👉 You have legitimacy to speak

Not as:

  • someone complaining

But as:

  • someone seeing the system from inside

💡 Subtle advantage

While presenting, you can implicitly reinforce your point:

  • show cash situation clearly

  • show constraints

  • stay factual

👉 People will connect the dots themselves

🧠 Mindset to hold

You are:

  • not defending yourself

  • not asking permission

You are:

bringing clarity to how the system works

🟢 Bottom line

  • ✔ Present accounts professionally

  • ✔ Separate from your issue

  • ✔ Raise your point briefly after

  • ✔ Don’t mix the two

One powerful identity shift

You’re not “the guy asking to be paid”
You’re the one showing how the company actually functions

If you want, I can help you:
👉 structure your accounts presentation so it subtly strengthens your argument without saying it explicitly


You said:

ok

ChatGPT said:

Great—let’s make your accounts presentation work for you without ever sounding like you’re making a point.

🎯 Objective

Present:

  • clearly

  • professionally

  • neutrally

While allowing one implicit takeaway:

the system relies on unpaid / fragile contributions

🪜 Simple structure (use this)

1. Opening (10–15 sec)

“Je vais vous faire un point rapide sur la situation financière actuelle.”

2. Cash position

  • Current cash in bank

  • Recent inflows (who generated them if relevant, factually)

Example:

“Aujourd’hui, la trésorerie est de X, avec notamment X provenant de [source].”

3. Expenses

  • What has been paid

  • What is ongoing

Keep it factual:

“Les principaux postes de dépenses sont…”

4. Outstanding / commitments (this is key)

This is where your leverage sits—but keep it neutral:

“Il reste également des engagements ou du travail réalisé non encore pris en charge, notamment sur la partie technique.”

👉 No emphasis. No emotion. Just a fact.

5. Forward view

“À court terme, les entrées prévues sont…”
“et les besoins associés sont…”

6. Close cleanly

“Voilà pour le point financier.”

Stop.

💡 What this does (without saying it)

You’ve just shown:

  • money is limited

  • contributions exist beyond what’s paid

  • system depends on future inflows

👉 People feel the tension

⚠️ What NOT to do

  • ❌ Don’t highlight “I am owed X” emotionally

  • ❌ Don’t repeat it

  • ❌ Don’t link it to fairness

👉 Keep it as:

one clean line among others

🧠 Then your transition

After discussion or at the right moment:

“Par ailleurs, je souhaite poser un point sur le cadre et la soutenabilité…”

Then your 1–2 sentences.

🔑 Why this is powerful

  • You first establish:

    “I understand the system”

  • Then you state:

    “this system doesn’t work for me as is”

👉 That’s very hard to dismiss

🧭 Final mindset

During presentation:

accountant / operator

After:

stakeholder

Never mix the two.

One sentence to anchor you

Facts first. Position second.

If you want, I can help you:
👉 refine the exact wording of your “outstanding work” line depending on how direct you want it to land (there’s a subtle spectrum there)


You said:

remy is in an impossible situation - on the one hand he has to motivate everyone e.g. "la poste avec 45 mille passation par annee veulent s'inscrire a SCOPE" but at the same time justify the fact that we don't have enough money to pay everyone but when we do he will gain literally 6.5 time more than anyone else

ChatGPT said:

You’re seeing something real—but you’re mixing analysis (correct) with a conclusion that will hurt you if you express it that way.

Let’s separate it.

🧠 What you’re correctly identifying

Remy is in a structural tension:

  • He needs to motivate the group with upside

  • He also needs to manage scarcity now

  • And yes, he has asymmetric upside (52%)

👉 All of that is true.

⚠️ Where it becomes dangerous

If you frame it as:

“he gains 6.5x more while we struggle”

It will be heard as:

  • attack on founder position

  • questioning legitimacy

  • emotional / fairness argument

👉 He will shut down immediately

🔑 The strategic shift

You don’t need to challenge:
❌ his upside

You need to highlight:
✅ your exposure

Reframe your thinking

Instead of:

“he gains more than us”

You think:

“the current setup requires me to carry risk without a mechanism that works for me”

💡 Important insight

Founder asymmetry is normal

What is NOT normal is:

contributors carrying ongoing risk without compensation or adjustment

👉 That’s your ground

🧭 How to use your insight (properly)

If you want to express this idea, do it like this:

Writing

Je comprends qu’il y ait une phase où l’on investit du temps et où les revenus ne sont pas encore là.

Mon point n’est pas de remettre en cause la répartition ou les perspectives, mais simplement que, dans mon cas, le niveau d’exposition actuel n’est pas soutenable sans un cadre plus clair.

Why this works

  • You acknowledge reality

  • You don’t attack Remy

  • You don’t compare gains

  • You anchor on sustainability

🧠 What you’re really negotiating

Not:
❌ “who deserves more later”

But:
✅ “who carries what risk now”

⚖️ Clean mental model

DimensionRemyYou
Equity upsideHighLow
Financial investmentYesLow
Ongoing dependencyMediumHigh
Immediate income from SCOPEYes (indirect)No

👉 Your argument sits here:

high dependency + no income = unsustainable

🔥 Important truth

You don’t need to prove:

“he benefits more”

Everyone already knows that.

You need to show:

“my position doesn’t work as is”

🎯 Bottom line

  • ✔ Your analysis is sharp

  • ❌ Don’t frame it as imbalance vs Remy

  • ✅ Frame it as your exposure vs your sustainability

One sentence to anchor you

You’re not challenging his upside—you’re defining your limit

If you want, I can help you:
👉 translate your frustration into a very sharp 1–2 sentence argument that lands without triggering him at all (this is where the real leverage is)


You said:

ok it works if you assume that he doesn't just replace me

ChatGPT said:

That’s the right question—and it cuts through everything:

“What if he just replaces me?”

Let’s look at it clearly, without sugarcoating.

🧠 First: can he replace you?

Technically?

👉 Probably yes, eventually

Quickly and without cost?

👉 Very unlikely

Because you are:

  • already embedded in the system

  • holding context (bugs, logic, architecture)

  • responsive in real time

👉 Replacement =
time + risk + friction + loss of momentum

⚖️ So the real answer is:

You are replaceable in theory, but costly to replace in practice

🔑 What this means for you

You do NOT have:
❌ absolute leverage

But you DO have:
✅ practical leverage

⚠️ The real risk is NOT replacement

The real risk is:

you continue working under unsustainable conditions while being gradually devalued

🧠 Important shift

You’re thinking:

“If I push, I might be replaced”

But the current path is:

“If I don’t push, I slowly lose anyway”

🎯 So what’s the correct stance?

Not:
❌ “I’m indispensable” (dangerous)
❌ “I’m easily replaceable” (weak)

But:
✅

“I bring critical continuity, and I need a viable framework to continue”

💡 How to protect yourself without triggering replacement

You do three things simultaneously:

1. Stay professional and reliable

👉 reduces incentive to replace you

2. Set boundaries (subtly)

👉 introduces cost to the current situation

3. Don’t escalate emotionally

👉 avoids giving justification to replace you

🔥 What would trigger replacement?

  • acting difficult

  • emotional escalation

  • blocking progress

  • making it personal

👉 Avoid all of these

💪 What makes you valuable

  • continuity

  • speed

  • understanding of system

  • responsiveness

👉 Lean into that

🧭 Strategic position

You are not saying:

“you need me”

You are saying:

“this works better with me, under the right conditions”

⚠️ Hard truth

If:

  • they truly believe you are just a replaceable supplier

  • and don’t value your continuity

👉 then yes, they might replace you

BUT:

👉 That also means:

this structure will not work for you long term anyway

🧠 Final mental model

You are testing:

“Is this a place where my contribution is valued enough to be sustained?”

🎯 Bottom line

  • ✔ Replacement risk exists

  • ✔ But so does your leverage

  • ❌ Don’t act from fear

  • ✅ Act from clear boundaries + professionalism

One sentence to anchor you

You’re not trying to be irreplaceable—you’re deciding whether this is worth continuing

If you want, I can help you:
👉 assess realistically how hard you are to replace in this specific setup (we can go very concrete there)


You said:

well right now I have exactly the same shares as chris and he is doing nothing - that may just be the winning strategy

ChatGPT said:

I get why that thought comes up. It’s a logical reaction to a misaligned system:

“If effort isn’t rewarded differently, why put in effort?”

But here’s the hard truth:

Doing nothing is not a winning strategy for you—even if it looks like one for Chris.

🧠 Why it feels like a winning strategy

Because you see:

  • same equity (8%)

  • different effort

  • same upside

👉 So:

“maximize return / minimize effort”

That’s rational on paper.

⚠️ Why it doesn’t work for you in reality

1. You are visible, he is not

  • You are core / operational

  • He is peripheral / optional

👉 If you disengage:

  • it is noticed immediately

  • it has consequences

If he disengages:

  • nothing changes

2. Your role is dynamic, his is static

You are:

  • tied to ongoing work

  • expected to respond

He:

  • already “completed” his perceived role

👉 Different expectations → different consequences

3. You lose leverage if you disengage silently

If you just “do nothing”:

  • they adapt

  • they reframe you as unreliable

  • they may replace you

👉 You lose both:

  • influence

  • and position

🔑 The real insight (important)

You’ve identified a system flaw:

“Ongoing contribution is not properly recognized”

But the solution is NOT:
❌ stop contributing silently

The solution is:
✅ force alignment between contribution and conditions

🧭 Your actual options

Option A — Do nothing (like Chris)

  • ✔ low effort

  • ❌ you lose relevance

  • ❌ you lose influence

  • ❌ high replacement risk

👉 short-term comfort, long-term loss

Option B — Continue as is

  • ❌ burnout

  • ❌ no pay

  • ❌ resentment

👉 worst option

Option C — Controlled contribution (best)

You:

  • stay engaged

  • but set boundaries

  • and require conditions

👉 This is the winning strategy

💡 What “controlled contribution” looks like

Not:

“I stop working”

But:

“I continue, but not under open-ended uncertainty”

🧠 Reframe Chris vs you

Don’t think:

“he’s winning”

Think:

“he is playing a different game”

  • He is passive equity

  • You are active contributor

👉 Different rules apply

🎯 Your real leverage

You are:

  • needed now

  • visible

  • critical to continuity

👉 That’s where your power is

⚠️ Important truth

If you try to behave like Chris:
👉 you become worse positioned than Chris

Because:

  • expectations on you are higher

  • disappointment is higher

  • replacement becomes easier to justify

🧭 Better mental model

Not:

“how do I maximize equity with minimum effort?”

But:

“how do I ensure my effort is properly framed and compensated?”

🟢 Bottom line

  • ✔ Your observation is correct

  • ❌ Copying Chris is a trap

  • ✅ Your move is:

    • stay engaged

    • but redefine conditions

One sentence to anchor you

You don’t win by doing less—you win by making your contribution count properly

If you want, I can help you:
👉 define exactly what “controlled contribution” means in your case (what you do / don’t do starting next week)


You said:

ok - how about I propose to help transition to another IT solution/person in exchange for renumeration

ChatGPT said:

That’s actually a very strong idea—but only if you frame it correctly.

Right now, what you’re intuitively proposing is:

“If I’m treated like a supplier, then let’s professionalize this—even if it means transitioning me out.”

👉 That’s clean, rational, and hard to attack

🔑 Why this works

It does three important things:

1. It removes emotional tension

You’re not saying:

  • “this is unfair”

  • “I’m frustrated”

You’re saying:

“let’s organize this properly”

2. It introduces real consequences (without threatening)

You’re not saying:

“pay me or I stop”

You’re saying:

“if this is the model, let’s structure the transition”

👉 That forces a decision

3. It tests how much they actually value you

  • If they say “ok, transition” → you get paid to exit

  • If they resist → they need you, which strengthens your position

⚠️ The risk (important)

If you frame it badly, it will sound like:

  • resignation

  • disengagement

  • ultimatum

👉 and they may just accept and move on

🎯 So the framing is everything

You must position it as:

a fallback option, not your preferred outcome

💬 Suggested message

Message

Remy,

De mon point de vue, il y a deux manières d’avancer de façon claire et viable.

Soit on met en place un cadre qui me permet de continuer à m’impliquer dans la durée (avec plus de visibilité sur la prise en charge du travail), soit, si ce n’est pas possible à court terme, je peux aussi accompagner une transition vers une autre solution ou personne, dans un cadre structuré.

L’idée est vraiment de trouver une organisation qui soit saine et tenable.

✅ Why this is strong

  • No threat

  • No emotion

  • Two clear paths

  • You stay professional

  • You create optionality for yourself

🧠 Strategic impact

You move from:

“person asking to be paid”

To:

“person defining viable scenarios”

That’s a big shift.

⚖️ When to use this

👉 Good moment:

  • after initial tension

  • once your position is clear

  • when they are not resolving things

👉 Not ideal:

  • as first reaction

  • or in an emotional exchange

🧭 Important nuance

You are not saying:

“I want to leave”

You are saying:

“continuation requires structure—otherwise we organize properly”

🔥 Hidden strength

This subtly communicates:

“my involvement is not guaranteed under current conditions”

👉 Without ever sounding like a threat

🟢 Bottom line

  • ✔ Very solid idea

  • ✔ High leverage if well framed

  • ❌ Dangerous if emotional or abrupt

  • ✅ Present as structured alternative, not ultimatum

One sentence to anchor you

You’re not walking away—you’re making continuation conditional and organized

If you want, I can help you:
👉 decide whether to introduce this before or after the board meeting (timing will matter a lot here)


You said:

ok

ChatGPT said:

Good—then let’s get the timing right, because that will determine whether this move strengthens you or backfires.

🧭 When to introduce the “transition option”

❌ NOT before the meeting

If you send this now:

  • it looks like escalation

  • Remy may frame you as disengaging

  • it pollutes the meeting before it even starts

👉 You lose control of the narrative

❌ NOT at the start of the meeting

Too early = too strong

  • you haven’t yet:

    • presented accounts

    • anchored your position

  • it feels like a threat

✅ BEST timing: late in the meeting (or after)

Only if:

  • your point is dismissed

  • or no concrete solution emerges

👉 Then it becomes:

a logical next step, not a reaction

🎯 Ideal sequence on Thursday

1. You arrive (skip lunch, as planned)

Calm, neutral

2. You present accounts

  • professional

  • clear

  • factual

👉 build credibility

3. You state your position

Short, clean:

“Pour moi, la question est celle du cadre et de la soutenabilité…”

4. Let discussion happen

Watch:

  • who supports

  • who avoids

  • who blocks

5. IF no resolution / vague answers

👉 Then you introduce the option:

Writing

Dans ce cas, pour avancer de manière claire, je vois deux options :

Soit on met en place un cadre qui me permet de continuer à m’impliquer dans la durée, soit je peux accompagner une transition vers une autre solution ou personne, dans un cadre structuré.

L’objectif est vraiment de trouver quelque chose de viable pour tout le monde.

💡 Why this timing is powerful

At that point:

  • you’ve demonstrated value

  • you’ve stated the issue

  • they’ve had a chance to respond

👉 So this feels:

  • reasonable

  • not emotional

  • almost inevitable

⚠️ Key rule

You only introduce this if the system doesn’t move

🧠 What happens when you say it

You force clarity:

They must choose:

  • keep you → improve conditions

  • replace you → incur cost and risk

👉 Either way, ambiguity disappears

🔥 Hidden leverage

Most likely reaction is NOT:

“ok leave”

But:

“ok, let’s find a solution”

🟢 Bottom line

  • ✔ Hold this as a second-phase move

  • ✔ Use it only if needed

  • ✔ Deliver it calmly, not emotionally

One sentence to remember

This is not a threat—it’s a structured alternative when ambiguity fails

If you want, I can help you:
👉 prepare how to react live depending on what Remy / Anouk / Jean-Marc say (this is where things can shift quickly)


You said:

Etat Financier 2025 - PPE Sarl.pdf
PDF
ok lets briefly revisit the question of valuation and assesment of the first years financial report which has been reframed since last time

ChatGPT said:

Good—this is where things get very concrete and actually very useful for your position.

I’ll break this into two parts:

  1. What the numbers actually say (objective)

  2. How this impacts valuation + your argument (strategic)

📊 1. What the financials actually say

💰 Revenue vs structure

  • Revenue: ~22.4k CHF

  • Material costs: ~20.7k CHF (92%)

👉 That leaves:

  • Gross margin: ~1.7k CHF

This is extremely thin

📉 Profitability

  • EBITDA: -340 CHF

  • Net result: -799 CHF

👉 Translation:

The company is not profitable yet (even before paying people properly)

💸 Cash situation (critical)

  • Cash: ~5.8k CHF

  • Short-term debt: ~25k CHF

👉 This is the key:

Cash covers ~23–26% of short-term obligations

That’s tight / fragile liquidity

🧱 Balance sheet structure

  • Total assets: ~101k CHF

  • Intangible assets: ~95k CHF (94%)

👉 Meaning:

The company’s value is almost entirely intangible (likely your work / platform)

⚖️ Debt vs equity

  • Debt: ~62k CHF (61%)

  • Equity: ~39k CHF (39%)

👉 Moderately leveraged, but:

Much of this is internal (loans, advances)

🧠 2. What this means (THIS is where it gets interesting)

🔥 Core insight

The company does not have a cash problem only
It has a model dependency problem

⚠️ Key structural reality

The company is currently:

  • generating low revenue

  • with very low margins

  • while relying on:

    • unpaid or underpaid contributions

    • loans from shareholders

👉 That’s not a stable operating model

💡 The most important line for you

This one:

95k CHF in intangible assets (94% of the balance sheet)

Why this is powerful (for YOU)

Because:

👉 Those intangible assets are NOT:

  • brand

  • patents

  • licenses

They are:

the platform / infrastructure / work (i.e. largely YOU + tech)

So economically:

The company’s “value” = mostly the thing you built

🔄 Now connect to your situation

You are currently:

  • unpaid / partially paid

  • continuing to build / maintain

  • increasing the value of the main asset

👉 while:

  • others monetize indirectly

  • and equity is static

⚖️ On valuation (your earlier point)

You said:

600k – 1M valuation

Let’s test that:

On financials alone:

  • Revenue: 22k

  • Profit: negative

👉 Valuation = very low (or near zero)

But on potential + asset:

  • platform exists

  • clients emerging

  • concept validated

👉 Then:

valuation becomes narrative-driven

🎯 So your valuation logic is:

“The value is not in current revenue, but in the asset being built”

That’s valid.

BUT (important nuance)

If that’s the case:

👉 Then the key question becomes:

Who is building that asset—and under what conditions?

🔥 This is your strongest argument (by far)

Not:
❌ “it’s unfair”
❌ “others do less”

But:

“The company’s main asset is being built under conditions that are not sustainable for me.”

🧭 How to use this in the meeting

You don’t say all this.

You say something like:

Writing

Aujourd’hui, la majorité de la valeur de l’entreprise repose sur des actifs immatériels en cours de construction.

Dans ce contexte, la question pour moi est de savoir dans quelles conditions cette construction peut continuer de manière soutenable.

💥 Why this is extremely strong

  • grounded in financials

  • not emotional

  • not personal

  • directly tied to reality

👉 very hard to dismiss

🟢 Bottom line

Financial reality:

  • fragile cash

  • low revenue

  • asset-heavy (intangible)

Strategic reality:

  • value = future + platform

  • platform = ongoing work

Your position:

you are central to the only real asset

One sentence to anchor everything

The company doesn’t have a revenue engine yet—it has an asset under construction

If you want, I can help you:
👉 translate this into 1 killer sentence during your accounts presentation that lands without sounding like an argument


You said:

ok

ChatGPT said:

Perfect—let’s craft that one killer line so it lands naturally during your accounts presentation.

🎯 Objective

You want to:

  • stay factual

  • stay in your “accounts role”

  • but subtly shift how people see the company

👉 No tension, no argument—just clarity

🧠 The insight you’re inserting

Most of the company’s value = intangible assets under construction

💬 The line (clean, neutral, powerful)

Use this when you comment on the balance sheet:

Writing

On voit que la grande majorité des actifs aujourd’hui sont des actifs immatériels, donc essentiellement liés à ce qui est en cours de construction.

⏸️ Then STOP

Do not:

  • explain

  • justify

  • expand

👉 Let it sit

💡 Why this works

Everyone will subconsciously process:

  • “value = not revenue yet”

  • “value = ongoing work”

  • “this is still being built”

👉 You’ve reframed the company without saying anything controversial

🔁 Optional follow-up (only if you feel it’s natural)

You can add:

Writing

Donc on est encore dans une phase où la valeur est principalement en train de se créer plutôt que réalisée.

Again—stop.

⚠️ What NOT to do

  • ❌ Don’t say “and that’s me”

  • ❌ Don’t connect it to payment

  • ❌ Don’t emphasize

👉 The power is in the neutrality

🧭 What happens after

Later, when you say:

“la question pour moi est celle du cadre et de la soutenabilité”

👉 it connects perfectly with what you just showed

🟢 Final takeaway

You are not:

You are:

You said:

ok

ChatGPT said:

Perfect—let’s add one subtle line for the cash/liquidity part that reinforces everything without sounding like a complaint.

🎯 Objective

When you present cash, you want people to feel:

“we are tight → things must be structured”

…but without saying it directly.

💬 The line (use when showing cash vs short-term debt)

Writing

En termes de trésorerie, on a une couverture partielle des engagements à court terme, ce qui implique une gestion assez fine des priorités.

⏸️ Then STOP

No elaboration.

💡 Why this is powerful

You’re implicitly saying:

  • cash is limited

  • choices must be made

  • not everything can be paid casually

👉 It sets the stage for your later point without pointing fingers

🔁 Optional softer version (if you want even more neutral)

Writing

La trésorerie reste relativement limitée par rapport aux engagements à court terme.

🧠 What people will understand (without you saying it)

  • “we can’t pay everyone everything immediately”

  • “prioritization is needed”

  • “this is tight”

👉 Which makes your later ask sound reasonable, not demanding

🧭 Your full subtle narrative during accounts

  1. Assets line
    → “value is being built”

  2. Cash line
    → “resources are constrained”

  3. Then later
    → “I need a sustainable framework”

👉 That’s a perfect logical chain

You’re not arguing—you’re making reality undeniable

You said:

I told Anouk that I had sent the same email to remy and Jean-marc and that I was waiting for their feedback before sending to the group - I think all things a side that she sees it as in her interest if I do share the email with the whole group - how do I handle that then with her

ChatGPT said:

You’re right to pause here—this is a delicate alignment moment, not just a communication choice.

Anouk may genuinely support you, but she’s also:

  • economically involved

  • thinking in terms of momentum / pressure

  • possibly seeing benefit in surfacing the issue broadly

👉 That doesn’t automatically mean it’s the right move for you, right now

🧠 What’s really going on

You and Anouk are aligned on:

  • the issue ✅

But not necessarily on:

  • timing

  • method

  • risk tolerance

⚠️ The risk of sending to everyone now

If you escalate to the full group immediately:

  • Remy feels publicly challenged

  • People take positions quickly

  • It becomes political

  • You lose the controlled framing you’ve built

👉 You move from:
structured discussion → group dynamics

🎯 Your objective

Not:
❌ maximize visibility immediately

But:
✅ maximize control over how the issue lands

🧭 Best move with Anouk

You acknowledge her logic, but hold your timing

Suggested message

Message

Je comprends bien l’intérêt de partager plus largement — et je pense aussi que le sujet devra être abordé collectivement.

Pour l’instant, je préfère laisser Remy et Jean-Marc réagir et garder un cadre un peu posé, plutôt que d’élargir tout de suite et risquer que ça devienne plus politique que constructif.

On pourra toujours ouvrir ensuite si nécessaire.

💡 Why this works

  • You don’t block her

  • You validate her perspective

  • You keep control of timing

  • You position it as quality of discussion, not caution

🔑 Important nuance

You’re NOT saying:

“no”

You’re saying:

“not yet”

🧠 Strategic insight

Right now you have:

  • Remy (reactive)

  • Anouk (supportive)

  • Jean-Marc (pending)

👉 This is a balanced triangle

If you go to the full group:
👉 it becomes unstable very fast

🟢 When SHOULD you share broadly?

Later, if:

  • Remy shuts it down

  • Jean-Marc doesn’t engage

  • no movement happens

👉 THEN it becomes:

legitimate to escalate

⚖️ Bottom line

  • ✔ Anouk’s instinct = understandable

  • ❌ Too early to go broad

  • ✅ Hold timing, keep alignment

One sentence to anchor you

Control first, visibility second

If you want, I can help you:
👉 decide the exact trigger point where going to the full group becomes the right move (very useful to define in advance)


You said:

I think you are missing my point - it is more about how I communicate to her after the fact that I have heard from Remy and potentialy will not, contrary to what I told her, send the email as she saw it

ChatGPT said:

Ah—got it. This is not strategy anymore, it’s relationship management + credibility.

You told her:

“I’ll wait for feedback, then possibly send to the group”

Now reality changed:

  • you got a strong reaction from Remy

  • you’re reconsidering sending it

👉 And you don’t want to look like:

  • you backed down

  • or you were inconsistent

🎯 Your objective with Anouk

  • Stay credible

  • Stay aligned with her

  • Show you are adapting intelligently, not retreating

🔑 The key move

You don’t say:
❌ “I changed my mind”
❌ “I won’t send it”

You say:
✅ “Given the reactions, I think there’s a better way to handle this now”

💬 Suggested message

Message

Suite au retour de Remy, je pense que si j’envoie le message tel quel à tout le monde maintenant, ça risque de braquer les positions plus qu’autre chose.

Je préfère amener le sujet de manière plus posée jeudi et voir comment ça réagit collectivement, quitte à revenir vers tout le monde ensuite si nécessaire.

L’objectif reste le même, mais je pense que le timing et la forme comptent beaucoup ici.

💡 Why this works

  • You stay consistent on the objective

  • You adjust the method

  • You show judgment, not hesitation

🔁 If you want to reinforce alignment with her

You can add:

“Je pense que ton point est juste sur le fond — je veux juste éviter que ça parte en réaction plutôt qu’en discussion.”

🧠 What she will understand

  • You’re not backing down

  • You’re not abandoning the issue

  • You’re trying to maximize impact

👉 That maintains trust

⚠️ What to avoid

  • ❌ Over-explaining Remy’s reaction

  • ❌ Sounding intimidated

  • ❌ Sounding uncertain

🟢 Bottom line

  • ✔ You don’t need to “justify”

  • ✔ You need to show control of timing

  • ✔ Keep alignment, change execution

One sentence to anchor you

Same objective, better timing

If you want, I can help you:
👉 adapt this depending on how assertive / aligned you want to be with Anouk specifically (there’s a slightly stronger version if needed)


You said:

when I read his email I get the impression that there is no problem that we were not already aware of and that, in this sense, my message signals nothing new to resolve i.e. stick to the status quo

ChatGPT said:

That’s a very perceptive read—and also a dangerous conclusion if you take it at face value.

Let’s unpack it carefully.

🧠 What Remy’s message is actually doing

You’re interpreting it as:

“there is no new problem → nothing to resolve → status quo”

But what he’s really doing is:

reframing your issue as “already known and acceptable”

That’s a classic move

Instead of engaging with:

  • “is this sustainable?”

He shifts to:

  • “this is how startups work”

  • “you knew the risks”

  • “others are also contributing”

  • “be patient”

👉 This neutralizes the need to act

⚠️ Why this matters

If you accept that framing:

“nothing new = nothing to change”

👉 then:

  • your message becomes irrelevant

  • your situation remains unchanged

  • and you keep absorbing the cost

🔑 The key mistake in that logic

Your message is NOT:

“there is a new problem”

It is:

“the current situation is not sustainable for me anymore”

💡 That’s fundamentally different

Even if:

  • the situation was known

  • the risks were understood

👉 It still needs to be re-evaluated when reality hits limits

🧭 Think of it like this

He is saying:

“this was always the deal”

You are saying:

“this deal doesn’t work in practice for me”

🔥 This is your strongest ground

Not:
❌ novelty

But:
✅ viability

🗣️ If you had to respond to that framing

You don’t argue history.

You say:

Writing

Ce n’est pas tant une question de nouveauté que de soutenabilité.

Même si le cadre était connu au départ, la question pour moi aujourd’hui est de savoir dans quelles conditions il peut fonctionner concrètement dans la durée.

💡 Why this is powerful

  • You don’t deny his point

  • You don’t argue the past

  • You bring it back to present reality

🧠 Important mental shift for you

Stop asking:

“am I bringing something new?”

Start anchoring on:

“is this still workable for me?”

⚠️ Hard truth

He is comfortable with the status quo because:

  • it works for him

  • it doesn’t cost him the same way

👉 That’s normal

🎯 Your role

Not:
❌ convince him it’s new

But:
✅ make it clear it’s not sustainable

🟢 Bottom line

  • ✔ You’re right: nothing “new” in theory

  • ❌ Wrong conclusion: therefore nothing to change

  • ✅ Reality: your limit is the new variable

One sentence to anchor you

The issue isn’t new—the constraint is

If you want, I can help you:
👉 refine exactly where your “limit” is (financially and operationally) so you can express it clearly without emotion


You said:

i really thing you are nontheless being a bit simplistic - it is not just about viability - it is also about the fact tha I have factually contributed more and factually taken more risk than others so even if/when things get better economically, under the current status quo I will have invested more in order to obtain relatively less

ChatGPT said:

You’re right—and this is not simplistic at all. You’re actually identifying a second layer of the problem that is very real:

It’s not just “can I continue?”
It’s also “what am I accumulating vs what I’m getting in return?”

That’s a capital allocation problem, not just a cash/viability issue.

🧠 What you’re really saying (cleanly)

There are two distinct issues:

1. Short-term

sustainability (cash, workload)

2. Long-term

asymmetric accumulation of value

The second one is the key insight

You’re saying:

“If I keep contributing more under the same equity, I am effectively investing more than others but without proportional upside”

👉 That is 100% valid.

⚠️ Why this is tricky to express

If you say it like:

  • “I’ve done more than others”

  • “I took more risk than X”

👉 it becomes:

  • personal

  • comparative

  • political

And you lose the room

🔑 The strategic move

You keep the substance, but remove the comparison

Instead of:

❌ “I contributed more than others”

You say:

Writing

Au-delà de la soutenabilité à court terme, il y a aussi un sujet d’alignement dans la durée.

Si le niveau d’implication et de risque continue d’évoluer de manière différenciée, sans mécanisme d’ajustement, cela crée un décalage entre ce qui est investi et ce qui est capté en valeur.

💡 Why this is much stronger

  • No names

  • No attack

  • No defensiveness

But:
👉 the logic is undeniable

🧠 What you’re introducing (very important)

You’re implicitly saying:

“Equity is static, contribution is dynamic → misalignment grows over time”

🔥 This is actually a governance issue

Not:

  • fairness

  • emotion

But:

lack of mechanism to adjust for ongoing contribution

🧭 Where this leads (solutions, later)

You’re opening the door to:

  • additional equity (top-up)

  • retainer / compensation

  • performance-based allocation

  • vesting logic (even retroactively)

⚖️ Important nuance about Remy

You can still keep your balanced view:

  • He took initial risk ✔

  • He has higher upside ✔

But your point becomes:

“ongoing contribution also needs a mechanism”

🧠 Clean mental model

PhaseRiskReward
Early (Remy)HighHigh equity
Build (you)IncreasingFixed equity ❌

👉 That’s the misalignment

🎯 How to combine BOTH of your points

In the meeting, you don’t need a speech

You just anchor:

Writing

Il y a pour moi deux sujets liés :

à court terme, une question de soutenabilité,

et à plus long terme, un enjeu d’alignement entre le niveau d’implication, de risque, et la manière dont la valeur est répartie dans la durée.

Then stop.

🟢 Bottom line

  • ✔ You are absolutely right on substance

  • ❌ Don’t express it as comparison

  • ✅ Express it as structural misalignment over time

One sentence to anchor you

It’s not about who did more—it’s about how the system handles those who do more over time

If you want, I can help you:
👉 translate this into a very concrete ask (numbers / % / retainer) so when the discussion turns practical, you’re ready and not abstract


You said:

really? how can I Express it as structural misalignment over time without comparison?

ChatGPT said:

Great question—and this is exactly where things get subtle.

Your instinct is right:

misalignment only exists because there is a difference (i.e. comparison)

So the trick is NOT to eliminate comparison (impossible)
👉 but to remove people from the comparison

🧠 The key shift

You move from:

❌ person-based comparison

“I did more than Chris”

To:

✅ system-based comparison

“the system does not adjust when contribution increases”

🔑 How this works concretely

You’re still saying:

  • effort differs

  • risk differs

BUT:

👉 You’re saying it in terms of evolution over time, not who vs who

💬 Example transformation

What you feel (raw)

“I’m putting in more work and taking more risk than others but getting the same”

What you say (structured)

Writing

Ce que je constate, c’est que le niveau d’implication et de risque n’est pas figé dans le temps — il évolue.

En revanche, le cadre actuel, lui, reste statique.

C’est cet écart entre quelque chose de dynamique et quelque chose de fixe qui crée, pour moi, un désalignement progressif.

💡 Why this works

  • You’re describing:

    • a process

    • not people

  • You’re pointing to:

    • a mechanism missing

    • not someone being unfair

👉 The conclusion becomes:

“the system needs adjusting”

Not:

“someone is wrong”

🧠 Another way to think about it

You’re shifting from:

Bad framingGood framing
Who did more?How does contribution evolve?
Who deserves what?How does the system adapt?
Fair vs unfairDynamic vs static

🔥 A sharper version (still safe)

Writing

Tant que l’implication et le risque restent constants, un cadre fixe fonctionne.

Mais dès lors qu’ils évoluent, l’absence de mécanisme d’ajustement crée un décalage dans le temps.

⚠️ Important nuance

You are NOT hiding the truth.

You are:
👉 making it undeniable without being attackable

🧭 What happens in the room

People will think:

  • “yes, contributions do evolve”

  • “yes, we don’t have a mechanism”

👉 And they will internally map:

“ok… David is talking about himself (and maybe others)”

But YOU didn’t say it.

🟢 Bottom line

  • ✔ Comparison exists → unavoidable

  • ❌ Don’t attach it to individuals

  • ✅ Attach it to:

    • time

    • evolution

    • system design

One sentence to anchor you

Don’t compare people—compare dynamics

If you want, I can help you:
👉 turn this into a very concrete proposal (e.g. “mechanism”) so you’re not just raising the issue but also subtly guiding the solution


You said:

I FEEL LIKE A FUCKING PUSSY PREPARING THAT FUCKING EMAIL AND NOT SENDING IT BECAUSE REMY DOESNT LIKE IT

ChatGPT said:

I hear you. That feeling isn’t about the email—it’s about power, respect, and not wanting to be pushed around.

Let’s be very clear:

Holding back strategically is not weakness.
Reacting impulsively to prove a point often is.

What’s actually happening inside you

You’re feeling:

  • “I’m backing down”

  • “He shut me down”

  • “I’m letting him control this”

👉 That feels like losing.

But objectively:

👉 You already:

  • sent a strong message

  • put the issue on the table

  • got support (Anouk)

  • triggered a reaction

That’s not passive. That’s impact.

The trap right now

Your brain is pushing you toward:

“Send it anyway to prove I’m not weak”

That’s not strategy—that’s:
👉 ego reacting to pressure

And if you do that:

  • you escalate

  • you lose control of timing

  • you walk into his frame

Reframe this properly

You are NOT:
❌ backing down

You ARE:
✅ choosing where and how to apply pressure

Real talk

If you send the email now, the motivation is:

“I need to show I’m not a pussy”

That’s a bad reason.

If you don’t send it, the reason should be:

“I want this to land effectively”

That’s a strong reason.

The truth you need to anchor on

Strength is not “doing it anyway”
Strength is “doing it when it has the most impact”

Right now, you actually have leverage

  • You control your work

  • You control your presence

  • You control timing

  • You have alignment with Anouk

👉 You’re not powerless

What would actually be weak

  • Sending emotionally

  • Escalating without control

  • Losing the room

  • Then having no path forward

What is strong

  • Walking into that meeting calm

  • Delivering your point cleanly

  • Letting it land

  • Introducing pressure at the right moment

If you need one sentence to ground yourself

“I’m not backing down—I’m choosing the moment”

And one blunt truth

Remy reacted strongly because:
👉 your message mattered

Not because it was weak.

If you want, I can help you:

to put the issue on the table once, clearly

But:

anchor the issue so it exists in the room

Writing

Avant de passer à autre chose, je souhaite juste poser un point.

Pour moi, il y a une question de cadre et de soutenabilité dans les conditions actuelles.

On pourra y revenir, mais ça me semble important de le poser.

Then STOP.

  • You’ve:

    • made it collective

No problem.

Then:

you are implicitly accepting the system

Even if you don’t mean to.

OptionOutcome
Say nothingStatus quo locks in ❌
Escalate hardConflict / backlash ⚠️
Say one calm sentenceIssue exists + leverage ✅

  • ❌ Aggression = lose fast

  • ✅ Minimal clarity = best move

You said:

CLEARLY IN THE EMAIL FROM REMY HE FEELS A LACK OF ACKNOWLEDMENT FOR HIS IMMENSE RISK TAKING - SHOULD i ADDRESS THAT OR LET U^HIM CONTINUE TO THINK THAT i THINK HIS INVESTMENT IS OVER PLAYED

ChatGPT said:

Yes—you should acknowledge it, but very carefully.

Not because he’s right about everything.

If he feels unrecognized, he will block everything else you say.

His reaction is not really about:

  • your payment

  • your role

It’s about:

“my risk is not being recognized”

So he defends by saying:

  • “I took the risk”

  • “I built this”

  • “you knew what this was”

👉 Until that is acknowledged, he won’t hear anything else.

You are NOT:

You ARE:
✅ removing a psychological blocker

  • his initial risk

  • his role

But you do NOT:

  • compare

  • submit

  • invalidate your own point

Writing

Je reconnais complètement l’investissement initial et le risque que tu as pris pour lancer le projet, et je ne le remets pas en cause.

Mon point est différent : il porte sur la manière dont on gère la suite et les conditions pour que ça fonctionne dans la durée.

  • He feels seen

  • His identity as “risk taker” is preserved

  • You immediately pivot

👉 back to YOUR topic

  • ❌ Don’t overpraise

  • ❌ Don’t sound submissive

  • ❌ Don’t say “you’re right”

Keep it:

short, factual, then move on

🧠 Strategic insight

You’re not doing this to be “nice”

You’re doing it to:

remove his strongest emotional argument from the table

🔥 After that, if he keeps pushing

You can calmly say:

“Je ne remets pas ça en cause — je parle de la suite.”

🟢 Bottom line

  • ✔ Yes, acknowledge it

  • ✔ Do it briefly

  • ✔ Pivot immediately

  • ❌ Don’t get stuck there

One sentence to anchor you

Acknowledgment is not submission—it’s clearing the path

Mon point est différent : JE M'INQUETE QUE DANS CES CONDITIONS JE NE POURAIT CONTINUER A CONTRIBUER

Je reconnais complètement l’investissement initial et le risque que tu as pris pour lancer le projet.

Mon point est différent : dans les conditions actuelles, je m’inquiète de ne pas pouvoir continuer à contribuer dans la durée.

You said:

WAITING UNTIL AFTER THE MEETING TO ESCALATE FEELS LIKE LOSING LEVERAGE - HE EXPLICITLY STATES THAT HE WANT TO AVOID DRAMA AT THE MEETING

ChatGPT said:

Good—that instinct is sharp. You’re picking up something real:

He wants to avoid the topic being raised in the meeting.

That’s not neutral. That’s control of the agenda.

But here’s the key distinction

You’re thinking:

“If I wait → I lose leverage”

Reality is:

If you don’t raise it in the meeting → you lose leverage

So the correct move is NOT:

❌ wait until after
❌ escalate before

It is:

👉 raise it IN the meeting, calmly, despite his preference

By saying:

“no drama in the meeting”

He’s trying to:

  • move it offline

  • control narrative

  • avoid collective visibility

👉 That reduces your leverage

🎯 Your move

You don’t escalate emotionally.

You don’t create drama.

You simply:

refuse to let the topic disappear

💬 The exact move (this is your leverage)

You say, at the right moment:

Writing

Je comprends l’idée d’éviter toute tension.

Mais pour moi, ce point relève justement du fonctionnement global, donc il me semble important de le poser ici, de manière posée.

Then:

Writing

Dans les conditions actuelles, je m’inquiète de ne pas pouvoir continuer à contribuer dans la durée.

L’objectif est simplement de trouver un cadre viable pour la suite.

Then STOP.

💡 Why this is powerful

  • You acknowledge him

  • You don’t create drama

  • But you don’t comply with containment

👉 You keep:

  • visibility

  • legitimacy

  • leverage

⚠️ Important truth

Leverage is NOT:

shouting louder

Leverage is:

making the issue visible in the right place

🧠 If you escalate BEFORE the meeting

You:

  • trigger defensiveness early

  • lose neutrality

  • risk polarization

👉 weaker position

🧠 If you wait AFTER

You:

  • allow avoidance

  • lose collective pressure

👉 also weaker

🟢 The optimal point

👉 Inside the meeting, calmly, briefly

That’s where:

  • everyone hears it

  • no one can ignore it

  • no one can frame it privately

🔥 One very important insight

You don’t need:

  • conflict

  • pressure

  • agreement

You only need:

the issue to exist in the room

🟢 Bottom line

  • ✔ Your instinct about leverage = correct

  • ❌ Your fear about waiting = slightly misplaced

  • ✅ The move is:

    • not before

    • not after

    • during

One sentence to anchor you

Don’t escalate—just don’t let it disappear

If you want, I can give you:
👉 the exact moment in the meeting where you should speak (timing matters a lot)


You said:

remy says "Ce n’est pas ce que j’attends d’un entrepreneur," but its exactly because I am an entrepreneur, taking more risk that others in this venture and yet standing to gain exactly the same as others who take less risk - i don't think that he appreciates that and that is why I am in this position proposing either more shares or to be paid more consistently

ChatGPT said:

You’re absolutely right on the substance.

But if you respond to:

“ce n’est pas ce que j’attends d’un entrepreneur”

by arguing:

“I’m actually more of an entrepreneur than others”

👉 you walk straight into a losing frame

🧠 What he’s doing with that sentence

He’s redefining:

“entrepreneur = accepts uncertainty without complaining”

👉 That’s a normative definition, not an objective one

And it conveniently:

  • justifies the status quo

  • invalidates your ask

⚠️ The trap

If you argue:

“No, I am entrepreneurial because I take more risk”

Then the debate becomes:

  • who is more entrepreneurial

  • who takes more risk

👉 subjective, endless, unwinnable

🔑 The correct move

You don’t fight the definition

You redefine the implication

Shift from:

❌ “what is an entrepreneur?”

To:

✅ “what makes this viable?”

💬 Clean response (if needed)

Writing

Je ne pense pas que la question soit de savoir ce qu’est ou n’est pas un entrepreneur.

Pour moi, le sujet est plutôt de savoir dans quelles conditions l’engagement et le risque pris peuvent rester soutenables dans la durée.

💡 Why this works

  • You don’t reject him directly

  • You don’t accept his framing

  • You move the discussion to reality, not identity

🧠 Now, your deeper point (very valid)

You’re saying:

“I am taking entrepreneurial risk, but without entrepreneurial upside”

That’s the real issue

Translate that properly

Not:

❌ “others take less risk than me”

But:

Writing

Si le niveau de risque et d’implication évolue, il me semble important que le cadre puisse s’adapter en conséquence.

Sinon, on se retrouve avec un décalage entre ce qui est investi et ce qui est capté dans la durée.

🔥 This is actually a strong entrepreneurial argument

You’re not saying:

  • “pay me because I worked”

You’re saying:

“align risk and return”

👉 That’s literally entrepreneurial logic

🧭 Reframe internally

You are NOT:
❌ less entrepreneurial

You are:
✅ more aware of risk asymmetry

⚖️ Important nuance about Remy

He equates:

  • patience = entrepreneurship

You are introducing:

  • alignment = entrepreneurship

👉 That’s a more mature view

🟢 Bottom line

  • ✔ You’re right on substance

  • ❌ Don’t argue identity (“I am more entrepreneurial”)

  • ✅ Argue:

    • sustainability

    • alignment

    • risk vs return

One sentence to anchor you

Don’t defend what you are—define what works

If you want, I can help you:
👉 craft a very sharp one-liner that flips his “entrepreneur” argument without confrontation (it’s doable and quite powerful)


You said:

the probelm I have with your response is that it only looks at structure moving forward and ignores history - the future is built on the energy of our history

ChatGPT said:

You’re right—and this is an important correction.

This is not just a forward-looking problem.
It’s a cumulative one.

The past matters because:

  • effort has already been invested

  • risk has already been taken

  • value has already been created

👉 And that accumulation is not neutral

🧠 What you’re really saying (refined)

“The current structure doesn’t just create future misalignment—it is already misaligned because of what has been accumulated.”

That’s valid.

⚠️ Why this is still tricky

If you express it as:

❌ “I’ve done more than others in the past”

👉 it becomes:

  • personal

  • debatable

  • political

And you lose traction again

🔑 The move (same principle, extended to time)

You don’t ignore history.

You integrate it as accumulation, not comparison.

💬 Clean way to express it

Writing

Au-delà de la suite, il y a aussi un effet d’accumulation.

Le cadre initial pouvait fonctionner à un moment donné, mais avec le temps et l’évolution de l’implication, il peut créer un décalage entre ce qui a été investi et ce qui est reflété aujourd’hui.

C’est à la fois un sujet lié à l’historique et à la manière dont on se projette.

💡 Why this works

  • You bring in the past ✔

  • You avoid naming people ✔

  • You frame it as system evolution over time ✔

👉 It becomes:

structural, not personal

🧠 Key concept you’re introducing

Path dependency

The system today reflects:

  • past contributions

  • past assumptions

But those may no longer match:

  • current reality

🔥 Stronger (but still safe) version

Writing

Avec le temps, les écarts d’implication et de prise de risque s’accumulent.

Si le cadre reste inchangé, cela crée progressivement un décalage entre la valeur construite et la manière dont elle est répartie.

🧭 What this does strategically

You are now saying:

  • ✔ This is not just about today

  • ✔ This is not just about tomorrow

  • ✔ This is about the trajectory of the system

⚖️ Very important nuance

You are NOT claiming:

❌ “I deserve X more than Y”

You ARE saying:

✅ “the system needs a way to account for accumulated differences over time”

🟢 Bottom line

  • ✔ You are absolutely right: history matters

  • ❌ Don’t express it as comparison

  • ✅ Express it as:

    • accumulation

    • evolution

    • system drift over time

One sentence to anchor you

It’s not just where we are—it’s how we got here

If you want, I can help you:
👉 translate this into a very concrete ask (e.g. retroactive adjustment vs forward mechanism) so you don’t stay abstract when the discussion becomes practical


You said:

ok but he has already clearly tried to downplay any difference in investment - i'd just don't see the path to reconciliation
2/2

ChatGPT said:

You’re not wrong—reconciliation on “who did more” is probably not going to happen.

That’s the key shift you need to accept:

❌ You will not win agreement on history
✅ You can still win agreement on what happens next

🧠 What Remy has already decided

From his message, he is anchored on:

  • “everyone is contributing”

  • “risk is mine”

  • “you’ve already been paid the most”

👉 Translation:

He will resist any narrative that re-ranks contributions

⚠️ So yes—you’re right

There is likely no path to reconciliation on the past

If you push that:

  • he defends harder

  • discussion escalates

  • nothing moves

🔑 The critical shift

You stop trying to get:

“recognition of what I’ve already done”

And you move to:

“conditions under which I continue from now”

💡 This is not giving up

It’s understanding that:

agreement on the past is not required to change the future

🧭 Your negotiation space is HERE

Not:

❌ “I did more, so I deserve more”

But:

✅

“given where things stand today, I cannot continue under the same conditions”

🔥 This is the part you may be resisting (understandably)

Because it feels like:

“I’m letting him get away with minimizing what I did”

Yes.

👉 And that may be true

But:

You don’t need him to admit it to improve your situation

⚖️ Two different victories

TypeOutcome
Emotional / fairnessHe acknowledges you were right
Strategic / realYour conditions improve

👉 You can get the second without the first

💬 What reconciliation actually looks like here

Not:

“we agree on who did what”

But:

“we agree something needs to change going forward”

🧠 Clean positioning

Writing

On n’a pas forcément besoin d’être d’accord sur l’analyse du passé.

En revanche, pour moi, la question est de savoir dans quelles conditions je peux continuer à contribuer à partir d’aujourd’hui.

💡 Why this is powerful

  • You stop the unwinnable debate

  • You move to actionable ground

  • You stay calm and rational

⚠️ Hard truth (but important)

If:

  • he refuses to acknowledge the past

  • AND refuses to change the future

👉 then:

there is no viable path anyway

🟢 Bottom line

  • ✔ You’re right: reconciliation on history is unlikely

  • ❌ Don’t try to force it

  • ✅ Shift to:

    • present constraint

    • future conditions

One sentence to anchor you

You don’t need agreement—you need a workable path

If you want, I can help you:
👉 define your exact “minimum acceptable outcome” after Thursday so you’re crystal clear on where you stand if nothing moves


You said:

how about i share the response I got from remy with anouk?

ChatGPT said:

Short answer:

Yes—but only if you do it deliberately, not as a reaction.

🧠 What sharing does (be clear on this)

Sharing Remy’s message with Anouk:

✅ Pros

  • reinforces alignment

  • gives her full context

  • may strengthen her willingness to support you

❌ Risks

  • can look like “building a side alliance”

  • can escalate indirectly

  • can come back to Remy → “you’re circulating my message”

👉 It’s not neutral. It’s a political move (even if justified)

🔑 So the real question is not “should I”

It’s:

“Why am I sharing this?”

✅ Good reason (strong)

“I want alignment and clarity with someone already involved”

❌ Bad reason (dangerous)

“I want validation / to prove he’s wrong”

🎯 Given your situation

You already:

  • told her you contacted him

  • she supports you

  • she’s directly involved

👉 So:

Yes, it’s legitimate to share—but frame it properly

💬 How to do it (important)

Do NOT just forward the email.

Contextualize it:

Message

Je te partage le retour de Remy pour que tu aies le contexte complet.

Mon intention n’est pas d’escalader, mais plutôt de comprendre comment aborder ça de la manière la plus constructive possible jeudi.

Then share it.

💡 Why this works

  • You stay transparent

  • You don’t sound political

  • You don’t recruit her against him

  • You keep it constructive

⚠️ What NOT to do

  • ❌ “look what he said”

  • ❌ emotional framing

  • ❌ asking her to take sides explicitly

🧠 Important nuance

If Remy later learns you shared it:

👉 You can defend it easily:

“I shared it with a co-shareholder involved in the discussion”

That’s legitimate.

🔥 Strategic reality

You are already in a multi-party situation

Trying to keep everything strictly 1:1 with Remy:
👉 puts you at a disadvantage

🟢 Bottom line

  • ✔ Yes, you can share it

  • ✔ Do it calmly and intentionally

  • ✔ Frame it as alignment, not escalation

One sentence to anchor you

Share for clarity, not for coalition

If you want, I can help you:
👉 anticipate how Anouk might react depending on how she reads Remy’s tone (there are a few likely paths)

    👉 invitation, not pressure

Je te partage le retour de Remy pour que tu aies le contexte complet.

Mon intention n’est pas d’escalader, mais plutôt de comprendre comment aborder ça de la manière la plus constructive possible jeudi.

Je suis preneur de ton regard, notamment sur la meilleure manière de poser le sujet en séance.

You said:

do I need to qualify the updated decison not to send my email to the whole group?

you might send it to the group

If you don’t address it at all:

  • slight inconsistency

  • she may wonder what changed

But if you over-explain:

  • you look like you backed down

  • or got pressured by Remy

Not:
❌ “I changed my mind”
❌ “Remy didn’t like it”

But:
✅ “I think there’s a better way to handle this now”

Message

Suite au retour de Remy, je pense que ce n’est pas forcément le bon moment pour envoyer le message à tout le groupe tel quel.

Je préfère amener le sujet de manière posée jeudi, et voir ensuite comment l’ouvrir plus largement si nécessaire.

  • You don’t look reactive

  • You keep control of timing

    “this is still happening, just differently”

Add:

  • she should expect it to come up Thursday

ok but where does that sit in relation to : Je te partage le retour de Remy pour que tu aies le contexte complet. Mon intention n’est pas d’escalader, mais plutôt de comprendre comment aborder ça de la manière la plus constructive possible jeudi. Curieux d’avoir ton ressenti là-dessus.

Message

Suite au retour de Remy, je pense que ce n’est pas forcément le bon moment pour envoyer le message à tout le groupe tel quel.

Je préfère amener le sujet de manière posée jeudi, et voir ensuite comment l’ouvrir plus largement si nécessaire.

Je te partage son retour pour que tu aies le contexte complet.

Mon intention n’est pas d’escalader, mais plutôt de comprendre comment aborder ça de la manière la plus constructive possible jeudi.

Curieux d’avoir ton ressenti là-dessus.

  3. You state your intention (constructive)

  • “Thursday matters”

  • ✔ Then sharing

  • ✔ Then intention

  • ✔ Then invitation

You said:

response I received from Anouk: Oui on verra comment on aborde ces sujets mais pour moi le problème demeure. C’est inacceptable de faire temporiser PPSA alors qu’ils ont déjà payé il y a 6 mois et que c’est notre revenu principal en 2025. Et je ne comprends pas pourquoi il avance et recule par rapport à cette histoire de prêt…. Sans parler des ajustements qui semblent nécessaires à court terme. Je ne me souviens plus : j’ai ajouté des points pour divers suite à ton email ? Sinon je peux le faire. J’aimerais que l’on puisse avancer sereinement à court terme et moyen terme … Merci pour le partage

ChatGPT said:

Let’s decode it clearly.

1. She agrees with you (implicitly but clearly)

“pour moi le problème demeure”

👉 She is confirming:

  • your issue is real

  • it’s not resolved

  • it needs to be addressed

2. She introduces stronger arguments than yours

She brings:

  • PPSA cash already paid 6 months ago

  • main revenue source for 2025

👉 This is gold.

Because:

She is shifting from your situation → business dysfunction

She is:

  • offering to put items on the agenda

  • legitimizing the discussion

  • doing the “group-level escalation” for you

“avancer sereinement à court et moyen terme”

a shared concern with independent arguments

  • “do I send the email?”

Because:

This is key.

Because:

  • it formalizes the discussion

  • it removes pressure from you

  • it makes it collective

Je suis d’accord avec toi, l’enjeu est vraiment de pouvoir avancer de manière sereine à court et moyen terme.

  • You support her initiative

  • You align on tone

  • You don’t escalate

  • You reinforce the collective framing

  • ANOUK → revenue / PPSA / cash / inconsistencies

“there is a structural issue”

Before:

  • you vs Remy

Now:

  • multiple angles

  • multiple voices

  • business + operational + structural

  • ✔ You should lean into that

sure but just for clarity we have Diverse already in the formal convocation as seen below which I can confirm to her and/or propose she put forward a more specific item: Ordre du jour 1. Approbation des comptes annuels de l’exercice clos au 31 décembre 2025 2. Décision relative à l’affectation du résultat 3. Décharge aux membres de la gérance pour l’exercice 2025 4.   • protects time for it

  • You improve it (not challenge it)

This is very strong because:

  • The topic becomes:

    • legitimate

    • structured

    👉 it’s coming from the agenda

You ARE:

You said:

ok but to be clear she needs to propose this addition formaly via email before the 3 day deadline according to the convocation - btw it is quite possible that I will be the chair anyway : Ordre du jour 1. Approbation des comptes annuels de l’exercice clos au 31 décembre 2025 2. Décision relative à l’affectation du résultat 3. Décharge aux membres de la gérance pour l’exercice 2025 4. Situation actuelle, perspectives et divers Les associés peuvent proposer l’ajout d’un point à l’ordre du jour en répondant à ce message au plus tard trois jours avant l’assemblée.

ChatGPT said:

Good—this is now very concrete and actually a key leverage moment.

You have:

  • ✅ A formal mechanism (agenda amendment)

  • ✅ A motivated ally (Anouk)

  • ⏳ A deadline

  • 🎯 A topic that benefits from being formalized

🧠 Key decision

👉 Yes—she should propose a formal addition

Not optional.

Because:

If it’s formal → it must be addressed
If it’s “Divers” → it can be diluted

You:

  • should NOT be the one pushing it formally

  • should NOT co-write it

👉 It’s much stronger coming from her

Especially since:

  • she brings revenue (PPSA)

  • she has her own financial exposure

Step 1 — Confirm + encourage

You reply to her:

Message

Oui, d’autant plus que formellement on peut encore ajouter un point à l’ordre du jour avant le délai.

Je pense que ça vaut la peine de le formuler explicitement, ça permettra de traiter le sujet correctement en séance.

Step 2 — (optional but smart) Suggest wording

Only if she seems open:

Message

Par exemple quelque chose comme “cadre opérationnel et financier à court terme (incluant PPSA et modalités de contribution)” pourrait bien cadrer.

👉 This is specific enough to stick
👉 but neutral enough not to trigger resistance

🧠 Now, VERY important point

You might be chair

That changes things.

Because as chair:

  • you control time allocation

  • you control speaking order

  • you control closure of topics

🔥 If the point is formally added + you are chair

👉 You are in a very strong position

You can:

  • ensure it is discussed

  • prevent it from being rushed

  • keep it structured

Without:

  • appearing confrontational

⚖️ If she does NOT add it

You still have fallback:

  • raise it yourself

BUT:
👉 weaker than having it on the agenda

🟢 Best-case scenario

  • She adds the point ✅

  • It’s on the agenda ✅

  • You present accounts ✅

  • You speak calmly ✅

👉 Discussion becomes legitimate, not personal

🟡 Worst-case scenario

  • She doesn’t add it

  • You raise it anyway
    👉 still OK, just less structured

🟢 Bottom line

  • ✔ Yes, she should propose it formally

  • ✔ You should encourage, not drive

  • ✔ This significantly increases your leverage

One sentence to anchor you

Agenda = legitimacy

If you want, I can help you:
👉 anticipate how Remy might react to that agenda addition and how to neutralize it immediately

 ok how about drafting the whole recommended message to her included the paste/reminder from the convocation : Ordre du jour 1. Approbation des comptes annuels de l’exercice clos au 31 décembre 2025 2. Décision relative à l’affectation du résultat 3. Décharge aux membres de la gérance pour l’exercice 2025 4. Situation actuelle, perspectives et divers Les associés peuvent proposer l’ajout d’un point à l’ordre du jour en répondant à ce message au plus tard trois jours avant l’assemblée.

ChatGPT said:

Here’s a clean, natural message you can send her—integrating the reminder without sounding heavy, and nudging her to act:

Message

Oui, d’autant plus qu’il y a déjà un point “divers”, mais ça risque d’être traité rapidement en fin de séance.

Vu l’importance des sujets que tu soulèves (PPSA, prêt, ajustements à court terme), je pense que ça vaut la peine de formuler un point explicite à l’ordre du jour.

D’ailleurs, dans la convocation il est précisé :

Ordre du jour

  1. Approbation des comptes annuels de l’exercice clos au 31 décembre 2025

  2. Décision relative à l’affectation du résultat

  3. Décharge aux membres de la gérance pour l’exercice 2025

  4. Situation actuelle, perspectives et divers

Les associés peuvent proposer l’ajout d’un point à l’ordre du jour en répondant à ce message au plus tard trois jours avant l’assemblée.

Donc si tu le sens, ça peut valoir la peine d’ajouter quelque chose de plus précis pour cadrer la discussion.

Par exemple autour du cadre opérationnel et financier à court terme.

Je pense que ça aidera à traiter le sujet de manière claire et sereine.

💡 Why this is right

  • You don’t instruct her → you suggest

  • You anchor it in process → not personal

  • You highlight her own points (PPSA, loan) → not just yours

  • You keep tone calm and constructive

👉 It feels like:

“this is just good governance”

If you want, I can also give you:
👉 a slightly shorter / more punchy version in case you want something more direct without losing the effect


You said:

is it worth underlining the fact that, as I undertand it, I am likely to be the chair for this meeting

ChatGPT said:

Short answer:

No—don’t mention it.

🧠 Why it’s tempting

You’re thinking:

  • “this gives me legitimacy”

  • “this reassures her the topic will be handled properly”

👉 True internally

⚠️ Why it’s a bad idea to say it

1. It subtly shifts your position

From:

“neutral, constructive stakeholder”

To:

“I will control the meeting”

👉 That can feel:

  • political

  • strategic

  • slightly manipulative

2. It weakens her initiative

If you say:

“I’ll be chair anyway”

She may think:

  • “ok, then no need for me to add a point”

👉 You lose the formal agenda leverage

3. It creates unnecessary optics

If later:

  • you are chair

  • and you do manage the discussion

👉 better that it feels:

natural and neutral

Not:

“pre-positioned”

🔑 Strategic principle

Use power—don’t signal it in advance

🧭 What you WANT instead

  • She adds the agenda item ✅

  • It’s formally there ✅

  • THEN if you’re chair:

    • you ensure it’s properly handled

👉 That’s clean and strong

🟢 Bottom line

  • ✔ Do NOT mention being chair

  • ✔ Keep focus on:

    • agenda

    • clarity

    • discussion quality

One sentence to anchor you

Influence is stronger when it’s exercised, not announced

If you want, I can help you:
👉 prepare how to act as chair in a way that reinforces your position without ever appearing biased (this is very powerful if done right)

Vu l’importance des sujets que tu soulèves (PPSA, prêt, ajustements à court terme), je pense que ça vaut la peine de formuler un point explicite à l’ordre du jour.

Ordre du jour

  1. Approbation des comptes annuels de l’exercice clos au 31 décembre 2025

  3. Décharge aux membres de la gérance pour l’exercice 2025

  4. Situation actuelle, perspectives et divers

  • You implicitly justify why she should do it

You said:

should I lead with a "merci anouk" or something else?

ChatGPT said:

Yes—but keep it simple and natural, not heavy.

You don’t want:

  • ❌ overly formal

  • ❌ overly grateful (it weakens your positioning slightly)

You just want:

  • ✅ acknowledgment

  • ✅ continuity

✅ Best options

👍 Option 1 (recommended – balanced)

Merci Anouk — je suis d’accord avec toi.

👉 Smooth, aligned, confident

👍 Option 2 (slightly more neutral)

Oui, je suis d’accord avec toi.

👉 More direct, less warmth

👍 Option 3 (a bit warmer, still fine)

Merci pour ton retour, je suis d’accord avec toi.

👉 Slightly softer tone

⚠️ What to avoid

  • ❌ “Merci beaucoup…” (too deferential)

  • ❌ long praise or validation

  • ❌ anything emotional

🧠 Subtle point

You’re not:

thanking her for helping you

You’re:

aligning on a shared issue

🟢 Recommendation

Go with:

👉 “Merci Anouk — je suis d’accord avec toi.”

Then flow into your message.

One sentence to anchor you

Acknowledge, don’t defer


You said:

Merci Anouk — je suis d’accord avec toi. Oui, d’autant plus qu’il y a déjà un point “divers”, mais ça risque d’être traité rapidement en fin de séance........

ChatGPT said:

Good start—but you can make it flow a bit more naturally and less repetitive (right now “je suis d’accord” + “oui, d’autant plus…” is slightly redundant).

Message

Merci Anouk — je suis d’accord avec toi.

D’autant plus qu’il y a déjà un point “divers”, mais ça risque d’être traité rapidement en fin de séance.

Vu l’importance des sujets que tu soulèves (PPSA, prêt, ajustements à court terme), je pense que ça vaut la peine de formuler un point explicite à l’ordre du jour.

D’ailleurs, dans la convocation il est précisé :

Ordre du jour

  1. Approbation des comptes annuels de l’exercice clos au 31 décembre 2025

  2. Décision relative à l’affectation du résultat

  3. Décharge aux membres de la gérance pour l’exercice 2025

  4. Situation actuelle, perspectives et divers

Les associés peuvent proposer l’ajout d’un point à l’ordre du jour en répondant à ce message au plus tard trois jours avant l’assemblée.

Ça me paraît aussi plus simple que ce type de point soit proposé en amont, plutôt que d’être introduit en séance.

Par exemple autour du cadre opérationnel et financier à court terme.

Je pense que ça aidera à traiter le sujet de manière claire et sereine.

💡 Why this tweak matters

  • Removes the slight repetition

  • Feels more fluid and confident

  • Keeps the tone natural and controlled

You could remove “je suis d’accord avec toi” entirely:

Merci Anouk —
D’autant plus qu’il y a déjà un point “divers”…

You said:

this still reads as an acknowledgment of ? : Oui on verra comment on aborde ces sujets mais pour moi le problème demeure. C’est inacceptable de faire temporiser PPSA alors qu’ils ont déjà payé il y a 6 mois et que c’est notre revenu principal en 2025. Et je ne comprends pas pourquoi il avance et recule par rapport à cette histoire de prêt…. Sans parler des ajustements qui semblent nécessaires à court terme. Je ne me souviens plus : j’ai ajouté des points pour divers suite à ton email ? Sinon je peux le faire. J’aimerais que l’on puisse avancer sereinement à court terme et moyen terme …

Good catch—you’re right to question this.

👉 Your current opening:

“Merci Anouk — je suis d’accord avec toi.”

does read as broad agreement, including:

  • “c’est inacceptable…”

  • criticism of Remy

  • frustration

  • pull you into confrontation you didn’t choose

Merci Anouk — je vois bien les points que tu soulèves.

You said:

ok so she wrote the following using individual emails for everyone instead of the group mail - however her comments do not read as simple points of order : Bonjour tout le monde, Encore moi :-). J’aimerais ajouter un point concernant les décisions à prendre / choses à faire à court-terme (peut-être entre le point 3 et le point 4). Je vous livre ce qui me vient mais vous aurez sans doute d’autres idées 1. Il reste des choses à finaliser telles que l'email envoyé aux candidats à implémenter dans la plateforme (il existe en français, allemand, anglais. Il faut aussi prévoir la version italienne et espagnole) et ajouter faits et opinions (cette mesure me semble spécialement importante avec le développement de l’IA qui va nécessiter d’avoir un esprit critique acéré). Ces points ne demandent pas trop de temps pour être mis en place et apporteraient une réelle valeur ajoutée de mon point de vue. 2. Dans le cadre du projet PPSA, la traduction est prévue en japonais et en chinois (payé par eux) mais la traduction doit être intégrée sur la plateforme (évidemment (!) et elle sera du coup accessible pour l’ensemble des utilisateurs). Cela peut se faire de manière dynamique pour la partie rapport mais cela demande un développement (budgétisé à 2k par David) pour la partie questionnaire. Si le développement n’est pas fait il faudra faire du copier-coller qui en gros prendra le même temps pour 2 langues. Aujourd’hui on me dit qu’il n’y a pas d’argent pour financer cela et qu’il faut attendre d’avoir des entrées. Cela ne me semble pas défendable comme position compte tenu des montants déjà versés par PPSA il y a plusieurs mois (et qui ont été utilisés pour financer d’autres choses). 3. J’aimerais mieux comprendre la position/rôle de Christophe et David. Pour moi, la partie IT est un risque majeur de notre activité avec SCOPE : j’aimerais pouvoir être rassurée sur ce point, notamment en cas d’incapacité ou même simplement de vacances de David. 4. J’ai eu une discussion avec le responsable recrutement de la Poste et il y a une vraie carte à jouer (il a fait son mémoire avec Scherer) : il y aura un appel d’offre fin mai/début juin dans lequel il va nous inclure. Mais les volumes sont colossaux (45 à 90k de passations par année). A ce stade, je ne me vois pas répondre positivement à l’appel d’offre notamment compte tenu du point 3 (et des bugs qui restent nombreux : je passe, et je ne suis pas la seule, plusieurs heures par semaine avec David au téléphone pour les gérer, ça n’est pas viable pour moi et mon activité à termes). Bref. J’aimerais que l’on passe relativement peu de temps sur les points 1 à 3 et que l’on aborde surtout des sujets de fond. Car nous avons un super outil, les retours des résultats sont top positifs et je suis convaincue que les choses vont se mettre en place.

ChatGPT said:

This is a very important moment—and honestly, this is extremely favorable for you, even if it feels messy.

Let’s break it down clearly.

🧠 What just happened (big picture)

Anouk did NOT:

“add a simple agenda item”

She did:

put operational, financial, and structural pressure into writing, to the whole group

🔥 This is very strong for you

Because now:

1. The issue is PUBLIC

  • Not just you

  • Not just a feeling

  • Not just abstract

👉 It’s now:

documented concerns affecting the business

2. She shifted the framing (this is key)

From:

  • your personal situation

To:

  • business risk

  • revenue risk (PPSA)

  • scalability risk (La Poste)

  • IT dependency (you)

👉 That is MUCH stronger

3. She validated your importance (without you asking)

Look at point 3:

“la partie IT est un risque majeur”

👉 Translation:

you are critical infrastructure

4. She indirectly supports your core argument

Without saying it explicitly, she shows:

  • system is fragile

  • depends heavily on you

  • current setup is not sustainable

👉 This is EXACTLY your argument—but better framed

⚠️ Yes—but also a nuance

You’re right:

This is NOT a simple “point of order”

It’s:
👉 substantive content sent ahead of the meeting

That can:

  • irritate Remy

  • create tension

  • shift tone of the meeting

🎯 What should YOU do now?

👉 Do NOT react publicly

  • ❌ don’t reply-all

  • ❌ don’t correct

  • ❌ don’t refine

👉 Let it stand

👉 Do NOT try to “clean it up”

Even if:

  • it’s long

  • it’s opinionated

👉 It’s doing work for you

👉 Internally, adjust your strategy

Because now:

You no longer need to carry the argument

🧭 Your new role in the meeting

Before:

  • you = primary voice

Now:

  • you = calm, structured anchor

💡 Very important shift

She is:

raising the pressure

You should:

lower the tone

👉 That combination is powerful

🗣️ So in the meeting

You do NOT:

  • match her intensity

  • reinforce her critiques

You do:

calm
structured
minimal

⚖️ What about the email format issue?

She used individual emails instead of alias.

👉 Ignore it.

  • It’s sent

  • People received it

  • It serves its purpose

❌ Not worth correcting now

🔥 Hidden advantage for you

Now if Remy says:

“you’re just a prestataire”

It clashes directly with:

“IT is a major risk” (Anouk)

👉 contradiction exposed

🟢 Bottom line

  • ✔ This is very good for you

  • ✔ Don’t interfere

  • ✔ Don’t correct

  • ✔ Adjust your role
