You said:

ok - nonetheless I have prepared and sent out this convocation so objectively at some point, whether I am chair of the meeting or not, I have an implict reponsability to, at the very least acknowledge, if not structure her comments - furthermore I had initially planned, givien the format of lunch followed by the actual formal assembly, to recommend that the assembly follow a tight formal structure and that, ideally any operational or even preparatory discussions take place independently over lunch

ChatGPT said:

You’re absolutely right—and this is where you need to separate roles very cleanly.

You are now acting (at least implicitly) as:

organizer / guarantor of process

Not:

stakeholder with a position

🧠 What just changed

Because you sent the convocation:

👉 You have a procedural responsibility

Which means:

  • you can’t ignore her email

  • you shouldn’t engage on substance

  • you should frame how it will be handled

🎯 Your objective now

  • ✔ Acknowledge her message

  • ✔ Protect the structure of the meeting

  • ✔ Avoid opening debate by email

  • ✔ Keep control of where discussions happen

🔑 Key principle

Structure ≠ suppression

You are not:
❌ shutting her down

You are:
✅ placing topics in the right space (lunch vs formal session)

💬 Recommended response (to all)

Bonjour à toutes et tous,

Merci Anouk pour ces éléments, très utiles pour préparer la discussion.

Pour rappel, la séance formelle (14h15–14h45) est destinée aux points statutaires et aux décisions formelles.

Je vous propose que les sujets opérationnels et de préparation (dont ceux mentionnés ci-dessous) puissent être abordés de manière plus ouverte en amont, notamment pendant le déjeuner, afin d’alimenter ensuite les échanges sur les points “situation actuelle et perspectives”.

Bien à vous,
David

💡 Why this is the right move

  • ✔ You acknowledge her contribution

  • ✔ You don’t validate or challenge content

  • ✔ You reinforce the structure (very important legally & practically)

  • ✔ You subtly guide discussion to lunch

⚖️ Strategic effect

You achieve 3 things at once:

1. You stay legitimate as organizer

→ clean governance

2. You avoid email debate escalation

→ no back-and-forth chaos

3. You still allow the topic to exist

→ not suppressed, just channeled

⚠️ Important nuance

You are NOT saying:

“this will not be discussed in the meeting”

You are saying:

“this will be prepared before, then structured in the meeting”

🧠 Subtle benefit for you personally

  • You reduce emotional intensity

  • You create space to control tone

  • You enter the formal session with:

    • more clarity

    • less chaos

🟢 Bottom line

  • ✔ Yes, you should acknowledge

  • ✔ Yes, you should structure

  • ✔ Do NOT engage on substance

One sentence to anchor you

Your role now is to manage the container, not the content

You said:

what about timing of this? and while acknowledging her input keeping space within the deadline i.e. before the 3 day cut off for others to add / react - also perhaps, given the situation, I should advise anouk first of this draft message such to preempt any misinterpretation on her behalf since we had explicitly talked about the agenda - another issue is that we know that Jean-Marc will not attend the lunch, in fact that is why the actual assembly was pushed to 2:15 to accomodate him and yet his input is likely to be very valuable

Let’s handle this cleanly.

  • Gives space for Anouk’s email to land

💬 Message to Anouk (before sending to all)

Message

Je vais faire un petit message au groupe pour cadrer la manière d’aborder ces points (notamment distinguer déjeuner / séance formelle), histoire d’éviter que ça parte dans tous les sens par email.

Rien sur le fond — juste pour structurer.

Je t’envoie ça dans la matinée.

💡 Why this matters

  • avoids her thinking:

    “he’s diluting my message”

  • keeps alignment

  • shows respect without losing control

🧠 3. The Jean-Marc issue (very important)

You spotted something critical:

He won’t be at lunch
→ but he’s key

⚠️ Risk if you do nothing

  • discussion happens at lunch

  • Jean-Marc excluded

  • then:

    • misalignment

    • re-explanations

    • frustration

🔑 Solution: subtle inclusion

In your group email, slightly adjust framing:

Not:

“discuss at lunch”

But:

“prepare informally (lunch or upstream), then address in formal session”

💬 Adjusted version (important tweak)

Email
Subject
Re: Convocation – Assemblée ordinaire PPE

Bonjour à toutes et tous,

Merci Anouk pour ces éléments, très utiles pour préparer la discussion.

Pour rappel, la séance formelle (14h15–14h45) est destinée aux points statutaires et aux décisions formelles.

Je vous propose que les sujets opérationnels et de préparation (dont ceux mentionnés ci-dessous) puissent être abordés de manière plus ouverte en amont — notamment pendant le déjeuner pour ceux qui y participent, ou en préparation — afin d’alimenter ensuite les échanges sur les points “situation actuelle et perspectives”.

Bien à vous,
David

💡 Why this version is better

  • Includes Jean-Marc implicitly

  • Avoids exclusion

  • Keeps structure

  • Doesn’t overcomplicate

🧭 4. About the 3-day deadline

Your message actually:

  • ✔ reinforces process

  • ✔ keeps the window open

  • ✔ doesn’t close anything

👉 You are supporting governance, not limiting it

🟢 Final strategy (clean)

Step 1

→ message to Anouk (heads-up)

Step 2 (next morning)

→ send structured email to all

Step 3

→ no further email debate

🟢 Bottom line

  • ✔ Timing: tomorrow morning

  • ✔ Yes, brief heads-up to Anouk

  • ✔ Include Jean-Marc implicitly

  • ✔ Stay procedural, not substantive

One sentence to anchor you

You’re not reacting—you’re organizing the discussion space

If you want, I can help you:
👉 prepare how to handle it if Remy pushes back on this framing (he might)


You said:

in light of step 2 the following should rather end with "Je vous envoie ça dans la matinée.": Je vais faire un petit message au groupe pour cadrer la manière d’aborder ces points (notamment distinguer déjeuner / séance formelle), histoire d’éviter que ça parte dans tous les sens par email. Rien sur le fond — juste pour structurer. Je t’envoie ça dans la matinée.

ChatGPT said:

Yes—good refinement. That small change actually makes the message feel more deliberate and less reactive.

👉 “Je vous envoie ça dans la matinée”:

  • reinforces that this is planned, not improvised

  • aligns perfectly with your timing strategy

  • subtly positions you as organizing the process

✅ Final version (clean)

Message

Je vais faire un petit message au groupe pour cadrer la manière d’aborder ces points (notamment distinguer déjeuner / séance formelle), histoire d’éviter que ça parte dans tous les sens par email.

Rien sur le fond — juste pour structurer.

Je vous envoie ça dans la matinée.

💡 Tiny nuance (optional)

If you want it slightly more direct (since you're speaking to her alone), you could say:

“Je t’envoie ça dans la matinée.”

👉 More natural in 1:1
👉 Slightly warmer

Both are fine—the difference is tone, not strategy.

🟢 Bottom line

  • ✔ Your instinct is right

  • ✔ This improves clarity and positioning

  • ✔ You’re now fully consistent with your plan

One sentence to anchor you

Small wording tweaks = big signal of control


You said:

ok but this response from you is confusing not least of all because it directly counters my suggestion - 💡 Tiny nuance (optional) If you want it slightly more direct (since you're speaking to her alone), you could say: “Je t’envoie ça dans la matinée.” Je t'envoie in this context could be misconstrued to mean that i will send it to her individually for approval / feedback before sending to everyone!
2/2

ChatGPT said:

You’re absolutely right—and thank you for calling that out.

👉 In this context, “Je t’envoie ça dans la matinée” is ambiguous and can indeed be interpreted as:

“I’ll send it to you first”

Which is not what you want.

✅ Correct conclusion

Stick with: “Je vous envoie ça dans la matinée.”

Even in a 1:1 message.

💡 Why this is actually better (even if slightly less “natural”)

  • Removes ambiguity

  • Signals clearly:

    this is going to the group

  • Avoids:

    • expectation of validation

    • perception of alignment-seeking before sending

👉 Which is exactly your intention

🧠 Subtle but important

You are:

  • informing her

  • not asking her

  • not pre-clearing

👉 “vous” reinforces that cleanly

🟢 Final version (confirmed)

Message

Je vais faire un petit message au groupe pour cadrer la manière d’aborder ces points (notamment distinguer déjeuner / séance formelle), histoire d’éviter que ça parte dans tous les sens par email.

Rien sur le fond — juste pour structurer.

Je vous envoie ça dans la matinée.

🟢 Bottom line

  • ✔ Your instinct was correct

  • ✔ My previous nuance would have introduced confusion

  • ✔ This version is precise and aligned with your strategy

One sentence to anchor you

Clarity beats naturalness when stakes are high
